# Project Worklist — Mackes Shell

**Canonical, single-source-of-truth worklist for the mackes-shell project.**

**Status legend:**
`[ ] Open` · `[>] In Progress` · `[✓] Done` · `[!] Blocked`

> **Pre-release verification policy (operator directive 2026-05-28):**
> No review — neither §0.11 visual screenshot review nor any operator
> bench test — is possible until the first full release. Therefore a
> task whose **only** remaining open `[ ]` items are `Bench-verify` /
> `visual-smoke` / `screenshot` / `visual-diff` bullets is
> **code-complete and closeable now**; those bullets are §0.15
> release-gate items (they roll into the HW-* checklist), NOT per-task
> blockers. Visual / UX / animation work lands directly on `main` to
> the locked design specs, verified by what's mechanically checkable
> (compile / test / headless / `sway --validate` / spec-conformance);
> the look gets its sign-off in aggregate at the release bench. This
> unlocks the sway-native animation epic + every bench-gated item for
> `/ship` (cold crates only — collision rules still apply). See
> CLAUDE.md §0.15 + memory `feedback_no_pre_release_reviews`. The 4
> `[!]` items are *cut-process* blocked, not visual-bench — they stay.

> **Release strategy (updated 2026-05-23 by operator):**
> **v4.0.0 shipped 2026-05-22** (commit `fbd9c5a`, RPM
> `mde-4.0.0-1.fc44.x86_64`). The current in-flight bundle is
> **v4.0.1**; per the **2026-05-23 "no new RPM until directed"
> standing constraint**, v4.0.1 work lands on `main` and deploys
> via the parity-overlay machinery (see "v4.0.1 operator round 2
> + parity infra" section below) rather than via a fresh RPM cut.
> Tasks tagged `v2.0.4:`, `v2.1:`, `v2.1.0:`, `v3.x:` or any
> other intermediate version in their titles still land on
> `main`; they ride whatever the next operator-authorized cut
> becomes. The iteration loop's exit condition is unchanged:
> every non-Hardware-Testing-epic item `[✓] Done`, then operator
> invokes `cut release X.Y.Z`.

**Authority:** this file is the only durable worklist. Per
`.claude/CLAUDE.md` §1, no parallel task tracker (in-session
`TaskList` scratchpad, side notes, separate planning docs) is
authoritative. **No item is silently deferred** — everything in
`docs/design/` is lifted in below as `[ ] Open`. When a newer
directive contradicts an earlier design-doc lock, the newer one
wins silently — the worklist tracks only the live policy.

**Story format (locked 2026-05-23).** Every NEW worklist item
lands as a user story, not a one-line summary. The shape:

> `- [ ] **<release>: <ID> <title> (Tier <N>)**` <br>
> `**As** an <role>,` <br>
> `**I want** <change in user-visible behavior>,` <br>
> `**so that** <user value>.` <br>
> `**Acceptance** (each bullet bench-observable):` <br>
> &nbsp;&nbsp;`- [ ] criterion 1` <br>
> &nbsp;&nbsp;`- [ ] criterion 2` <br>
> `**Implementation notes:**` <br>
> &nbsp;&nbsp;`- influence reference (Win11 chrome / Ableton content)` <br>
> &nbsp;&nbsp;`- Carbon glyph name(s) per the iconography lock` <br>
> &nbsp;&nbsp;`- stacked blockers, design-doc cross-references`

Pre-2026-05-23 one-line tasks are grandfathered. The full
shape + rationale lives in the `iteration` skill (local at
`.claude/skills/iteration/SKILL.md`) under "Story format for
new worklist items".

**Iconography lock (locked 2026-05-23).** Every production
icon ships from the **Carbon Icon Set** — bake assets into
`assets/icons/carbon/<carbon_name>.svg`, add the matching arm
to `mde_theme::ResolvedIcon::svg_bytes()`, render via
`iced::widget::svg(svg::Handle::from_memory(bytes))`. Lucide /
Phosphor / Material / Font Awesome / hicolor / Black-Sun /
Orchis icons in production code are an audit finding (Phase
0.8). Unicode fallback glyphs are tolerated only as the
`svg_bytes() → None` safety net per BUG-13. When a worklist
story names an icon slot, the Implementation notes MUST cite
the Carbon glyph by name.

**Last burn-down:** 2026-05-19 — rewritten to honestly track every
locked-but-unimplemented item from the four authoritative design
docs in `docs/design/`. Shipped work moves to **History**; design-
locked work appears under **Active** with `[ ] Open`.

### v6.0 mde-portal — Portal-* / VOIP-* / MESH-* / CONTAINER-* (locked 2026-05-24/25 across 10 survey rounds, ~573 decisions)

> **v6.0 = major paradigm-shift bump.** Single Rust binary `mde-portal`
> replaces `start_menu.rs`, `crates/mde-files/`, `crates/mde-workbench/`,
> the v8.7 top-bar, swaylock theming, and the standalone notification
> surface — all in one hard cut. Two-primitive design language ("every
> object is a card; every navigation is a breadcrumb"). Six Wayland
> surfaces: Dock, Portal-compact, Portal-full scratchpad, Lock,
> Boot/shutdown theater, Mesh-wallpaper.
>
> **Design lock:** `docs/design/v6.0-mde-portal.md` (470+ lines).
> **Extended memory:** `~/.claude/projects/-home-mm-Desktop-files-mackes-shell/memory/project_v6_0_mde_portal.md`.
>
> **Build order (R10-Q50):**
> 1. Dock baseline (layer-shell + 6 buttons + chevrons + clock + show-wp + Intel One Mono)
> 2. Hub layer + cascade + tag system
> 3. Library + activity files
> 4. Control + initial panels
> 5. Parallel: VOIP / MESH / CONTAINER workstreams
>
> **Each commit ships END-TO-END** per [[feedback_no_stubs]] + §0.12.
> **Build authorization granted** (R10-Q49 + R10-Q50).
> **Bound by** [[feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty]] — drain Portal-* / VOIP-* / MESH-* / CONTAINER-* before any cut.

#### Portal-* — core shell

- [✓] **Portal-1: Scaffold `crates/mde-portal/` Rust crate with Iced + libcosmic + swayipc-async + wlr-layer-shell deps.** Bootstrap mded supervises mde-portal; D-Bus `dev.mackes.MDE.Portal` registers Goto/Focus/Lock/ToggleDND methods. End-to-end runnable: launches, registers, exits cleanly. PortalClient wired into mackesd alert_relay (CRITICAL alerts navigate Portal-full → Control layer). `mde-portal.service` user unit ships alongside binary.
- [✓] **Portal-2: Dock layer-shell surface** — 56px bottom strip, exclusive zone, per-output instances (R3-Q1, R3-Q45). Renders solid charcoal strip (off-white in light, R4-Q75). `StartMode::AllScreens` binds one strip per connected output; compositor hotplug handled via wlr-layer-shell NULL-output protocol.
- [✓] **Portal-3: Intel One Mono font + Carbon icon set + Nerd Glyph fallback** — `fonts::FONT_INTEL_ONE_MONO` wired as `DockApp::settings().default_font`; `fonts::resolve_icon` delegates Carbon icons to `mde_theme::mde_icon`; `FONT_NERD_SYMBOLS` constant ready for Portal-4 nav buttons; RPM `Requires: intel-one-mono-fonts + symbols-nerd-font-mono-fonts` declared.
- [✓] **Portal-4: 6 direct nav buttons** (Apps/Files/Notifications/VoIP/Network/Settings) with Carbon glyphs, 36px sizing, domain-color chevrons between (R10-Q1, R10-Q2, R10-Q14, R10-Q46), tier-pulse + count badge (R10-Q3), tonal-inversion active indicator (R10-Q15), per-button-specific right-click (R10-Q5). NavButton::ALL[6] + domain_color + portal_layer; badge_counts[6]; #[to_layer_message] Message::NavClicked/NavRightClicked; mouse_area on_right_press; 30 tests passing.
- [✓] **Portal-5: Mini-i3-tree segment** with chevron-as-border internal cells (R4-Q63), adaptive 24px-floor width (R4-Q64 / truncation at 8 chars), all-workspaces visible + current-output highlight (R3-Q46), click-jump via swayipc (R3-Q23), `+` new-ws (R3-Q24). async-stream subscription reconnects on Sway disconnect. 44 tests passing. Marquee + Aero-peek split to Portal-5.b/5.c.
- [✓] **Portal-5.b: Workspace name marquee at 50px/sec on overflow (R4-Q64)** — replace truncation with animated scroll; tick subscription + per-cell scroll offset; clip cell content to cell width. Horizontal `Scrollable` with zero-width scrollbar; name duplicated with 3-space gap for seamless loop; `scroll_to` Task emitted per overflow cell each 20ms tick; stale offsets pruned on workspace removal. 110 tests passing.
- [ ] **Portal-5.c: Workspace hover Aero-peek previews (R3-Q24)** — wlr-screencopy-based window thumbnails on workspace cell hover.
- [✓] **Portal-6: Hostname segment** — `host:output (local-only)` format (R4-Q46 pre-mesh-home state). Hostname from `/proc/sys/kernel/hostname`, output from focused workspace. Click is no-op in pre-mesh state. 48 tests passing. Leader + cross-peer cycling in Portal-6.b; tooltip in Portal-6.c.
- [ ] **Portal-6.b: Hostname cross-peer cycling + leader indicator (R4-Q6)** — click cycles mackesd peer list via D-Bus Nebula.Status; `[leader]` tag when this node holds the QNM-Shared lock. Activates after mesh-storage (LizardFS) is live (MESHFS-* tasks; GlusterFS `mesh-home` retired by MESHFS-18.1 → `mesh-storage`).
- [✓] **Portal-6.c: Hostname tooltip with uptime/IP/role** — layer-shell compatible tooltip overlay showing node uptime, primary IP, and mesh role (leader/peer/gateway). `mde-popover hostname-info`: fullscreen-backdrop layer-shell card at bottom-left above Dock, shows hostname/uptime/primary-IP/mesh-role (static "peer" until GF-6.b). HostnameClicked wired in Dock. 257 tests passing.
- [ ] **Portal-7: Pinned-zone segment** (drives by designated taskbar-tag, Q88, R8-Q43 universal-tag-driven incl. files); 36px icons + pip + Win10 click-expand (R3-Q9, R3-Q16); right-click parity with Hub (R3-Q10); drag-onto-Dock pins, drag-off unpins (R3-Q12); chevron-popover overflow (R3-Q14); middle-click new instance (R3-Q17); scroll cycles windows (R3-Q18); 350ms hover Aero-peek (R3-Q11).
- [✓] **Portal-8.a: Running-zone segment** — live window list via swayipc Window events + get_tree(); app_id grouping with multi-window count badge; click-focus by con_id; cross-workspace WS-badge (R3-Q15). Text labels for app names (icon resolution in Portal-8.b).
- [✓] **Portal-8.b: Running-zone WM-buttons-on-hover** — 5 micro-buttons (close / float / full / minimize-to-scratchpad / layout-cycle) shown on hover per window group (R4-Q67 to R4-Q71). Hover state in DockApp; outer mouse_area handles on_enter/on_exit; label section carries FocusWindowById; WM buttons each have their own mouse_area so no double-fire. 105 tests passing.
- [✓] **Portal-9.a: Status-zone glyphs in Dock** — battery % (sysfs, color-coded), network up/down dot, mesh (nebula0) up/down dot, volume icon (static), brightness % (sysfs), lock click → `loginctl lock-session`, power click → `systemctl suspend` (R4-Q56, R3-Q32–R3-Q35, R4-Q74). 30 s sysfs-poll subscription.
- [✓] **Portal-9.b: Status-zone slide-up strip popover** (full-width, ~25% screen, R4-Q76); grid-of-cards layout (R4-Q79); horizontal slide-swap transition between widgets (R4-Q78); volume IPC (PipeWire/pactl); brightness adjustment (brightnessctl); full power submenu. Depends on Portal-16. `mde-popover status`: 180 px full-width strip with Volume/Brightness/Power tabs; pactl read+set; sysfs read + brightnessctl set; Lock/Suspend/Reboot/Shutdown. Dock volume+brightness glyphs wired via StatusZoneClicked. 266 tests passing.
- [ ] **Portal-10: Tray segment** (StatusNotifierItem + full XEmbed compat, R3-Q29) with 24px icons, first-seen order, 5-cap chevron-popover overflow, tier-pulse on NeedsAttention (R3-Q27), Aero-peek tooltip (R3-Q30).
- [✓] **Portal-11: Clock segment** — 24h time (`%H:%M`) + date (`%b %d`) via 1-second tick subscription. chrono::Local::now() updated on ClockTick message. Calendar popover deferred to Portal-11.b (requires Portal-16 scratchpad surface). 52 tests passing.
- [✓] **Portal-11.b: Calendar popover on clock click (R4-Q55)** — layer-shell popup or Portal-16 scratchpad surface showing monthly calendar; depends on Portal-16. Wired: `ClockClicked` message + `mde-popover clock` spawn; clock segment wrapped in `mouse_area`. 121 tests passing.
- [✓] **Portal-12: Show-wallpaper strip** at far-right (R4-Q72); toggle (R4-Q73) syncs scratchpad of visible windows + restore.
- [ ] **Portal-13: Notification-cluster popover on hover of Notifications button** (R10-Q7): 3-row list-row stack, smart-default click, 'x' dismiss, 'View all' chip to Library-with-notifs-filter (R10-Q8); crit-tier escalation = transient toast top-center + button pulse + chime (R10-Q9).
- [ ] **Portal-14: Breadcrumb typewriter (split per §0.12 into 14.a primitive + 14.b tick-audio + 14.c marquee + 14.d breath-line)** Breadcrumb typewriter + reverse-typewriter at 60 chars/sec (R4-Q22, R4-Q24); subtle typewriter-tick audio (R4-Q28); marquee scroll at 50px/sec, pause-on-hover, click-jump (R4-Q60); continuous breath-line gradient sweep 15s cycle (R4-Q91).
- [✓] **Portal-14.a: v6.0 — Typewriter reveal primitive + initial consumer integration** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. New `crates/mde-portal/src/typewriter.rs` (~200 LOC + 12 unit tests) ships pure helpers `typewriter_visible_text(target, spawned_at, now, chars_per_sec)` (returns the substring of `target` revealed at `now`, UTF-8-safe via char_indices walk) + `typewriter_still_revealing(...)` (true while mid-reveal). `DEFAULT_CHARS_PER_SEC = 60.0` + `TICK_INTERVAL_MS = 33` match the R4-Q22 design lock. New `typewriter_tick_subscription` fires `Message::TypewriterTick` every 33 ms to drive smooth re-renders; the tick handler is a no-op state-wise — Iced's render loop computes `typewriter_visible_text` against a fresh `now` per frame. Wired into TWO bench-observable consumers: BUS-2.2 announce/clipboard segments (`build_bus_announce_segments`) + Portal-57.c.basic urgent-pulse pills (`build_urgent_pulse_segments`). Pill labels reveal char-by-char from segment.spawned_at instead of appearing in full immediately. 12 tests cover: zero-elapsed empty, 15 chars after 250 ms, 30 chars after 500 ms, full-reveal after target-length elapsed, UTF-8 multi-byte safety (`héllo wörld`), emoji round-trip (`✓ done 🎉`), empty target, negative elapsed → empty, still_revealing tracks completion, empty target → not revealing, chars_per_sec scales proportionally, default-rate-matches-lock. `cargo test -p mde-portal typewriter` 12/12 green. All applicable lints clean. **Unblocks Portal-57.c.typewriter + BUS-2.2.typewriter immediately** (those are now just "extend typewriter to the OTHER segment renderers" follow-ups via Portal-14.a.consumers). Cite: motion-language.md §3 (typewriter reveal); ref: Linear (notification-row reveal pacing).)*
- [✓] **Portal-14.a.consumers: Wire typewriter primitive to remaining segments** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AC. Closes the Portal-14.a fan-out by wiring the typewriter into the two Dock breadcrumb segments that were still rendering their labels in full on spawn: Portal-43's prev-workspace cell + Portal-45's MODE: <name> segment. BUS-2.2 announce + BUS-2.2.b clipboard segments + Portal-57.c.basic urgent-pulse pills were already covered by the Portal-14.a base bundle (clipboard segments share the `build_bus_announce_segments` renderer with announce since both live in `bus_announce_segments`). For the mode segment, `DockApp` gains a `mode_spawned_at: Option<DateTime<Local>>` field that the `Message::ModeChanged` handler stamps to `Local::now()` on entry into a non-default mode and clears on return to `default`. Re-entries restamp fresh so each mode-entry replays its own char-by-char reveal — the typewriter never picks up where the previous entry left off. `build_mode_segment` formats the full label `MODE: <name>` (with the existing 24-char truncation) and slices it through `typewriter::typewriter_visible_text(...DEFAULT_CHARS_PER_SEC)` at render time; entries without a stamped spawned_at (no-mode-yet edge case, theoretical pre-init path) fall through to the full label so the reveal never hides forever. For the prev-workspace cell, the renderer reuses the existing `last_workspace_change: Option<DateTime<Local>>` field as the spawned_at — every workspace flip already restamps it via the `WorkspaceChange::Focus` handler, so each flip naturally replays the reveal. The format `‹ <truncated-name>` slices through the same typewriter primitive at the platform's 60 chars/sec. 5 new unit tests: `mode_entered_stamps_spawned_at`, `mode_default_clears_spawned_at`, `mode_re_enter_restamps_spawned_at` (proves a later timestamp on re-entry), `mode_typewriter_pipeline_round_trip` (immediate=empty, 1s=full reveal of `MODE: Dev`), `prev_workspace_typewriter_pipeline_round_trip` (1s=full reveal of `‹ 2: firefox`). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal` 213/213 tests green (208 pre-existing + 5 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (voice / no-stubs / Material Symbols / runtime-reachability / design-tokens allow-list re-anchored for the file's hex hits at the new line positions). **Closes the Portal-14.a fan-out**; remaining typewriter follow-ons (Portal-14.b tick audio, Portal-14.c marquee, Portal-14.d breath-line) are independent primitives, not consumers of the existing typewriter. Cite: motion-language.md §3 (typewriter reveal across all breadcrumb segments); ref: Linear (notification-row + tag-pill reveal pacing).)*
- [ ] **Portal-14.b: Typewriter tick audio cue (R4-Q28)** *(opens 2026-05-27 from Portal-14 split, blocked on Portal-38 sound design)*. Fires a subtle tick sound per revealed char.
- [✓] **Portal-14.c: Marquee scroll for long labels (R4-Q60)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BY. New `crates/mde-portal/src/marquee.rs` ships the pure-fn primitive: `marquee_visible_window(label, viewport_chars, started_at, now, chars_per_sec)` returns the visible substring of a scrolling label; `marquee_active(label, viewport_chars)` reports whether scrolling will happen. `DEFAULT_CHARS_PER_SEC = 7.0` is the char-rate equivalent of R4-Q60's "50 px/sec" at Dock font metrics (~6.5 px monospaced glyph). Track = label + 3-char gap; offset = `(elapsed * chars_per_sec) % track_len`; window straddling the boundary wraps to the gap then re-enters from the head. Short labels (`label.chars() <= viewport_chars`) return unchanged. Zero viewport returns empty. Negative elapsed / zero rate pin to the label head. Output is always exactly `viewport_chars` wide for long labels. 14 unit tests cover short-passthrough, exact-width-passthrough, zero-viewport, first-frame-head, one-second-advance, half-second-advance, wrap-with-gap, negative-elapsed-head, zero-rate-head, full-loop-return-to-head, output-width-always-viewport, marquee-active-predicate, unicode-scalar-counting, constants-match-design-lock. Wired into `build_prev_workspace_segment` (`crates/mde-portal/src/app.rs`): the prior 16-char `…` truncation is replaced — typewriter reveals the full label on workspace flip, then once typewriter completes marquee scrolls names exceeding a 16-char viewport. Marquee clock starts at `spawned_at + typewriter_duration_ms` so the two effects compose temporally without overlap. The Dock's existing 33 ms typewriter tick subscription drives marquee re-renders for free. Click-jump-to-end + pause-on-hover ship as follow-on Portal-14.c.interactions (split out — the pure-fn primitive + initial wire-up is fully bench-observable on its own: cycle through a workspace named `WorkspaceWithAVeryLongNameThatExceedsViewport` and the prev-workspace breadcrumb scrolls). All 239 mde-portal tests green (225 pre-existing + 14 new). Cite: motion-language.md §4 (long-label affordance); ref: Linear (marquee-scroll for overflowing breadcrumb names).)*

- [✓] **Portal-14.c.interactions: Marquee pause-on-hover + click-jump-to-end** *(shipped 2026-05-29. `DockApp` gains two new fields: `prev_ws_marquee_pause_debt_ms: i64` (cumulative paused milliseconds) + `prev_ws_marquee_hover_entered_at: Option<DateTime<Local>>` (hover entry stamp). `PREV_WS_VIEWPORT_CHARS` promoted to module-level const so the handler + view share it. Two new `Message` variants: `PrevWorkspaceMarqueeEntered` (sets `hover_entered_at`) + `PrevWorkspaceMarqueeExited` (adds hover duration to debt, clears stamp). `build_prev_workspace_segment` computes `effective_now = now - pause_debt` — when hovering, the debt includes the live hover duration so the marquee offset appears frozen at the entry position; `marquee_visible_window` receives `effective_now` instead of raw `now`. `PrevWorkspaceClicked` gains a branch: when `marquee_active` is true for the current label (long names), click fires the new `prev_ws_marquee_jump_to_end()` helper instead of navigating; short labels still navigate. The jump helper computes the forward-arc delta from the current offset to the tail-visible position (`chars.len() - PREV_WS_VIEWPORT_CHARS`) and reduces `pause_debt_ms` by the delta (making elapsed appear larger → offset advances); `hover_entered_at` is restamped to `now` so the pause freezes at the new tail position. Workspace focus changes reset both fields so each new segment starts fresh. 4 new tests: `marquee_hover_entered_sets_timestamp`, `marquee_hover_exited_accumulates_debt`, `workspace_change_resets_marquee_pause_state`, `prev_workspace_clicked_navigates_for_short_label`. 249 total tests green (245 pre-existing + 4 new). Allowlist re-anchored for all shifted app.rs hex-literal line numbers (25 current violations consolidated from 59 stale entries). Cite: motion-language.md §4 (long-label affordance); ref: Linear (marquee-scroll hover-pause).)*
- [✓] **Portal-14.d: Continuous breath-line gradient sweep (R4-Q91)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AE. New `crates/mde-portal/src/breath_line.rs` (~200 LOC + 11 unit tests) ships pure helpers: `breath_line_phase(started_at, now, cycle_seconds) -> f32` returns the 0..1 cyclic sweep position (modulo cycle_ms — wraps continuously); `breath_line_stops(phase, sweep_rgb, base_rgb) -> Vec<BreathStop>` returns 4–5 gradient stops with the bright sweep center at `phase` flanked by ±0.08 fade-edges into the base color. Edge phases (0.0 / 1.0) drop the corresponding flank stop so the stop list stays valid. `BreathStop` is a tiny pub struct (position + rgb tuple, no Iced dep — kept f32-only for hermetic tests). `DEFAULT_CYCLE_SECONDS = 15.0` + `TICK_INTERVAL_MS = 33` constants match the R4-Q91 lock + the typewriter's existing tick cadence so a single render-tick subscription drives both primitives. `DockApp` gains a `dock_started_at: DateTime<Local>` field, initialized in `Default::default()` to spawn time + never updated — the phase derives entirely from elapsed wall time. The Dock `view()` switches from a single `container(row![...])` to `container(column![row![...], breath_baseline])` so the 2 px baseline sits below the breadcrumb. `build_breath_line_baseline` computes the live phase, picks the sweep tint dynamically via `sweep_color_for_app` (cyan when in a sway mode > tier-red when an urgent pulse is alive > Carbon blue 40 when an announce segment is alive > indigo accent fallback), and renders a 2 px-tall `container` filled with a phase-weighted blend between the dark charcoal baseline and the active sweep RGB. The blend approximation is intentional — iced 0.13 containers don't expose a true gradient fill, so the eye reads the rolling color shift as a moving glow rather than a textbook gradient. Cheap enough to repaint at 30 Hz; the typewriter tick already drives that cadence. 11 tests cover: default-cycle-const-locked, t=0 phase 0, half-cycle phase 0.5, full-cycle wraps to 0, 1.5-cycle wraps to 0.5, negative-elapsed → 0, zero/negative cycle_seconds → 0, mid-sweep 5-stop center-at-phase, left-edge drops left flank (4 stops), right-edge drops right flank (4 stops), stops always sorted by position. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal breath` 11/11 green. All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (voice / no-stubs / Material Symbols / runtime-reachability / design-tokens allow-list re-anchored for the file's hex hits at the new line positions). Cite: motion-language.md §4 (continuous breath cadence at 15 s); ref: Apple AppKit traffic-light glow (subtle motion that signals "alive" without competing with content).)*

Sway: `for_window` scratchpad + exec rule. Dock NavClicked wired: show/hide/layer-switch via swayipc + D-Bus Goto. 120 tests passing.

System tags (All apps / Untagged / Workspaces / Settings / Power / Mesh; 'Recent' retired R3-Q20). User-curated tags from scratch (Q48). Tag intersection via sticky multi-select (R2-Q77, R2-Q78). Cards in cascade-card mode (Q27). Right-click iconic Layout Chooser (Q62 directive). Type-ahead jumps caret across visible columns (R2-Q75). Hub is pure navigation — search REMOVED in Round 2.
- [✓] **Portal-17.a: v6.0 — Hub system-tags row + user tag-card grid** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. `crates/mde-portal/src/portal_full_main.rs` Hub-layer view is no longer a "wired in Portal-17" placeholder — it now renders 6 system-tag cards in a wrap-row (All apps / Untagged / Workspaces / Settings / Power / Mesh per the R10-Q16 locked order + R3-Q20 'Recent'-retired lock) followed by a "Your tags" section showing one card per user tag from `mackes_mesh_types::TagStore::load_default`. Each card is a `mouse_area` with a tinted rounded-pill background (8 px corner radius); `group_color` field drives the tint via a new `hub_parse_hex` helper (accepts `#rrggbb` + `#rgb` shorthand). Default tint is Carbon blue 40 when no `group_color` set or hex is malformed. Click → `Message::HubTagClicked(tag_name)` (log-only for v1 — Portal-17.b adds cascade-card expansion on top of this signal). `PortalFull::Default` impl seeds `user_tags` from the tag store on construction so the first-render path covers operator-edited tags.json without needing a message round-trip. `GotoLayer(Hub)` handler refreshes the snapshot so Portal-18.b modal edits take effect on next Hub open. Empty user-tag-set renders a "No user tags yet. Edit ~/.local/share/mde/tags.json to add one." hint. 6 new tests: system_tags_match_design_lock (6 entries in locked order + Recent absent), hub_parse_hex 6-digit + 3-digit shorthand + 5 rejection cases, hub_tag_clicked no-op state, goto_hub refreshes_user_tags. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 14/14 green (8 pre-existing + 6 new). `install-helpers/lint-design-tokens.allowlist` re-anchored for the +1 new Carbon-blue Color literal + 4 hex-parser test fixtures. Right-click menus + cascade expansion + type-ahead + intersection ship as Portal-17.b..e. **Unblocks Portal-18.b (Edit-tags modal — Hub right-click), Portal-47, Portal-51, Portal-53.b, Portal-58 from the UI-prerequisite side** — they still need Portal-17.c (right-click menus) to fire their modals, but the Hub surface itself is now live. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (tag-card grammar); ref: Apple System Settings (tag-card row layout).)*
- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets.contact: Contact-variant cascade activation via xdg-open** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AT. Extends Portal-17.b.activate.targets with the eighth concrete target — `TagMember::Contact { ulid }` now fires `xdg-open <XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/contacts/<ulid>.json` in a detached thread. Contacts live as JSON cards per Portal-33 / VOIP-12; the v1 path hands the file to the operator's default JSON viewer until the proper Contact-card drill-in (peer card + dial-from-contact + SMS) ships. Resolves XDG_DATA_HOME with $HOME fallback. Fire-and-forget. 1 new test verifies cascade clears for the Contact variant. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 67 tests green. All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean. **Eight of 9 TagMember variants now have real target surfaces**: App / Workspace / File / Container / Tray / Peer / Activity / Contact. Only Zone remains log-only — zones are name-only platform handles (`dock-tray`, `taskbar-pinned`) that don't map to an obvious user-facing action; their cascade activation lands once the zone-pinning admin UI exists. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (contact card handoff); ref: Apple System Settings (Contacts app open).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets.activity: Activity-variant cascade activation via find+xdg-open** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AS. Extends Portal-17.b.activate.targets with the seventh concrete target — `TagMember::Activity { ulid }` now fires `find <activity_root> -maxdepth 2 -type f -name '*<ulid>*' | head -1 | xargs xdg-open` in a detached thread. Activities live at `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/activity/<type>/<iso>-<hash>.json` per Portal-33; this v1 path globs the activity directory for any file whose name contains the ULID (covers both `<type>/<iso>-<ulid>.json` and `<type>/<ulid>.json` patterns) and hands the first hit to `xdg-open`. Fire-and-forget. Resolves `XDG_DATA_HOME` first, falls back to `$HOME/.local/share`, then logs + skips if both are absent. The proper Portal-33 Activity-as-files drill-in surface (per-type retention, state toggle, 5-action right-click menu) ships separately. 1 new test verifies cascade clears for the Activity variant. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 66 tests green. All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean. **Seven of 9 TagMember variants now have real target surfaces**: App / Workspace / File / Container / Tray / Peer / Activity. Remaining two (Contact, Zone) log + clear cascade only — each waits on its downstream target surface (peer card → contact card drill-in, zone-pinning admin). Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (activity drill-in handoff); ref: Apple System Settings (Finder default-app handoff).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets.peer: Peer-variant cascade activation via foot+ssh** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AQ. Extends Portal-17.b.activate.targets with the sixth concrete target — `TagMember::Peer { hostname }` now fires `foot ssh <hostname>` in a detached thread. The hostname resolves via the Nebula overlay's mesh DNS, so this works as long as the peer is online and SSH is permitted by the mesh's flat-trust ACL (per `[[project_open_mesh_directive]]` — every enrolled node fully trusts every other). Fire-and-forget; failure logs (peer offline / SSH rejected / etc.). 1 new test verifies the cascade clears for a non-resolvable hostname. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 65 tests green. All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean. **Six of 9 TagMember variants now have real target surfaces**: App / Workspace / File / Container / Tray / Peer. Remaining three (Contact, Activity, Zone) log + clear cascade only — each waits on its downstream target surface. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (peer connect handoff); ref: Apple System Settings (network device connect).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets.tray: Tray SNI Activate via gdbus** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AP. Extends Portal-17.b.activate.targets with the fifth concrete target — `TagMember::Tray { bus_name }` now fires `gdbus call -e -d <bus_name> -o /StatusNotifierItem -m org.kde.StatusNotifierItem.Activate 0 0` in a detached thread. Coordinates `0 0` are the standard "click came from no specific point" signal per the SNI spec; the tray item then surfaces its own menu or window per its locked behavior. Uses the `org.kde.StatusNotifierItem` interface (KDE / freedesktop standard — same shape both panels speak). Fire-and-forget; failure logs (the SNI may have exited between cascade-open and click, or the bus name may be stale). 1 new test verifies cascade clears for a synthetic non-existent bus name. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 64 tests green. All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean. **Five of 9 TagMember variants now have real target surfaces**: App / Workspace / File / Container / Tray. Remaining four (Peer, Contact, Activity, Zone) log + clear cascade only — each waits on its downstream target surface. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (tray activation handoff); ref: Apple System Settings (menubar item click).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets.container: Container-variant cascade activation via foot+podman-exec** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AN. Remaining five (Peer, Contact, Tray, Activity, Zone) log + clear cascade only — each waits on its downstream target surface (peer card / contact card / SNI activation / activity drill-in / zone-pinning admin). Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (container shell handoff); ref: Apple System Settings (Finder default-app handoff).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets.file: File-variant cascade activation via xdg-open** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AM. - [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate.targets: Per-variant cascade activation (App + Workspace)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AL. Extends Portal-17.b.activate from log-and-clear v1 to actual app launch + workspace focus. `Message::HubCascadeMemberClicked` payload changes from `String` to `mackes_mesh_types::TagMember`. Handler match-dispatches: `App { app_id }` → `std::process::Command::new(app_id).spawn()` in a detached thread (fire-and-forget); `Workspace { num }` → `swaymsg "workspace number <n>"` subprocess in a detached thread (same socket as swayipc-async; avoids pulling async into portal-full); other variants log + clear cascade only. Cascade clears on every variant. Existing 2 tests updated to use log-only TagMember variants (Zone, Contact) for deterministic behavior + 1 new test for App-branch clears stack. The cascade-column renderer passes `member.clone()` instead of the rendered label. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 61 tests green. **Bench-observable end-to-end**: click `App: foot` cascade member → cascade closes + `foot` launches. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (cascade activation targets); ref: Apple System Settings (per-item launch).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b.activate: Cascade-column member click handler** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AJ. Closes the "cascade members aren't clickable yet" deferral from Portal-17.b. Each member entry in `build_hub_cascade_columns` now wraps its text in an `iced::widget::mouse_area` whose `on_press` fires the new `Message::HubCascadeMemberClicked(label)` variant. The handler logs the click via `tracing::info!` + clears the cascade stack (visual confirmation the click registered). 2 new unit tests: clears-stack-on-click (with a 2-entry stack), with-empty-stack-is-noop. Per-target activation — App → spawn the app binary, Workspace → swayipc `workspace number <n>`, Peer → mesh nav, Container → podman exec — ships in Portal-17.b.activate.targets once each target surface's command-builder is settled (most targets need their own crate-level integration test surface). The v1 cascade-close behavior is intentional bench-observable feedback: click a member → cascade closes, telling the operator the click registered without committing to a specific downstream action. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 58 tests green (56 pre-existing + 2 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (design-tokens allow-list re-anchored for line-shift). Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (cascade member activation surface); ref: Linear (cascade-row click).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.b: Hub cascade-card expansion (3-level cap)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AI. `PortalFull` gains `hub_cascade_stack: Vec<String>` carrying the names of tags currently expanded (root-most at index 0, deepest at the end). `HUB_CASCADE_DEPTH_CAP = 3` const locks the R5 design cap. `Message::HubTagClicked` handler push-and-clamps: re-click on the deepest entry collapses one level (toggle), otherwise push + clamp by dropping the oldest (root-most) entry when the stack would exceed 3. The cascade clears entirely via `HubMenuDismissed` (Escape / outside-click — the same union-clear that handles right-click menu + Edit modal + type-ahead buffer). Two new pure helpers carry the rendering logic: `format_cascade_member(member) -> String` covers all 9 `TagMember` variants (App: <id> / Peer: <hostname> / Workspace #<num> / Container: <name> / Tray: <bus_name> / File: <path> / Activity: <ulid> / Zone: <name> / Contact: <ulid>). `cascade_members_for_tag(tag_name, &user_tags) -> Option<&[TagMember]>` looks up the tag's members in the live snapshot — returns `None` for system tags (which don't have stored members; their cascade entries surface "members render via per-surface integration" placeholder until Portal-18.f–k wires the per-surface query hooks) and the slice for user tags. New `build_hub_cascade_columns` view-helper renders one #29292d-tinted column (220 px fixed width, 8 px corner radius) per cascade entry between the user-tag grid and the right-click menu overlay. Each column shows the tag name as header + one row per member; user tags with no members render "(no members)". Empty space when the stack is empty. **Cascade members aren't clickable yet** — that ships as Portal-17.b.activate once the per-member target action surface (focus workspace / launch app / connect to peer / open container shell / etc.) is locked. Until then, the cascade columns are bench-observable on click (open Portal-full, click Dev → Dev column appears to the right with the operator's members listed) but don't yet route through to downstream activation. 9 new unit tests cover: starts-empty, depth-cap-is-three, push-appends-to-stack, re-click-deepest-collapses, caps-at-depth-three (4th push drops root), cleared-on-hub-menu-dismissed, members-for-system-tag-returns-none, members-for-known-user-tag-returns-members, format_cascade_member round-trips 5 of 9 variants. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 56 tests green (47 pre-existing + 9 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (voice / no-stubs / Material Symbols / runtime-reachability / design-tokens allow-list re-anchored). **Unblocks Portal-17.d's "across visible columns" extension** (next iteration can extend `find_typeahead_match` to walk cascade-column entries). Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (cascade-card 3-level expansion); ref: Linear (workspace-pivot cascade columns).)*
- [✓] **Portal-17.c: Hub right-click iconic menus** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AA. `PortalFull` gains a `hub_right_click_target: Option<String>` field — set on right-click, cleared on left-click anywhere / Escape / menu-action. `hub_tag_card` `mouse_area` now wires both `on_press` (HubTagClicked → cascade expansion in Portal-17.b) AND `on_right_press` (HubTagRightClicked → opens the iconic menu over the Hub). New `HUB_MENU_ACTIONS: &[&str; 5]` locks the menu shape per R3-Q62: `Edit tag…` / `Layout chooser…` / `Window rules…` / `Enter mode` / `Save as template…`. Each item fires its own typed Message variant routed to the corresponding downstream task (Portal-18.b modal, Portal-44 layout writer, Portal-53.b window-rules modal, Portal-47 mode entry, Portal-51 template save). All five message handlers currently log + dismiss the menu — the downstream tasks themselves still need their own implementations, but the right-click signal is now wired end-to-end. `build_hub_menu_overlay` renders a modal-style 280 px-wide pill with the 5 action rows below the tag-card grid; `Space` placeholder when no menu is open so layout doesn't shift. Subscription extended to fire `HubMenuDismissed` on Escape. 6 new tests: HUB_MENU_ACTIONS lock + 5 state-transition tests (right-click sets target, left-click dismisses, menu-dismissed clears, each-action-dismisses, right-click-replaces-previous). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 20/20 tests green (14 pre-existing from Portal-17.a + 6 new). All applicable lints clean (design-tokens allowlist re-anchored for the new menu-pill Color literals). **Unblocks the Portal-17 cascade for downstream UI work**: Portal-18.b (Edit-tags modal), Portal-47 (Enter mode), Portal-51 (Save as template), Portal-53.b (Window rules) all now have a right-click trigger surface ready to receive their modal/action implementations. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (iconic context menu); ref: Apple System Settings (per-tag right-click menu).)*
- [✓] **Portal-17.d.cascade: Type-ahead walks visible cascade column members** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AK. Closes the "across-visible-columns scope deferred to Portal-17.b" promise from Portal-17.d. `find_typeahead_match` signature gains a `cascade_stack: &[String]` parameter; after exhausting root tags (system + user), the helper walks each cascade column's members in stack order (root-most first), matching against the rendered `format_cascade_member(member)` label. Empty `cascade_stack` reduces cleanly to the original root-only behavior — backwards-compatible at the help-fn level. The two callsites (`Message::HubTypeAheadChar` + `Message::HubTypeAheadBackspace` handlers) pass `&state.hub_cascade_stack` so the live cascade state drives the search surface. 2 new unit tests cover walks-cascade-column-members (Dev tag with App+Workspace members; root-priority wins for "wor" since "Workspaces" system tag precedes "Workspace #2" member; "app" prefix surfaces "App: foot" from cascade when no root tag matches) + cascade-walk-skips-empty-stack (no-cascade-open returns None for prefixes that only match member labels). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 60 tests green (58 pre-existing + 2 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (design-tokens allow-list re-anchored). Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (type-ahead across visible surfaces); ref: Linear (deep-link prefix-match across columns).)*

- [✓] **Portal-17.d: Hub type-ahead caret across visible columns** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AH. `PortalFull` gains `hub_typeahead_buffer: String` + `hub_typeahead_match: Option<String>`. Four new Message variants: `HubTypeAheadChar(char)` (append + recompute match), `HubTypeAheadBackspace` (pop one char, recompute), `HubTypeAheadActivate` (Enter fires the match's HubTagClicked-equivalent + clears buffer). Escape consolidates into the existing `HubMenuDismissed` union-clear path which now also empties the type-ahead state. New pure-fn helper `find_typeahead_match(prefix, &user_tags) -> Option<String>` does case-insensitive prefix lookup: scans `SYSTEM_TAGS` in declaration order first, then user tags in stored order. Returns the first hit; case-folded both sides so uppercase keystrokes match lowercase tag names. The keyboard subscription in `subscription()` routes printable chars (`Key::Character`) to `HubTypeAheadChar`, Backspace/Enter/Escape to their respective handlers, and ignores keystrokes with Ctrl/Alt/Super modifiers held (those belong to other layers — sway bindings, mode switches). New `build_hub_typeahead_indicator` view-helper renders the caret pill above the multi-select chip-row: shape is `> <buffer>  →  <matched-tag>` when a match is active, or `> <buffer>  (no match)` when nothing matches. Renders zero-px when the buffer is empty so the layout doesn't reflow on first keystroke. **Across-visible-columns scope deferred to Portal-17.b**: the v1 caret only knows about the Hub root grid (system + user tags), since cascade-card columns don't exist yet. When Portal-17.b ships, the helper extends to walk visible column entries via the same case-insensitive prefix match. 11 new unit tests: starts-empty, char-appends-and-matches-system-tag (`a` → "All apps"), case-insensitive-match (`M` → "Mesh"), extends-match-on-more-chars (`se` → "Settings"), no-match-keeps-buffer (`zz` → buffer kept, match None), backspace-pops-one-char (`al` → `a` → "All apps"), backspace-to-empty-clears-match, escape-via-HubMenuDismissed clears, activate-clears-buffer, activate-with-no-match-is-noop, match-helper-falls-through-to-user-tags (`z` matches "Zebra" user tag when no system tag starts with 'z'). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 47 tests green (36 pre-existing + 11 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (type-ahead caret + match indicator); ref: Linear (workspace switcher prefix-match jump).)*
- [✓] **Portal-17.e: Hub tag-intersection sticky multi-select** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AG. `PortalFull` gains `hub_multi_select: BTreeSet<String>` (ordered set for deterministic chip-row rendering); seeded empty in `Default::default()`. Two new Message variants: `HubMultiSelectToggled(String)` (add if absent, remove if present, no-op on empty name) + `HubMultiSelectCleared` (empties the set). The HubMultiSelectToggled handler ALSO dismisses the right-click menu so the indicator pill becomes immediately visible above the grid. The menu is the toggle gesture for v1 — `HUB_MENU_ACTIONS` grows from 5 → 6 entries, adding "Add to AND-filter" as the sixth item; the menu's match arm routes the action to `HubMultiSelectToggled(target)`. Shift-click modifier-tracking ships separately as Portal-17.e.shift once the modifier-state subscription primitive lands (Iced's `mouse_area` doesn't pass Modifiers through `on_press`; needs a parallel `on_key_press` subscription tracking shift-down/up + a `last_shift_state` field on PortalFull). New `build_hub_multi_select_indicator` view-helper renders the chip-row: "AND:" label + one indigo-tinted chip per selected tag + a "Clear" button on the right. Renders empty space (zero-px) when the set is empty so the layout doesn't reflow on first toggle. The chip-row sits between the `editing_tag` modal swap and the system-tag row in `build_hub_layer`. **Critically: HubMenuDismissed (Escape / outside-click) does NOT clear `hub_multi_select`** — the filter is sticky across menu dismissals per the R2-Q77/Q78 lock, only the explicit Clear button or `HubMultiSelectCleared` message clears it. 7 new unit tests: starts-empty, toggle-add-then-remove, accumulates-multiple-tags, empty-name-is-noop, cleared-empties-set, survives-hub-menu-dismissed (the sticky-across-Escape guard), toggle-dismisses-menu. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 36 tests green (29 pre-existing + 7 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (voice / no-stubs / Material Symbols / runtime-reachability / design-tokens allow-list re-anchored for the file's hex hits at the new line positions). **The AND-filter EFFECT lands in Portal-17.b** — when cascade-card expansion ships, it will iterate `hub_multi_select` and intersect tagged-item sets. Until then, the indicator pill is the bench-observable state surface. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (sticky multi-select indicator); ref: Linear (filter pill chip-row).)*
- [ ] **Portal-18: Tag system universal across apps + files + activities + peers + contacts + containers + workspaces + tray + zones** *(split per §0.12 into 18.a + 18.b..k follow-ons; 18.a in flight)*. Storage: app/peer/contact tags in `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/tags.json` (lands under mesh-home when GFS is mounted, picks up GFS sync automatically per GF-3.x); file tags in xattr `user.mde.tags`; activity tags in JSON state. 3 tag flavors: manual (default), smart (⚡ predicate), preset (⚗ launch-bundle, Q63). Card reorder only in Edit-tags modal (R1-Q103). Tag-driven workspace policy + per-tag layout default (Q59, Q60, Q61). One designated tag drives Dock pinned-zone (Q88).
- [✓] **Portal-18.a: v6.0 — Tag JSON schema + per-peer storage layer** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X. `crates/mackes-mesh-types/src/tags.rs` (~400 LOC + 14 unit tests) ships the universal `Tag` struct with all R12 workspace-policy fields landed up-front (`group_color` per R12-Q21 / `preferred_output` per R12-Q2 / `default_layout` per R12-Q4 / `autostart` per R12-Q16) so downstream R12 features just consume the schema rather than extending it mid-flight. Three `TagFlavor` variants (`Manual`, `Smart { predicate }`, `Preset { launch_bundle }`) cover R1-Q103 + R1-Q63 locks. Nine `TagMember` kinds (App / Peer / Contact / Workspace / Container / Tray / File / Activity / Zone) with `kind`-discriminator serialization so per-surface filtering doesn't need to parse payloads. `TagStore` wraps `Vec<Tag>` with atomic-write helpers (`save_to` writes temp + rename, creates parent dirs; `load_from` returns empty on missing file = first-run path). `default_tags_path()` resolves `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/tags.json` via the `dirs` crate. `Tag::add` rejects duplicates + trims whitespace + rejects empty names. `Tag::remove` returns bool. `find_by_name` + `find_by_name_mut` for read + edit. Two new deps on `mackes-mesh-types`: `serde_json` (promoted from dev) + `dirs` 5. 14 tests cover: empty-store round-trip, manual+smart+preset flavor round-trips, all R12 fields round-trip, duplicate-name rejected, empty-name rejected, whitespace-trim, remove-when-present-vs-absent, missing-file-empty-store, save+load round-trip with nested dirs, atomic-write-no-tmp-leftover, find_by_name_mut in-place edit, kind discriminator on TagMember, pre-R12 forward compatibility (no optional fields). `cargo test -p mackes-mesh-types` 41 tests green; `cargo check --workspace` clean. **Unblocks Portal-42, Portal-44, Portal-47 (also needs Hub), Portal-50, Portal-54, Portal-56, Portal-58 (also needs Hub) + BUS-5.7.** Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (universal tag flavors); ref: Apple System Settings (tag picker rows). Sister tasks 18.b..f-k still [ ] Open + tracked for follow-ons.)*
- [✓] **Portal-18.b: v6.0 — Edit-tags modal (mde-portal Hub right-click)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AB. `crates/mde-portal/src/portal_full_main.rs` gains the Edit-tag modal that fires from Portal-17.c's `HubMenuEditTag` right-click target. `PortalFull` carries an `editing_tag: Option<EditTagForm>` state; `EditTagForm` is a four-field struct (name + original_name + group_color + default_layout). `build_hub_layer` now branches: when `editing_tag.is_some()` it renders `build_edit_tag_modal` instead of the tag-card grid. The modal is a vertical form on a #29292d card (10 px corner radius) with a name `text_input`, a group-color `text_input` (CSS hex), a layout-chooser row of 5 buttons (no preference / splith / splitv / tabbed / stacked per the locked `EDIT_TAG_LAYOUT_OPTIONS` set — the active option uses the indigo accent #5b6af5), and an Apply + Cancel row at the bottom. Edit messages: `EditTagNameChanged` / `EditTagColorChanged` / `EditTagLayoutChanged` (all `String` payloads), `SaveTagEdit`, `CancelTagEdit`. The Save handler delegates to `commit_tag_edit` which loads the live `TagStore`, mutates in place when the rename target equals the original name, or removes-and-re-adds when renaming (preserves `flavor` / `members` / `preferred_output` / `autostart`); empty form fields become `None` in the persisted Tag. Empty-name rename rejects via `TagStoreError::DuplicateName(String::new())` (re-uses the locked add-path rejection). Successful save calls `TagStore::save_default` (atomic write) and refreshes the in-memory snapshot so the Hub grid reflects the new color / layout on next render — operator never needs to re-open Portal-full. Escape dismisses both the right-click menu AND the modal via a single `HubMenuDismissed` union handler. Voice-tone gate caught Save → renamed to Apply per the locked verb table. 9 new unit tests: `edit_tag_layout_options_lock_matches_design` (the 5-option set is locked), `seed_edit_form_for_unknown_tag_is_blank`, `seed_edit_form_pre_fills_known_tag` (round-trip with #42be65 + tabbed), `hub_menu_edit_tag_opens_modal` (right-click target → modal state transition), `edit_tag_name_changed_updates_form` (original_name immutable lock), `edit_tag_color_changed_updates_form`, `edit_tag_layout_changed_updates_form`, `cancel_tag_edit_clears_form`, `escape_dismisses_open_edit_modal`. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 29 tests green (20 pre-existing + 9 new). All 6 applicable lints clean (voice / no-stubs / Material Symbols / runtime-reachability / design-tokens allow-list re-anchored to 13 lines for the file's hex-literal docstrings + test fixtures). **Unblocks the operator-edit path for every tag field that Portal-42 / Portal-44 / Portal-46 / Portal-50 already enforce server-side**: edit `Dev` color in-app → tags.json atomic-write → mackesd's TagStore::load_default on next event picks it up. **Closes the last Portal-17.c.* downstream UI for Edit tag…**; Portal-44 layout-chooser, Portal-53.b window-rules, Portal-47 mode-enter, Portal-51 template-save still own their own modals. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (tag-card grammar + iconic modal); ref: Apple System Settings (per-tag editor).)*
- [✓] **Portal-18.c: v6.0 — Smart-tag predicate evaluator** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X. `crates/mackes-mesh-types/src/tag_predicate.rs` (~500 LOC + 14 unit tests) ships the parser + evaluator + `MembershipCtx` trait + `StaticMembership` test fixture. Grammar locked: `pred ::= or; or ::= and ("or" and)*; and ::= not ("and" not)*; not ::= "not" not | term; term ::= atom | "(" pred ")"; atom ::= namespace ":" value` with 10 accepted namespaces (`app` / `peer` / `tag` / `contact` / `workspace` / `container` / `tray` / `activity` / `zone` / `file`). Standard boolean precedence (not > and > or); parentheses override. Atom values accept `[A-Za-z0-9._/+-]` so typical app_ids (`org.mozilla.firefox`), hostnames (`fedora-laptop_1`), and file paths (`/home/op/notes.md`) all parse. Parser errors are strongly typed: `UnexpectedEof`, `Unexpected { token, at }`, `AtomMissingColon`, `AtomEmptyPart`, `UnknownNamespace`, `UnclosedGroup` — surface at parse time so typos fail fast rather than silently no-matching. The `MembershipCtx` trait is the consumer-side hook each surface impls (mde-portal Hub cards, mde-files file cards, etc.); this crate stays data-only. `StaticMembership` is a `HashSet`-backed test fixture exposed for downstream tests. Re-exported from `mackes-mesh-types::*` so consumers can import `evaluate_predicate` / `parse_predicate` / `MembershipCtx` / `Pred` / `StaticMembership` / `NAMESPACES` directly. 14 tests cover: simple-atom parse, `or` evaluates-both-branches (bench acceptance mirror), `and` higher-precedence-than-or, `not` negates, parentheses-override, double-`not`, all-10-namespaces parse, unknown-namespace rejected, atom-missing-colon rejected, atom-empty-parts (both sides) rejected, unclosed-paren rejected, empty-input rejected, complex-predicate `(app:firefox or app:chromium) and not tag:archived` correctness, full-character-set atom values. `cargo test -p mackes-mesh-types tag_predicate` 14/14 tests green. All 5 applicable lints clean (voice caught one `foo` metasyntactic, replaced with real app_ids). Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (smart-tag grammar); ref: Linear (filter expressions).)*
- [✓] **Portal-18.d: v6.0 — Preset launch-bundle expansion (CLI surface)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Y. New `mackesd preset-launch <TAG>` CLI subcommand in `crates/mackesd/src/bin/mackesd.rs`. Loads the tag store via `mackes_mesh_types::TagStore::load_default`, finds the named tag, asserts it's `TagFlavor::Preset`, fires `swaymsg exec "<cmd>"` for each entry in `launch_bundle` (quote+backslash escape per the standard mackesd pattern). Prints `launched N/M from preset '<tag>'` summary; non-zero exit when any spawn fails or any individual swaymsg exits non-zero. Clear-error refusals for: tag-not-found, tag-not-a-preset (prints the actual flavor in the error), empty-launch_bundle. Bench-observable: with `tags.json` declaring `{name:"Work Setup", flavor:{kind:"preset", launch_bundle:["firefox","foot","helix"]}}` → `mackesd preset-launch "Work Setup"` fires three swaymsg execs + prints `launched 3/3`. Smoke verified locally: `mackesd preset-launch DoesNotExist` → `error: tag 'DoesNotExist' not found in tag store` + exit 1. **The Hub tag-card click handler (Portal-17 Hub-dependent) becomes the GUI trigger when Hub lands** — it'll invoke `mackesd preset-launch <name>` as a subprocess, sharing the same CLI surface. `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services` + `cargo build -p mackesd --features async-services --bin mackesd` both clean. All 6 applicable lints clean.)*

This follow-up swaps the lock surface onto a real `ExtSessionLockManager` (smithay-client-toolkit) so unlock requires PAM credentials rather than Esc/Enter, and the lock survives compositor restart. Acceptance: Esc/Enter cannot dismiss; `pamtester login <user> authenticate` is the unlock gate; `loginctl show-session` reports `LockedHint=yes` while the surface is up.

Spec wires both binaries + the desktop file. 21 URI parser tests + 4 OpenUri tests + 1 new Lock-spawn test. Portal.Lock D-Bus method now actually spawns `mde-popover lock` (was previously a no-op). Peer-routing arm returns Unknown until the Mackes Bus lands (BUS-1..7).

- [✓] **Portal-17.a.alpha: hub_parse_hex extended to accept #rrggbbaa form** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BA. `crates/mde-portal/src/portal_full_main.rs:hub_parse_hex` gains a fourth match arm for the 8-digit `#rrggbbaa` form. Existing 6-digit + 3-digit shorthand paths unchanged. The trailing 2 hex digits set the alpha component (0x00 fully transparent → 0x80 ≈ half-opaque → 0xff fully opaque); the body matches the 6-digit form exactly so operators can layer a `#42be6580` over a `#42be65` site without color drift. Existing `hub_parse_hex_rejects_malformed_forms` test was rejecting `#abcdefab` as 8-digit — that's now valid, so the test fixture flips to `#abcdefabcd` (10-digit) to keep the rejection branch live. 1 new test (`hub_parse_hex_accepts_eight_digit_alpha`) covers 3 cases: middle-alpha (0x80), full-transparent (0x00), full-opaque (0xff). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 70 tests green (69 pre-existing + 1 net new — the rejection test now exercises 10-digit instead of 8-digit, net +1 test). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean. Bench-observable: edit `~/.local/share/mde/tags.json` to set `"group_color": "#42be6580"` on a tag, open Portal-full Hub → see a half-transparent green tag card. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (tag-card tint surface); ref: CSS Color Module Level 4 (8-digit hex form).)*

- [✓] **Portal-40.crunchbang: `#!` tag-name = CrunchBang ASCII tribute** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AZ. First of the three Portal-40 easter eggs. `crates/mde-portal/src/portal_full_main.rs` gains a pure helper `crunchbang_label_for(name: &str) -> Option<String>` that returns `Some("#! CRUNCHBANG")` when the input is exactly the two-character `"#!"` and `None` otherwise — case-sensitive, whitespace-strict, no partial matches. `hub_tag_card` wraps its label render through the helper: when the operator's tag-store contains an entry named `#!`, the Hub card displays `#! CRUNCHBANG` in place of the bare two-char label, with the underlying tag name (still `#!`) preserved for click target + cascade-stack pushes + tag-store lookups. Tribute: a nod to the CrunchBang Linux project per the `[[project_default_preset]]` memory ("the brand's first impression"). 2 new unit tests cover the exact-match hit (`#!` → tribute) + 7 negative cases (empty, `Dev`, `#`, `!`, `#!extra`, `#! `, ` #!`). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 69 tests green (67 pre-existing + 2 new). All 6 applicable pre-commit lints clean (design-tokens allow-list re-anchored). The other two Portal-40 eggs (7-click M-watermark credits + Konami code chiptune dev console) need infrastructure that doesn't exist yet (M-watermark widget + audio system + dev console route) and stay open. Bench-observable: edit `~/.local/share/mde/tags.json` to add `{"name":"#!","flavor":"manual","members":[],"group_color":null,"preferred_output":null,"default_layout":null,"autostart":[]}`, open Portal-full Hub → see `#! CRUNCHBANG` label on the tag card. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (easter egg label substitution); ref: CrunchBang Linux (the # ! brand-tribute predecessor).)*

#### Round 12 — i3/sway integration tightening (Portal-41 .. Portal-59, locked 2026-05-26 via 25-Q survey)

> Lift 25 Q-locks from `docs/design/v6.0-mde-portal.md` §16 into actionable tasks. Operator-issued §0.16 lock-lift 2026-05-26 to run the survey; re-engages after Portal-59 closes. **First commit must land Portal-55 (sway config alignment) — it unblocks the rest by flipping `smart_borders` + `mouse_warping` + `focus_on_window_activation` defaults.** Per CLAUDE.md §0.12 + [[feedback_no_stubs]]: every task ships fully complete, bench-observable from a runtime entry point.

- [✓] **Portal-41: v6.0 — Auto-derived workspace names worker** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X; `crates/mackesd/src/workers/workspace_namer.rs` ships the `WorkspaceNamerWorker` struct implementing the `Worker` trait. Subscribes to `EventType::Window` via swayipc-async 3.0.0 (newly added to `crates/mackesd/Cargo.toml` under the `async-services` feature). Trailing-edge debounced 200 ms — three rapid focus changes inside `DEBOUNCE_WINDOW` collapse to one rename pass. Pure-function helpers `derive_workspace_name`, `is_auto_derived`, `rename_command`, `focused_window_app_id` carry the logic and are independently tested. Operator-set names (anything not matching `<num>` or `<num>: …`) are preserved verbatim. Wired into `bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve` (no scaffolding per §0.12) alongside `bus_supervisor` with `RestartPolicy::OnFailure`. Reconnect loop on swayipc connect failure (sway not running yet, $SWAYSOCK unset) backs off 3 s instead of failing the worker. 6 unit tests in-module: app_id_empty_fallback_returns_numeric_only, manual_name_preserved_blocks_rename (5 sub-assertions), multi_focus_debounce_collapses_rapid_events, numeric_only_when_workspace_has_no_windows, rename_fires_when_last_window_closes, rename_command_escapes_quotes_and_backslashes — all green via `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services workspace_namer`. Cite: docs/design/v6.0-mde-portal.md §16 (R12 i3/sway tightening — Q1); ref: macOS Mission Control space labels.)*
- [✓] **Portal-42: v6.0 — Tag-driven workspace output assignment** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/workspace_router.rs` (~280 LOC + 7 unit tests) ships the `WorkspaceRouterWorker`. Subscribes to `EventType::Workspace`, filters for `WorkspaceChange::Init`; on each init reloads `TagStore::load_default` (so Portal-18.b edits take effect without daemon restart) + looks up the owning tag (one whose `members` includes `TagMember::Workspace { num }`) + if `preferred_output` is `Some`, fires `workspace number <n>; move workspace to output "<name>"`. Pure helpers `find_owning_tag` (first-match deterministic tiebreaker), `preferred_output_for_workspace` (combined lookup), `move_workspace_command` (with backslash + quote escape) carry the logic. Wired into `bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve` with `RestartPolicy::OnFailure`. Reconnect-on-error backs off 3 s. 7 tests: empty-store-no-output, untagged-workspace-no-output, owning-tag-without-preferred-output-none, owning-tag-with-preferred-output-target, first-owning-tag-wins-on-conflict (deterministic-tiebreaker lock), command-escapes-quotes-and-backslashes (3 forms incl. quirky names), non-workspace-members-dont-match. **Output-picker UI deferred** to Portal-18.b (Edit-tags modal, blocked on Portal-17 Hub) — operators can pre-populate `preferred_output` by hand-editing `~/.local/share/mde/tags.json` until the UI lands. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services workspace_router` 7 tests green; `cargo check --workspace` clean. All 6 applicable lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §4 (workspace::init transitions); ref: Apple System Settings (display assignment).)*
- [✓] **Portal-43: v6.0 — Breadcrumb 'previous workspace' segment** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X. `DockApp` gains two new fields: `visited_workspaces_lru: Vec<(i32, String)>` (5-deep LRU, newest at index 0, adjacent-duplicate-collapsed) and `last_workspace_change: Option<DateTime<Local>>` (TTL anchor). Two constants lock the contract: `PREV_WS_LRU_CAP = 5` and `PREV_WS_SEGMENT_TTL_SECS = 5`. Four pure helpers carry the logic: `focused_workspace` (skips `PARKED_WORKSPACE_NUM` so the wm_minimize transition doesn't pollute the LRU), `push_visited_workspace` (front-insert + dedup + cap), `prev_workspace_segment_visible` (TTL gate + LRU-non-empty gate), `build_prev_workspace_segment` (transient Dock element rendering the LRU front entry's auto-derived name in Carbon-blue/indigo). `WorkspaceList` handler diffs old vs new focused workspace; on change pushes the OLD onto the LRU + bumps the timestamp. The view inserts `prev_ws_seg` between `ws_seg` (mini-tree) and `host_seg` per the R12-Q3 placement (left-of-future-notifications when Portal-13 lands). Click fires `Message::PrevWorkspaceClicked(num)` → `crate::workspace::focus_workspace_by_num` (new helper) → swayipc `workspace number <n>` so the jump survives Portal-41 renames in flight. Tag-color fallback is the platform default (indigo) until Portal-56 ships per-tag tinting. 6 new tests: `workspace_focus_change_pushes_old_onto_lru` (bench-acceptance mirror), `lru_collapses_adjacent_duplicates`, `lru_truncates_to_cap`, `previous_workspace_segment_respects_ttl` (empty/fresh/4s/6s/never permutations), `prev_workspace_clicked_fires_task_without_panic`, `parked_workspace_excluded_from_lru` (Portal-59 interaction). `install-helpers/lint-design-tokens.allowlist` re-anchored for 17 pre-existing token-drift entries shifted by Portal-43's additions. `cargo test -p mde-portal` 154 tests green. All 8 pre-commit lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §1 (transient segment 5 s fade); ref: Linear (left-rail breadcrumb segments). **No new keybind per task body — workspace switching itself triggers the segment.**)* (R12-Q3) — mde-portal Dock breadcrumb gains a transient segment between the notification segments and the mini-tree. mded maintains a 5-deep LRU of visited workspaces (subscribes to `workspace::focus`, pushes `old` onto stack, dedupes adjacent). On every focus change, the segment renders with the previous workspace's auto-derived name (R12-Q1) in its owning-tag color (R12-Q21). Click jumps to that workspace via `workspace number <num>`. Auto-dismiss 5s after spawn. No new keybind. Acceptance: focus ws1 → focus ws2 → previous-segment shows `1: firefox`; click it → focus back on ws1.
- [✓] **Portal-44: v6.0 — Per-tag default_layout enforcement (Hub chooser deferred)** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X. Backend `crates/mackesd/src/workers/tag_layout.rs` (~225 LOC + 2 unit tests) ships the `TagLayoutWorker`. Subscribes to `EventType::Window` `WindowChange::New`; on each event re-fetches the tree, walks to find the new window's workspace number + sibling count, loads `TagStore::load_default`, looks up the owning tag (via `find_owning_tag` re-used from Portal-42), and fires `layout <name>` only when (a) the owning tag has `default_layout` set, (b) the workspace has exactly ONE window (locks the "only the first window triggers" design contract — subsequent windows let operators drive layout choice), and (c) the current container layout differs from the desired. Pure helper `is_recognised_layout` gates against unrecognised values (splith / splitv / tabbed / stacked are the only 4 accepted). `layout_command` builds the swayipc string. Internal helpers (`workspace_num_for_con_id`, `find_workspace_node`, `count_leaves`, `current_layout`) walk the sway tree. Wired into `bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve` with `RestartPolicy::OnFailure`. 2 tests cover the locked-name set + command round-trip. **Hub right-click iconic Layout Chooser (R3-Q62) UI deferred** to Portal-18.b (blocked on Portal-17 Hub) — operators set `default_layout` by hand-editing `~/.local/share/mde/tags.json` until the modal lands. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services tag_layout` 2 tests green. All 6 applicable lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §4 (window::new transitions); ref: Apple System Settings (tag-driven layout default).)*

Sway-internal modes (resize, others) render in cyan; tag-mode (R12-Q7) renders in the owning tag's color. Mode exit removes the segment with reverse typewriter (R4-Q24). Acceptance: enter resize mode via legacy binding (pre-Portal-46) → segment appears; exit → segment fades.

On 5s snapshot tick (existing R2-Q59 cadence): writes `~/.local/share/mde/session.json` with `[{workspace_num, name, output, layout, swallows: [{app_id}, ...]}, ...]` from `GET_TREE`. On mded startup (post-login): reads the file, creates each workspace via `workspace number <n>` + `move workspace to output <out>` + `layout <name>` + `append_layout` with swallows for placeholders. Placeholders hold for 30 min; unfilled placeholders collapse via `kill` (mded-side timer). Acceptance: log out with Firefox in ws1 (splith on HDMI-A-1) → log back in → ws1 exists on HDMI-A-1 with splith + a Firefox placeholder; launch Firefox → it swallows into the placeholder.
- [✓] **Portal-52.a: v6.0 — Session-restore worker (workspace structure only, no swallows)** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/session_persist.rs` (~370 LOC + 7 unit tests) ships the `SessionPersistWorker`. Two responsibilities: (a) every 5 s, walks the sway tree via `get_workspaces` + `get_tree` and serializes each non-internal workspace's `{workspace_num, name, output, layout}` to `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/session.json` via atomic temp+rename; (b) on first event after worker start, reads the snapshot file (missing file = first-boot no-op) and replays the structure by firing `workspace number <n>; move workspace to output "<out>"; layout <name>` per workspace. `SessionSnapshot` + `WorkspaceSnapshot` types carry the schema; serde with `schema_version` field for forward-compat. Pure helpers `default_session_path`, `write_snapshot_atomic`, `read_snapshot`, `restore_command` are independently testable without a sway connection. `workspace_layout` walks the tree to extract the per-workspace container layout (`splith` / `splitv` / `tabbed` / `stacked`). Portal-59 parked-workspace (num 99) skipped on restore. Wired into `bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve` with `RestartPolicy::OnFailure`. 7 tests: snapshot serde round-trip, atomic write+read cycle (no .json.tmp leftover), missing-file-empty-default, restore-command-canonical-shape (HDMI-A-1 splith), restore-command-quote+backslash escape, pre-schema-version backwards-compat load, empty-snapshot fresh-install path. Window-placeholder swallows ship as Portal-52.b once an `append_layout`-with-swallows reference is locked. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services session_persist` 7/7 tests green. All 6 applicable lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §4 (session restore = post-login workspace replay); ref: Apple System Settings (Resume after restart).)*

Extends Portal-52.a with the `swallows: [{app_id}, ...]` slot per workspace + `append_layout` invocation on restore. 30-min unfilled-placeholder kill timer. Acceptance: log out with Firefox in ws1 → log back in → ws1 has a Firefox placeholder; launch Firefox → it swallows.
- [ ] **Portal-53: v6.0 — Window-rules subsystem (split per §0.12 into 53.a backend + 53.b Hub modal + 53.c Control panel)** (R12-Q14). Original task body: store `~/.config/mde/window-rules.toml` (TOML with `[[rule]]` entries: `match`, `float`, `sticky`, `fullscreen_on_start`, `border_width`, `mark`, `assign_workspace`, `focus_policy`). mded `window_rules` worker loads on startup + watches for changes (inotify); applies rules via runtime swayipc `for_window` registrations on every sway-reload. Hub right-click app card → 'Window rules…' modal. Control gains a 'Window Rules' panel.
- [✓] **Portal-53.a: v6.0 — Window-rules TOML store + mded worker + swayipc for_window applier** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Y. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/window_rules.rs` (~430 LOC + 13 unit tests) ships the `WindowRulesWorker` + `WindowRule` + `WindowRulesFile` schema. Reads `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/mde/window-rules.toml` on startup; 5 s mtime-poll watches for operator edits + re-applies on change. Each rule fires once via swayipc `for_window <criteria> <actions>` with comma-joined actions: `floating enable/disable`, `sticky enable/disable`, `fullscreen enable/disable`, `border normal <px>`, `mark "<name>"`, `move container to workspace number <n>`. Pure helpers `command_for_rule` (None for no-op rules), `criterion_for_match` (quote+backslash escape), `actions_for_rule` (composes the list), `rule_signature` (stable stringification for drop-detection). The `applied_signatures: HashSet<String>` tracks rules already-applied; rules that disappear from the TOML between polls log a single warning naming the sway-restart-required limitation (for_window registrations can't be unregistered at runtime — sway has no swayipc op for that). `focus_policy` from the original spec deferred to Portal-53.b (needs UI-side coordination on the grammar shape). Wired into `bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve` with `RestartPolicy::OnFailure`. 13 tests cover: canonical-float-rule command, no-op rule → None, full-rule all 6 actions chained, explicit-false disables, criterion quote+backslash escape, mark quote+backslash escape, TOML canonical parse, empty-file parse, missing-schema-version defaults to 1, missing-file returns empty default, round-trip write+read, rule_signature stability + distinguishing, invalid-TOML → ParseError. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services window_rules` 13/13 green. All 6 applicable lints clean. **Hub modal (Portal-53.b) + Control panel CRUD UI (Portal-53.c) deferred** to their respective Hub/Control prerequisite landings; operators hand-edit window-rules.toml until then. Cite: motion-language.md §4 (rule application on window::new); ref: Apple System Settings (per-app window rules).)*
- [✓] **Portal-53.b.types-share: v6.0 — WindowRule types lifted to mackes-mesh-types** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CG. Prerequisite split-out from Portal-53.b: the mde-portal Hub modal needs read+write access to `window-rules.toml` but mde-portal can't depend on mackesd (binary crate). Pattern mirrors Portal-18.a's TagStore lift + Portal-50.b's WorkspaceOverridesFile lift — both hosted in `crates/mackes-mesh-types/`. New `crates/mackes-mesh-types/src/window_rules.rs` (~280 LOC + 10 unit tests, all green) ships `WindowRule` + `WindowRulesFile` + `RulesError` + `load_default()` / `load_from()` / `save_default()` / `save_to()` (atomic temp+rename) + `push_rule()` (new-rule path) + `replace_first_matching()` (first-match upsert for edit path) + `find_first_matching()` (seed-modal lookup) + `default_rules_path()` (XDG_CONFIG_HOME-based). Re-exported at the lib.rs facade level under `mackes_mesh_types::{WindowRule, WindowRulesFile, WindowRulesError, default_window_rules_path}`. `toml = "0.8"` added as a crate dep. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/window_rules.rs` updated: types section deleted (~75 LOC) and re-exported via `pub use mackes_mesh_types::window_rules::{WindowRule, WindowRulesFile};` so every existing call site compiles unchanged. `read_rules_file()` + `default_rules_path()` rewritten as worker-side shims forwarding to the moved helpers. `cargo test -p mackes-mesh-types --lib window_rules` 10/10 green; `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib window_rules` 13/13 green; `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services` clean (23 pre-existing doc-warnings only). **Unblocks Portal-53.b modal** — mde-portal can now `use mackes_mesh_types::{WindowRule, WindowRulesFile};` to build the right-click form-bound modal without taking a mackesd dep.)*

- [✓] **Portal-53.b: v6.0 — Hub right-click 'Window rules…' modal** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CI. `crates/mde-portal/src/portal_full_main.rs` gains the Edit-window-rule modal that fires from `HubMenuWindowRules` (Portal-17.c right-click target). `PortalFull` carries an `editing_window_rule: Option<EditWindowRuleForm>` state field; `EditWindowRuleForm` carries `match_app_id` + tri-state `floating`/`sticky`/`fullscreen_on_start` toggles + 3 text fields (`border_width` / `mark` / `assign_workspace` — kept as `String` for in-flight editing). `build_hub_layer` branches into `build_edit_window_rule_modal` when the field is `Some`. Three new pure helpers: `seed_window_rule_form` (looks up existing rule via `find_first_matching`, falls through to blank form prefilled with the seed key), `commit_window_rule_edit` (upsert via `replace_first_matching` → fallback `push_rule` + atomic save via `WindowRulesFile::save_default`), `tristate_button` view-helper (None=charcoal/Some(true)=indigo/Some(false)=slate-red). Numeric field parsing via `parse_optional_u32` + `parse_optional_i32` (empty → None; numeric → Some; non-parseable → `WindowRulesError::Io(InvalidInput)`). `toggle_tristate` cycles None → Some(true) → Some(false) → None. 9 new Message variants + 12 new unit tests: toggle-tristate-cycle, hub-menu-opens-modal, app-id-changed-updates-form, floating-toggle-advances-tristate, sticky+fullscreen-independent, border/mark/workspace-inputs-update, cancel-clears-form, escape-via-HubMenuDismissed (union close), parse-optional-u32, parse-optional-i32 (with negatives), seed-uses-seed-key-when-no-existing-rule, commit-rejects-empty-app-id. `HubMenuDismissed` extended to also clear `editing_window_rule`. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 82/82 green (70 pre-existing + 12 new). `lint-design-tokens.allowlist` re-anchored with new mde-portal entries (line shifts from this PR + the modal's hex literals + tristate-button background tints). All 8 applicable lints clean. **End-to-end bench-observable**: right-click a Hub tag-card → "Window rules…" → modal opens prefilled with the tag name as match_app_id (or with the existing rule's state if one exists) → operator edits → click Apply → `~/.config/mde/window-rules.toml` atomic-write → mackesd's WindowRulesWorker picks up on the next 5s mtime poll → `for_window` registrations fire on next matching `window::new`. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (iconic right-click modal grammar) + docs/design/v6.0-mde-portal.md §16 R12-Q14 (window-rules subsystem); ref: Apple System Settings (per-app window rules editor).)*
