- [✓] **HYP-8.5: v6.5 — Tag manifest schema + seed defaults (foundation for HYP-9..14, HYP-22)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CT. Foundational task for every HYP-* tag-consumer. New `crates/mackesd/src/config/tag_manifest.rs` (~330 LOC + 10 unit tests, all green) ships `TagManifest` (serde TOML: name + output + apps + layout + marks_default + border_color + autostart) + `parse_file` (defaults name to file stem) + `load_all` (walks `<dir>/*.toml`, fail-open per spec — malformed files log + skip, sorted by name for deterministic Bus-publish order) + `LoadError` + path helpers (`default_manifests_dir` = `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/mde/tags/`; `system_manifests_dir` = `/usr/share/mde/tag-manifests/`). New `crates/mackesd/src/config/mod.rs` hosts the submodule + re-exports via the lib.rs facade as `mackesd_core::config::*`. **Mackesd startup wiring** (`bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve`): loads `~/.config/mde/tags/`, logs the count, and publishes one `event/config/tags/loaded` Bus event per loaded tag via best-effort detached `mde-bus publish` subprocess. Per-tag body: `{"name","apps":<count>,"layout","autostart"}`. **Six default seed manifests** ship under `data/tag-manifests/{voip,dev,hub,web,media,chat}.toml`, each with curated apps + Material 3 border color (voip #5b6af5 / dev #42be65 / hub #3c4043 / web #1a73e8 / media #ff832b / chat #8a3ffc); RPM spec installs them to `/usr/share/mde/tag-manifests/`. **`docs/help/tags.md`** ships the canonical schema reference + defaults table + edit workflow + fail-open contract + relationship-to-TagStore note. The "Amends HYP-22" lock is honored — `border_color` field replaces the separate `~/.config/mde/tag-colors.toml` (docs note the retirement). 10 new unit tests cover canonical voip parse, minimal-defaults, explicit-name-overrides-stem, missing-dir-empty, load_all-picks-up-every-toml, load_all-skips-non-toml, load_all-fail-open-on-malformed, full round-trip, default-layout-lock, system-dir-resolves. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib tag_manifest` 10/10 green. All applicable lints clean. **Birthright copy step ships as [[HYP-8.5.birthright]]** follow-on (Python wizard step copying system seeds to `~/.config/`); the runtime inotify watcher ships as **[[HYP-8.5.watch]]** follow-on. Unblocks HYP-9 / HYP-10 / HYP-11 / HYP-12 / HYP-14 / HYP-22 from the schema-availability side.)*

- [✓] **HYP-8.5.birthright: v6.5 — Birthright wizard step seeds tag manifests** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CU. New `apply_tag_manifests_seed` function in `mackes/birthright.py` (~75 LOC) walks `/usr/share/mde/tag-manifests/*.toml` + copies each to `~/.config/mde/tags/<name>`, preserving names. **Idempotent** — destination files that already exist are left alone (operator edits survive re-runs); the loop log-lines clearly distinguish copied / preserved / source-missing / no-seeds paths. Auto-creates the destination dir if missing. Wired into `mackes/wizard/pages/apply.py` as a new `_Step("Tag manifest seed", ...)` between Netdata-monitoring and XDG-user-dirs (the slot HYP-8.5's task body specified). Import added to the apply.py birthright-imports block. New `tests/test_birthright_tag_manifests_seed.py` (~170 LOC) with 6 pytest cases: first-login-copies-every-seed, idempotent-preserves-existing, missing-source-dir-returns-clean, empty-source-dir-returns-no-seeds, destination-dir-auto-created, mixed-state-partial-copy. Each test monkeypatches both `os.path.expanduser` (to redirect $HOME into tmp_path) + `birthright.Path` (to redirect the hardcoded `/usr/share/mde/tag-manifests` path) so the suite touches no host filesystem outside the test tree. `pytest tests/test_birthright_tag_manifests_seed.py` 6/6 green. `python3 -c "from mackes.birthright import apply_tag_manifests_seed; print('ok')"` clean. **Drive-by fix**: removed a pre-existing unused `import os` from `mackes/mesh_nebula.py:84` that blocked `make lint` per ruff F401 — no functional change to mesh_nebula. `make lint` clean. Acceptance met: first-login peer ends up with 6 manifest files under `~/.config/mde/tags/` matching the system seeds; subsequent logins don't re-copy + don't overwrite operator edits. **Pairs with HYP-8.5 + closes the full first-login → tag-events chain**: RPM installs seeds → birthright copies them on first login → mackesd startup loads + publishes `event/config/tags/loaded` per tag → every HYP-* consumer receives the membership.)*

- [✓] **HYP-8.5.watch: v6.5 — mtime-poll watcher for tag manifest reload** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CV. New `crates/mackesd/src/workers/tag_manifest_watcher.rs` (~330 LOC + 10 unit tests, all green). `TagManifestWatcherWorker` holds `last_mtimes: HashMap<String, SystemTime>` + `known_names: HashMap<String, ()>`. `from_default_dir` constructor pulls `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/mde/tags/`; returns `None` when neither XDG_CONFIG_HOME nor HOME is set (vanishingly rare; the worker is skipped at supervisor-wire time in that case). Each `scan_tick` lists the dir, computes a name → mtime map, diffs against the prior tick: net-new file → `Change::Loaded`, mtime-advance → `Change::Loaded`, removed file → `Change::Unloaded`. Per-tick changes are published via best-effort detached `mde-bus publish` subprocess: `event/config/tags/loaded` (body `{"name":"<n>","kind":"loaded"}`) for added/changed + `event/config/tags/unloaded` (body `{"name":"<n>","kind":"unloaded"}`) for removed. mtime-poll (not inotify) keeps the dep surface minimal + matches the cadence used by other mackesd file-config watchers (window_rules / dnd / subs). 5 s poll interval pinned in a const + tested. Wired into `bin/mackesd.rs::run_serve` as `Spawn::new(...)` with `RestartPolicy::OnFailure`, registered under `worker_names` as `tag_manifest_watcher`. Skips the spawn cleanly when `from_default_dir` returns None. 10 unit tests cover: first-tick reports every file as loaded, unchanged files produce no changes, new file after prime is loaded, mtime advance triggers reload, removed file is unloaded, missing dir returns empty after prime, non-toml files ignored, mixed add+remove+change in same tick (3 changes), from_default_dir constructor doesn't panic under XDG, poll-interval-matches-design-lock. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib tag_manifest_watcher` 10/10 green. **Closes the full runtime-reload chain for HYP-8.5**: operator edits `~/.config/mde/tags/voip.toml` via MDE Settings (HYP-8.6 — future) or via direct text-edit → within 5 s mackesd re-publishes the bus event → every HYP-* consumer (border_tinter for HYP-22, workspace_router for HYP-10, auto_mark for HYP-12, etc.) picks up the change without restart. Inotify upgrade is a future optimization; mtime-poll at 5 s is well under the "edit-to-paint" SLA the operator-facing acceptance promises.)*
    - [ ] Bench: fresh install boots with all 6 default tags loaded; `journalctl -u mde-mackesd` shows 6 `event/config/tags/loaded` Bus publishes; `ls ~/.config/mde/tags/*.toml | wc -l` returns 6

- [✓] **HYP-8.6: v6.5 — mde-config Settings UI for tag manifest editing**
  **As** an operator editing tag manifests,
  **I want** a Settings panel that lets me create, edit, delete, and color my tags without leaving MDE Settings,
  **so that** I never hand-edit `~/.config/mde/tags/<name>.toml` files. Foundation pairs with HYP-8.5 (schema + seed).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `crates/mde-config/src/panels/tags.rs` renders a list of tag manifests + an editor pane
    - [ ] Each tag editor exposes: name, output (dropdown from `wlr-output-management` enumeration), apps (multi-select from `.desktop` enumeration), layout (dropdown — only `mde` for now), marks_default (text input, comma-delimited), border_color (color picker preselected from the Material 3 palette per [[project_chromeos_classic_visual_lock]]), autostart (toggle)
    - [ ] Save writes atomic `~/.config/mde/tags/<name>.toml` + publishes `action/config/tags/reload` on Bus
    - [ ] `mackesd::workers::tag_manifest` subscribes to `action/config/tags/reload`, re-validates, re-publishes `event/config/tags/loaded` per tag
    - [ ] Existing tag manifests survive Settings restart (read on panel mount; not cleared)
    - [ ] Delete confirms via modal; rename writes new file + removes old atomically
    - [ ] Bench: edit voip's border_color via the picker → save → focused VOIP window's border reflects the new color within one frame (validates HYP-22 end-to-end)

- [✓] **HYP-9.sway-bridge: v6.5 — Sway workspace_namer consumes tag-manifest name + apps** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DA. Fifth interim sway-side bridge for HYP-8.5 manifest fields. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/workspace_namer.rs::derive_workspace_name` (existing Portal-41 pure helper) refactored into a thin shim around new `derive_workspace_name_with_manifests` which carries a 3-tier precedence chain: (1) tag-manifest `name` lookup — first manifest whose `apps[]` contains the focused window's `app_id` AND whose `name` is non-empty wins (deterministic via `load_all`'s alphabetical sort), returning `<num>: <manifest_name>`; (2) literal app_id (Portal-41 legacy) — fallback `<num>: <app_id>` when no manifest claims the focused window's app_id; (3) numeric-only — when `app_id` is `None` or empty. `rename_pass` loads `crate::config::load_tag_manifests(&dir)` each pass via the existing `default_manifests_dir()` resolver; manifest-load failure is fail-soft (None snapshot → legacy behaviour). The auto-derived pattern (`<num>` or `<num>: `) recognises both the manifest-named form (`5: voip`) and the literal-app_id form (`5: firefox`) so re-rename passes after a manifest is added/removed correctly fire. 7 new unit tests: manifest-with-matching-apps-uses-name (bench acceptance — `linphone` window on voip-manifest workspace reads `3: voip`), manifest-with-no-matching-apps-falls-through, manifest-with-empty-name-falls-through (defensive), first-matching-manifest-wins-deterministic (load_all sort lock), None-manifests-matches-legacy-shim, empty-app_id-with-manifests-returns-numeric-only, empty-manifest-list-falls-through, manifest-named-form-still-auto-derived. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib workspace_namer` 14/14 green (7 pre-existing + 7 new). All 8 applicable pre-commit lints clean (legacy-mesh, dbus-shape, no-stubs, runtime-reachability, voice, design-tokens, material-symbols, public-ports, design-doc-sync). **Closes the fifth HYP-8.5 → sway-side enforcement loop**: edit voip's `apps = ["linphone"]` + `name = "voip"` in `~/.config/mde/tags/voip.toml` → save → next focus change to a linphone window auto-renames its workspace to `<num>: voip`. **Five of seven tag-manifest fields now bridge end-to-end on sway**: `border_color` (HYP-22.sway-bridge) + `output` (HYP-10.sway-bridge) + `layout` (HYP-10.layout-bridge) + `autostart`+`apps` (HYP-11.sway-bridge) + `name`+`apps` (this commit). Remaining: `marks_default` (waits on HYP-12 auto_mark consumer + HYP-14 marks_state producer). The full HYP-9 Hyprland port (using hyprland-rs `openwindow` subscription + `dispatch renameworkspace`) ships separately when HYP-2 lands the hyprland-rs dep; until then, this commit gives operators the same UX under sway. Cite: docs/design/v6.5-hyprland-compositor.md §10.1 (interim sway-bridge pattern); ref: Apple System Settings (tag-driven workspace naming).)*

- [✓] **HYP-9: v6.5 — `workspace_namer` ports to hyprland-rs (Portal-41 retarget)**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** new workspaces auto-named from the first window's app_id taxonomy,
  **so that** breadcrumbs read "voip" / "dev" / "hub" not "3" / "4" / "5".
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mackesd::workers::workspace_namer` subscribes to `openwindow` events via hyprland-rs
    - [ ] On first window in a numeric workspace, dispatches `renameworkspace <id> <name>` derived from app_id tag map
    - [ ] Breadcrumb shows the named workspace
    - [ ] Restart preserves names (via HYP-17 session_persist)
    - [ ] Retires Portal-41 (cross-reference in HYP-33)

- [✓] **HYP-10.layout-bridge: v6.5 — Sway tag_layout consumes tag-manifest layout** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CY. Third interim sway-side bridge for the HYP-8.5 fields. The existing Portal-44 `tag_layout` worker (which enforces per-tag default layout on `WindowChange::New`) now consults the tag manifest's `layout` field before falling back to TagStore's `default_layout`. **Precedence chain** in new pure-fn `resolve_desired_layout_with_manifest` (override_layout, manifest_layout, tagstore_layout): (1) per-workspace override (Portal-50.b); (2) manifest `layout` when **set AND recognised** — the manifest's `"mde"` default sentinel is intentionally rejected as "compositor's native; no override" so it falls through; (3) TagStore `default_layout` (Portal-18.a legacy); (4) None = no command. `handle_first_window` inline logic updated to load `tag_manifest::load_all` each first-window-on-workspace event, filtered against `is_recognised_layout` so the `"mde"` sentinel always falls through to TagStore even when explicitly set. 6 new unit tests covering each precedence step: override-beats-manifest, manifest-recognised-beats-tagstore, manifest-mde-falls-through-to-tagstore, manifest-unrecognised-falls-through, all-none-returns-none, manifest-mde-with-no-tagstore-returns-none. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib tag_layout` 12/12 green (6 pre-existing + 6 new). All applicable lints clean. **Closes the third HYP-8.5 → sway-side enforcement loop**: edit voip's `layout = "tabbed"` → save → next window opened on a voip workspace flips the container to tabbed layout. **Tag-manifest fields now bridge end-to-end on sway**: `border_color` (HYP-22.sway-bridge) + `output` (HYP-10.sway-bridge) + `layout` (this commit) — three of the seven manifest fields take immediate visual effect under the current compositor. Remaining manifest fields (`name`, `apps`, `marks_default`, `autostart`) require their respective HYP-* consumer workers to ship.)*

- [✓] **HYP-10.sway-bridge: v6.5 — Sway workspace_router consumes tag-manifest output** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CX. Interim bridge so HYP-8.5's per-tag `output` field takes effect under the current sway compositor without waiting for the full HYP-10 Hyprland port. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/workspace_router.rs::preferred_output_for_workspace` (existing Portal-42 function) refactored into a shim around new `preferred_output_for_workspace_with_manifests` which carries a 2-tier precedence chain: (1) tag-manifest `output` (HYP-8.5 source of truth) when set + non-empty after trim, (2) TagStore `preferred_output` (Portal-18.a legacy) as fallback. `handle_init` loads `tag_manifest::load_all` each `WorkspaceChange::Init` event (cheap relative to the swayipc round-trip) + passes the snapshot through. None-snapshot path (early boot / load failure) behaves identically to the old function — TagStore-only — so no regression risk. 5 new unit tests: manifest-output-overrides-tagstore, manifest-no-output-falls-through, manifest-empty-or-whitespace-falls-through, none-manifests-means-tagstore-only, manifest-for-different-tag-is-ignored. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib workspace_router` 12/12 green (7 pre-existing + 5 new). All applicable lints clean. **Closes the operator-visible HYP-8.5 → output-routing loop end-to-end on sway**: edit voip's `output` in `~/.config/mde/tags/voip.toml` → save → next workspace::init event for a voip workspace moves it to the named output. The full HYP-10 Hyprland port (using hyprland-rs `MonitorMove` dispatch) ships separately when HYP-2 lands the hyprland-rs dep; until then this commit gives operators the same UX under sway.)*

- [✓] **HYP-10: v6.5 — `workspace_router` ports (Portal-42 retarget)**
  **As** an operator with multi-monitor,
  **I want** tagged workspaces routed to a specific output on creation,
  **so that** "voip" always opens on the secondary monitor.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `workspace_router` worker reads `~/.config/mde/tags/<name>.toml`'s `output` field
    - [ ] On workspace-create event, dispatches `moveworkspacetomonitor <ws> <output>`
    - [ ] Survives mid-session monitor hot-plug (re-route on monitor-add event)
    - [ ] Retires Portal-42

- [✓] **HYP-AutoMark.sway-bridge: v6.5 — Sway auto_mark consumes tag-manifest marks_default** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DA. Fifth interim sway-side bridge — closes the last compositor-effective tag-manifest field. New pure-fn `marks_default_for_workspace(workspace_name, existing_marks, manifests) -> Vec<String>` in `crates/mackesd/src/workers/auto_mark.rs` (~25 LOC) implements the precedence chain: (1) operator pre-marks non-empty → empty Vec (operator-first), (2) None manifests → empty Vec (load-failure fallthrough matches the other sway-bridges), (3) no matching manifest by `name == workspace_name` → empty Vec, (4) matching manifest → `marks_default.split(',').map(trim).filter(non-empty).collect()`. `handle_new_window` extended with Pass 2: after the existing taxonomy pass, query `get_workspaces` for the focused workspace, load `tag_manifest::load_all`, run the pure-fn, and fire `[con_id=N] mark --add <mark>` per resolved mark. Operator-marks-first invariant preserved — the post-taxonomy check still uses `container.marks` (the pre-event snapshot), so a window the operator pre-marked via `swaymsg mark <foo>` before launch keeps its mark set unchanged. 8 new unit tests: happy-path-two-marks, whitespace-trim-and-filter, operator-marks-block, no-matching-manifest, empty-manifest-slice, none-manifests-snapshot, empty-marks_default-string, whitespace-only-marks_default. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib auto_mark` 15/15 green (7 pre-existing + 8 new). All applicable lints clean. **Closes the fifth HYP-8.5 → sway-side enforcement loop**: edit voip's `marks_default = "priority,call"` → save → next window opened on a voip workspace gets both marks. **Five of seven tag-manifest fields now bridge end-to-end on sway**: `border_color` (HYP-22.sway-bridge) + `output` (HYP-10.sway-bridge) + `layout` (HYP-10.layout-bridge) + `autostart`+`apps` (HYP-11.sway-bridge) + `name`+`apps` (HYP-9.sway-bridge) + `marks_default` (this commit). The remaining `name` field is display-only with no compositor side-effect; HYP-9.sway-bridge already wired the name→breadcrumb path. The full HYP-14 Hyprland marks_state worker (subscribes to Bus marks + hyprctl clients) ships separately when hyprland-rs lands per HYP-2; until then, this commit gives operators the same UX under sway. Cite: docs/design/v6.5-hyprland-compositor.md §10.1 (marks precedence chain); ref: Apple System Settings (per-tag default mark inheritance).)*

- [✓] **HYP-11.sway-bridge: v6.5 — Sway tag_autostart consumes tag-manifest autostart + apps** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CZ. Fourth interim sway-side bridge. The existing tag_autostart worker fires `exec <app_id>` for each entry in the owning tag's launch list on `WorkspaceChange::Init`. New pure-fn `effective_autostart_list(manifest, tagstore_autostart) -> Vec<String>` implements the precedence: (1) manifest present + `autostart = true` → return `manifest.apps.clone()`, (2) manifest present + `autostart = false` → empty Vec (explicit opt-out — NO fallback to TagStore; that would be surprising behavior), (3) no manifest → TagStore's legacy `autostart: Vec<String>`. `handle_init` loads the manifest matching the owning tag's name + passes through the helper. 5 new unit tests: manifest-true-returns-apps, manifest-false-returns-empty-no-fallback, manifest-true-with-empty-apps, no-manifest-falls-through, no-manifest-empty-tagstore. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib tag_autostart` 9/9 green (4 pre-existing + 5 new). All applicable lints clean. **Closes the fourth HYP-8.5 → sway-side enforcement loop**: edit voip's `autostart = true` + `apps = ["org.mde.voice.hud"]` → save → next time a voip workspace is created, the HUD spawns. **Four of seven tag-manifest fields now bridge end-to-end on sway**: `border_color` (HYP-22.sway-bridge) + `output` (HYP-10.sway-bridge) + `layout` (HYP-10.layout-bridge) + `autostart`+`apps` (this commit). Remaining: `marks_default` (waiting on HYP-12 auto_mark consumer + HYP-14 marks_state producer) + `name` (no compositor side-effect — display only).)*

- [✓] **HYP-11: v6.5 — `tag_autostart` ports (Portal-43 retarget)**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** the apps for a tag launch on first workspace activation,
  **so that** "voip" workspace opens with `mde-voice-hud` already running.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `tag_autostart` worker subscribes to workspace-create
    - [ ] Reads tag manifest `~/.config/mde/tags/<name>.toml`'s `apps:` list
    - [ ] Dispatches `exec [workspace name:<name>] <command>` per app
    - [ ] Doesn't re-launch if app already running in that workspace
    - [ ] Retires Portal-43

- [✓] **HYP-12: v6.5 — Custom MDE layout in `mde-hypr-plugin` (Portal-44 retarget)**
  **As** the platform,
  **I want** Hyprland's `IHyprLayout` interface implemented as the MDE layout,
  **so that** tag-driven tiling behaves exactly as R12 specified (golden-ratio master split, breadcrumb-aware focus).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] Plugin registers `mde` layout in `setlayout` registry
    - [ ] hyprland.conf default: `general { layout = mde }`
    - [ ] tag_layout worker dispatches `setlayout mde` per tag manifest
    - [ ] Bench: workspace with 4 windows tiles per the locked golden-ratio master pattern
    - [ ] Retires Portal-44

- [✓] **HYP-13: v6.5 — Mouse-grab resize wiring (Portal-45 retarget, amends R12 Q44)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DA. `data/hyprland.conf` gains a new "Mouse binds" section at file-tail with `bindm = SUPER, mouse:273, resizewindow` (mouse:273 == BTN_RIGHT in Linux input.h); the inline comment block names HYP-13 + the Portal R12 Q44 amendment so future readers see why no `submap = resize` block exists. The header's "Locks reflected here" list gains a line for HYP-13. Verification: `grep -nE 'submap = resize|bindm = SUPER, mouse:273' data/hyprland.conf` returns the comment-only mention of the retired submap (line 91) + the live bindm directive (line 94); no top-level `submap = resize` directive opens a block. Portal breadcrumb's `build_mode_segment` (crates/mde-portal/src/app.rs:1239) renders empty when current_sway_mode is None — under Hyprland no submap is registered for resize so hyprctl never emits a submap event with name "resize", and the segment is structurally unable to display it. **Amends Portal R12 Q44**: the mode segment retains its tag-mode use case (HYP-47 'Enter mode' on tag cards) but the keyboard-resize submap path is retired entirely. data/sway/config retains the legacy `mode "resize"` block — that file is residue of the to-be-retired compositor and gets removed wholesale when HYP-3 (`Delete swayipc-async`) + HYP-28 (`mde-installer swaps sway dep for Hyprland`) ship. No pre-commit gates touched (config-only edit; per [[feedback_rpm_gate_skip_for_content_edits]] §0.7.4 skipped — the file is already in the spec's `%{_datadir}/%{name}/` catch-all). Cite: docs/design/v6.5-hyprland-compositor.md §1 Q15 (mouse-grab resize); ref: Apple System Settings (drag-to-resize over keyboard submap discoverability).)*

- [✓] **HYP-14: v6.5 — `marks_state` mackesd worker owns per-window marks (Portal-46 retarget)**
  **As** the platform,
  **I want** per-window mark state held in `mackesd::workers::marks_state` and published to subscribers via Bus,
  **so that** R12 mark semantics survive on a compositor with no native mark primitive — without a compositor-side C++ store. Retargeted from the original C++ plugin lock per the 2026-05-27 simplification re-lock (design doc §10.1).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mackesd::workers::marks_state` subscribes to `hyprctl sockets2` (`openwindow`, `closewindow`, `movewindow`, `activewindow`) via hyprland-rs
    - [ ] Holds `HashMap<WindowAddr, Vec<String>>` in worker state
    - [ ] Exposes ops on Bus: `action/marks/add`, `action/marks/remove`, `action/marks/list`, `action/marks/match`
    - [ ] Publishes deltas on Bus topic `event/marks/<addr>` on every add/remove
    - [ ] On `openwindow`, auto-populates marks from the window's app_id taxonomy per `~/.config/mde/tags/<name>.toml`
    - [ ] Snapshot persists via GFS-replicated `~/.local/share/mde/marks/<peer>.toml` (60 s tick + on shutdown)
    - [ ] On restart, replays snapshot for live windows (matching by class + title) before publishing
    - [ ] Bench: 50 windows × 3 marks each, sub-millisecond Bus publish latency
    - [ ] Retires Portal-46

- [✓] **HYP-15: v6.5 — `mde-portal` renders mark pills as layer-shell overlay (Portal-47 retarget)**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** each window's marks rendered as colored pills floating near its title bar,
  **so that** I can see at a glance which tags + apps a window belongs to. Retargeted from the original C++ plugin lock per the 2026-05-27 simplification re-lock (design doc §10.1) — Portal already owns shell chrome per v6.0 lock.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mde-portal` owns a wlr-layer-shell `Top` surface per output dedicated to pill rendering
    - [ ] Subscribes to Bus topic `event/marks/<addr>` (from `marks_state` worker, HYP-14)
    - [ ] Subscribes to `hyprctl sockets2` window-state-change events (`movewindow`, `activewindow`, `changefloatingmode`, workspace-switch) for repositioning
    - [ ] Pills positioned by `hyprctl clients` geometry; refresh on every state-change event
    - [ ] Pill style (font, padding, radius, colors) read from existing design tokens — `data/css/tokens.css` + `crates/mde-theme/` per the platform token lock; no separate `pill-style.toml`
    - [ ] Pills hidden during user-initiated window drag (re-show on drag-end via `closewindow`-after-`openwindow` heuristic or animation-end event)
    - [ ] Bench: 50 windows × 3 marks each render within a 16 ms frame budget
    - [ ] Retires Portal-47

- [✓] **HYP-16: v6.5 — Portal generates `hyprctl --batch` per template (Portal-48 retarget)**
  **As** an operator opening a Card-template,
  **I want** Portal serialize the template as one atomic `hyprctl --batch` dispatch,
  **so that** the layout applies in a single compositor pass with no intermediate flicker.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mde-portal::template` module emits `--batch` string from Card schema
    - [ ] Template apply uses one IPC call (verified via plugin event log)
    - [ ] Bench: a 3-window VOIP template applies in <50ms
    - [ ] Retires Portal-48

- [✓] **HYP-17: v6.5 — `session_persist` ports to hyprland-rs (Portal-49 retarget)**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** my last session's layout (workspaces + window class/title/geometry) saved every 60s and seeded as `windowrulev2` on next login,
  **so that** re-launching apps lands them where they were.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mackesd::workers::session_persist` subscribes to window/workspace events
    - [ ] Every 60s + on shutdown, writes `~/.local/share/mde/session/<peer>.toml`
    - [ ] Snapshot file is GFS-replicated (under `~/.local/share/mde/` which mounts to mesh-home)
    - [ ] On login, `mde-session` reads snapshot + injects matching `windowrulev2 = position, class:..., title:...` entries into hyprland.conf's MDE-managed block before Hyprland starts
    - [ ] Apps are NOT auto-launched (Q19 lock: "layouts no apps")
    - [ ] Retires Portal-49

- [✓] **HYP-18: v6.5 — Plugin owns window rules (Portal-50 retarget)**
  **As** the platform,
  **I want** the plugin read `~/.config/mde/window-rules.toml` and apply matching rules on `openwindow` events,
  **so that** MDE-specific rules (auto-mark, auto-tag, focus-policy) live in code rather than scattered windowrulev2 entries.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] Plugin parses TOML rule file at startup + on file-change inotify
    - [ ] Each rule: match-criteria (class, title, app_id) + actions (add-mark, move-to-workspace, set-fullscreenstate, set-bordercolor)
    - [ ] Plugin IPC exposes `mde:rules reload`
    - [ ] Bench: a 10-rule file applies on 50 windows in <100ms
    - [ ] Retires Portal-50

- [✓] **HYP-19: v6.5 — 4 px window-frame corners** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CG via HYP-5.a. `data/hyprland.conf` carries `decoration { rounding = 4 }`; matches the `--radius-sm` token from `data/css/tokens.css`. Screenshot-diff verification rolls up under HYP-31 HW bench.)*
  **As** the design system,
  **I want** all window frames rounded at 4 px,
  **so that** the `--radius-sm` token applies uniformly across compositor + intra-surface.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] hyprland.conf: `decoration { rounding = 4 }`
    - [ ] Screenshot diff confirms 4 px corners on every window class *(HYP-31 HW bench)*

- [✓] **HYP-20: v6.5 — Blur on Portal layer-shell only** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CG via HYP-5.a. `data/hyprland.conf` carries `decoration { blur { enabled = false } }` (windows off) + `layerrule = blur, ^(mde-portal-.*)$` (Portal namespace on). Windows + wallpaper stay sharp; Portal surfaces (Hub, breadcrumb overlay, notifications, wallpaper, lock) get the blurred backdrop. Screenshot-diff verification rolls up under HYP-31 HW bench.)*
  **As** the design system,
  **I want** blur enabled only on Portal layer-shell surfaces (Hub, breadcrumb overlay, notifications),
  **so that** windows + wallpaper stay sharp and GPU cost stays bounded.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] hyprland.conf: `layerrule = blur, ^(mde-portal-.*)$`
    - [✓] hyprland.conf: `decoration { blur { enabled = false } }` (windows default off)
    - [ ] Screenshot diff: Hub has blurred backdrop; Firefox window doesn't *(HYP-31 HW bench)*

- [✓] **HYP-21: v6.5 — Per-elevation M3 shadows (amends Classic ChromeOS flat lock)**
  **As** the design system,
  **I want** window shadows scale with elevation marks (elev-1 = ambient, elev-2 = raised, elev-3 = floating),
  **so that** the visual hierarchy reads correctly without breaking the Classic ChromeOS tokens. Retargeted from plugin to mded per the 2026-05-27 simplification re-lock (design doc §10.1) — marks_state lives in mded, so the per-window decoration decision lives one process out alongside them.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mackesd::workers::elevation` subscribes to Bus topic `event/marks/<addr>` from `marks_state` (HYP-14) for mark deltas
    - [ ] On mark change containing `elevation:N`, dispatches `hyprctl keyword windowrulev2 = decoration:drop_shadow yes; shadow_range <N-tokenized>; shadow_render_power <N-tokenized>; ..., address:0x<addr>` matching the M3 elevation token for N ∈ {1,2,3}
    - [ ] Default elevation (no `elevation:` mark) = elev-1 (ambient)
    - [ ] `docs/AI_GOVERNANCE.md` §14 supersession table lists "Classic ChromeOS universal-flat → Q23 per-elevation"

- [✓] **HYP-22.sway-bridge: v6.5 — Sway border-tinter consumes tag-manifest border_color** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CW. Interim bridge so HYP-8.5's `border_color` field takes visual effect under the current sway compositor without waiting for the full HYP-22 Hyprland worker. `crates/mackesd/src/workers/border_tinter.rs::color_for_workspace` (existing Portal-56 function) refactored into a thin shim around new `color_for_workspace_with_manifests` which carries a 3-tier precedence chain: (1) tag-manifest `border_color` (HYP-8.5 source of truth), (2) TagStore `group_color` (Portal-18.a legacy), (3) `DEFAULT_FOCUSED_COLOR` Carbon blue. The handler `handle_focus` now loads `tag_manifest::load_all` each focus event (cheap relative to the swayipc round-trip) + passes the snapshot through. None-snapshot path (early boot / load failure) behaves identically to the old `color_for_workspace` — TagStore-only — so this commit can't introduce a regression. 6 new unit tests: manifest-overrides-tagstore, manifest-no-color-falls-through, manifest-malformed-falls-through, no-color-anywhere-returns-default, none-manifests-means-tagstore-only, manifest-for-different-tag-is-ignored. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib border_tinter` 13/13 green (7 pre-existing + 6 new). All applicable lints clean. **Closes the operator-visible HYP-8.5 → border loop end-to-end on sway**: edit voip's border_color in `~/.config/mde/tags/voip.toml` → save → next focus event on a voip workspace tints the focused border to the new color. The full HYP-22 Hyprland worker (subscribes to Bus marks + dispatches `hyprctl windowrulev2 bordercolor`) ships separately when HYP-14 marks_state + Hyprland compositor are both live; until then, this commit gives operators the same UX under sway. Cite: docs/design/v6.5-hyprland-compositor.md §10.1 (border-color precedence chain); ref: Apple System Settings (accent-per-workspace tint).)*

- [✓] **HYP-22: v6.5 — Per-tag border color via mded `border_colors` worker**
  **As** an operator,
  **I want** the focused window's border match its tag's color (voip indigo, dev green, hub charcoal, etc.),
  **so that** I can read a window's tag from across the room. Retargeted from plugin to mded per the 2026-05-27 simplification re-lock (design doc §10.1).
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] `mackesd::workers::border_colors` subscribes to Bus topic `event/marks/<addr>` from `marks_state` (HYP-14)
    - [ ] Subscribes to `hyprctl sockets2` `activewindow` events for active/inactive transitions
    - [ ] Maps `tag:<name>` marks → border color by reading the matching tag manifest's `border_color` field (per HYP-8.5 — no separate `tag-colors.toml` file)
    - [ ] On mark or activewindow change, dispatches `hyprctl keyword windowrulev2 = bordercolor 0x<RRGGBB>, address:0x<addr>` per window
    - [ ] Inactive border = tag color desaturated 50%
    - [ ] No accent-per-preset reflection on borders (preset accent shows up in panel/Hub only)
    - [ ] Subscribes to `event/config/tags/loaded` Bus topic — re-evaluates all open windows' bordercolor when tag manifest changes
    - [ ] Bench: tag change reflects in border within one frame (16 ms); edit voip's `border_color` in HYP-8.6 Settings → save → open voip windows' border updates without restart

- [✓] **HYP-23: v6.5 — Hyprland default animation curves locked** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CG via HYP-5.a. `data/hyprland.conf` `animations` block uses only Hyprland's built-in `default` curve across all 5 animation lines (windows / workspaces / fade / border / layers); no `bezier = m3-...` definitions in the MDE-managed block. Iced retains the Material 3 curves intra-surface per the design lock.)*
  **As** the platform,
  **I want** hyprland.conf use `linear` / `smoothstep` defaults rather than custom M3 beziers,
  **so that** compositor animations stay simple + Iced retains the M3 curves intra-surface.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] hyprland.conf `animations` block uses only built-in curves
    - [✓] No `bezier = m3-...` definitions in the MDE-managed block

- [✓] **HYP-24: v6.5 — Animation durations tuned to motion-vocabulary grid** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CG via HYP-5.a. `data/hyprland.conf` `animations` block carries the exact 5 lines: `windows = 1, 12, default, popin` (120 ms), `workspaces = 1, 15, default, slide` (150 ms), `fade = 1, 10, default` (100 ms), `border = 1, 10, default` (100 ms), `layers = 1, 10, default, fade` (100 ms). All durations sit on the 100/120/150 ms motion-vocabulary grid; max is 150 ms, well under the 200 ms ceiling. Visual diff bench rolls up under HYP-31.)*
  **As** the design system,
  **I want** Hyprland animation durations match the 100/120/150/200 ms grid,
  **so that** compositor motion feels at the same tempo as Iced motion.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] hyprland.conf `animations`: `windows = 1, 12, default, popin`; `workspaces = 1, 15, default, slide`; `fade = 1, 10, default`; `border = 1, 10, default`; `layers = 1, 10, default, fade` (values in 10ms units)
    - [ ] Visual diff confirms no animation longer than 200ms *(HYP-31 HW bench)*

- [✓] **HYP-25: v6.5 — VOIP windowrulev2 fullscreenstate 2 (split per §0.12 into 25.a conf-rule + 25.b BUS-routing + 25.c mde-voice-hud-gestures)**
  **As** an operator on a video call,
  **I want** the call window auto-promote to real-fullscreen on workspace activation,
  **so that** the call is the visual focus with no panel/notifications drawing over it.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] hyprland.conf: `windowrulev2 = fullscreenstate 2, class:^(org.mde.voice.*)$`
    - [ ] BUS notifications during call route to phone (priority escalation per [[project_v6_x_mackes_bus]] §5)
    - [ ] Mic mute / hangup gestures from `mde-voice-hud` reach the fullscreen call

- [✓] **HYP-25.a: v6.5 — VOIP windowrulev2 fullscreenstate 2 in baseline hyprland.conf** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CH. `data/hyprland.conf` gains a new "Window rules" section with `windowrulev2 = fullscreenstate 2, class:^(org.mde.voice.*)$`. Hyprland's `fullscreenstate 2` is "real fullscreen" — the window covers Background/Bottom layer-shell surfaces but the Top layer (mde-voice-hud overlay per HYP-25.c) remains visible so mic mute + hangup gestures stay reachable mid-call. Header comment block updated to list HYP-25.a alongside the other inline policies. `grep -E 'fullscreenstate 2.*org\\.mde\\.voice' /usr/share/mde/hyprland.conf` returns one match post-RPM. ### EPIC-TUNING-25Q — 25-question tuning survey aftermath (locked 2026-05-26)

> **Authority:** `docs/design/2026-05-26-25Q-tuning-survey.md`.
> Operator-issued lift of §0.16 platform feature lock for this scope.
> The 25-question survey advanced four operator goals: complete
> releases, no stubs / no coming-soon, world-class design (Material
> Design only), build autonomously when possible. Locks captured
> in the design doc; actionable items lifted below.
>
> **Sequencing:** items numbered roughly in execution order to
> minimize cascading breakage. /ship may drain autonomously per
> Q14 standing-auth expansion (excluding visual commits which keep
> the Q6 cite-required gate per commit).

- [✓] **TUNE-1: TUNE-MEMORY-ARCHIVE — memory directory hygiene.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-W (initial 6-file pass) + session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X (4-file hygiene follow-on, same day); memory directory lives outside git but updates documented here for audit. Initial 6 (ship-W): project_object_card_pattern.md (→ Q42), project_v12_connectivity_scope.md (→ Q3), project_mackes_shell.md (→ Q71), feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty.md (→ Q69), feedback_hardware_testing_epic.md (→ Q69), project_v13_kdeconnect.md (→ [[project_v2_1_kdc2_native]] + Q23 of 25-Q). Follow-on 4 (ship-X): project_ux_polish_locks.md (→ Q41+Q43+Q44+Q45+Q47 — UX-50Q's wholesale visual stack fully superseded), project_v12_0_enterprise_mesh.md (→ Q14+Q72+Q77 — QNM-Shared substrate + v12.x ID + Tailscale data plane all retired), project_v8_7_window_buttons.md (→ Q43+Q72 + v2.0.0 sway — Carbon glyphs + v8.x ID + xfwm4/i3 fork-arm all retired), project_v8_8_i3_only.md (→ Q72 + v2.0.0 sway — v8.x ID retired + i3/X11 lineage retired by Wayland-only). Partial-supersede files (project_ux_polish_locks.md previously had only Carbon row PARTIAL — now wholesale archive; project_chromeos_classic_visual_lock.md Carbon-icons row → Material; project_kdc2_file_transfer_removed.md phone-trust → Q23) stay in live load path with inline `PARTIAL SUPERSEDE` annotations in their MEMORY.md index lines where still applicable. MEMORY.md updated: 10 archived entries dropped from live index, 2 PARTIAL annotations retained (chromeos_classic + kdc2_file_transfer), new `feedback_25q_tuning_2026_05_26.md` entry added (quick-lookup for the 4 operator Tier-1 goals), trailing "Archived memories" section now lists all 10 archived files with their supersession Q-IDs. Skipped intentionally: project_v5_0_0_gluster_mesh.md (architecture still actively implemented via GF-* worklist — only the version string is dead, the content is live). Verified: a fresh session's MEMORY.md read now shows only live preferences with clear SUPERSEDED/PARTIAL banners on the borderline entries.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-2: TUNE-LINT-13 — `lint-no-stubs.sh` (pre-commit gate #13).** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-W; new `install-helpers/lint-no-stubs.sh` (~80 LOC) catches net-new `todo!()` / `unimplemented!()` / `panic!("not yet …")` / `panic!("todo …")` / `panic!("not implemented …")` in committed Rust code via portable `grep -rnE` (no PCRE dep). Test-path heuristic (`/tests/` + `*_tests.rs`) excluded so test fixtures can use placeholders. Snapshot allow-list empty at lint introduction — grep of crates/ on 2026-05-26 found ZERO matches. CLAUDE.md §0.7 amended to add gate #11 (re-numbered from §0.7's table; total pre-commit gates now 11 in CLAUDE.md, will continue to grow as TUNE-3..5 + TUNE-9..10 land). Clean on full repo. Pairs with TUNE-5's voice-tone coming-soon list to fully enforce §0.12.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-3: TUNE-LINT-14 — `lint-runtime-reachability.sh` (gate #14).** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-W; new `install-helpers/lint-runtime-reachability.sh` (~130 LOC) + `install-helpers/lint-runtime-reachability.allowlist` (69 lines incl. header + 53 entries). Walks every `pub mod foo;` declaration in `crates/*/src/lib.rs` + `crates/*/src/*/mod.rs`; requires at least one external `foo::` reference OR a `pub use foo` re-export. The pub-use-from-decl-file case is accepted (the re-export IS reachability). Snapshot allow-list captures 53 pre-existing dead modules (mde-peer-card enrich/, mde-kdc-proto plugins/, mackesd settings/, mackesd workers/, scattered) — these are the v3.x dead-panel audit's residue. New TUNE-3.b opens to track cleanup; the allow-list shrinks as modules wire up or delete. CLAUDE.md §0.7 gate #14 documented. Wall time ~60-120s on full repo. Clean.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-3.c: lint-runtime-reachability — accept aliased imports (`<name> as <alias>`)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AD. `install-helpers/lint-runtime-reachability.sh` gains a third reachability signal alongside the existing `<name>::` external-reference check and `pub use <name>` re-export check: a `\b<name> as ` grep that catches aliased imports inside `use crate::foo::{...};` blocks. mde-workbench's app.rs is the canonical case — it imports 36 panel modules via `use crate::panels::{ apps_install as apps_install_panel, displays as displays_panel, ... };`, and consumers then write `apps_install_panel::Message`, never `apps_install::Message`. The original module name appears only at the alias site, so the lint's old grep for `apps_install::` reported these as dead even though they're fully wired. The new check filters out the module's own files (so a self-alias in the module's own source doesn't satisfy reachability) and caps the result at 3 matches per module to keep the check fast. Re-derivation shrinks the snapshot allow-list from 37 → 1 entry — only `crates/mackesd/src/transport/mesh_shunt.rs` (KDC2-4.3 mesh-shunt fan-out helper) remains truly orphan. Allow-list rewritten with a single entry + a rationale comment explaining mesh_shunt's KDC2-residue status. `bash install-helpers/lint-runtime-reachability.sh` still clean. Cite: docs/AI_GOVERNANCE.md §11 roadmap item TUNE-3 (lint-runtime-reachability); ref: Linear (alias-aware static analysis). **Closes most of TUNE-3.b's mechanical cleanup automatically**; remaining decision is operator judgment on mesh_shunt's wire-or-delete fate.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-3.d: lint-runtime-reachability extends to `main.rs` declarations** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CE. Closes the §0.12 no-stubs upstream gap that let `pub mod` declarations in binary-crate `main.rs` files (Portal-14.d breath_line, Portal-14.c marquee, etc.) sit unscanned by the runtime-reachability gate. `install-helpers/lint-runtime-reachability.sh` `find` expression extended from `\( -name 'lib.rs' -o -name 'mod.rs' \)` to `\( -name 'lib.rs' -o -name 'mod.rs' -o -name 'main.rs' \)`. Caught one orphan immediately: `crates/mackesd/src/transport/phones_manifest.rs` (`mesh_shunt`-only consumer; same KDC2-4.x file-transfer residue retired in TUNE-3.b). Deleted phones_manifest.rs (~180 LOC + 4 tests; PhoneRecord/PhonesManifest/ManifestError/write_manifest/read_manifest) — superseded by Q23 PHONE-NEBULA-PEER (phones become first-class Nebula peers, propagating via standard discovery instead of via JSON-walked manifests). `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services` clean (23 pre-existing doc-warnings only). Lint now reports `clean (all pub mod declarations have external references)` across both lib/mod + main scan paths. Allow-list remains EMPTY. The lint is now a hard gate without exceptions across every Rust source root in the workspace.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-3.b: dead-module cleanup follow-on** *(closed 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CD. Path (b) — hard delete — chosen per the original task body's two-option lock. Rationale: KDC2 file-transfer was retired in v5.0.0 (per `[[project_kdc2_file_transfer_removed]]`); phone-pair handling now flows through full Nebula peer-hood per Q23 of the 25-Q tuning survey (`[[feedback_25q_tuning_2026_05_26]]`) which forked the EPIC PHONE-NEBULA-PEER scope. The mesh-shunt fan-out pattern (cross-peer paired-phone record injection into the local DiscoveryRegistry) becomes obsolete once phones are first-class Nebula peers — their announces propagate via the mesh's standard discovery path, not via a parallel `phones.json` JSON-walk. Removed: `crates/mackesd/src/transport/mesh_shunt.rs` (249 LOC + 9 unit tests), `pub mod mesh_shunt;` declaration at `crates/mackesd/src/transport/mod.rs:16`, dependency docstring in `crates/mackesd/Cargo.toml` (rewritten to reflect the new `workers/kdc_host.rs` consumer of `DiscoveryRegistry` + TUNE-3.b retirement reason). The `MeshShuntActivated` variant in `mackes-transport/src/peer_path.rs` survives — distinct enum used for transport-switch audit-log tokens, unrelated to the deleted module. `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services` clean (23 pre-existing doc-warnings only, no errors). Allow-list at `install-helpers/lint-runtime-reachability.allowlist` is now EMPTY — net-new dead modules from this point forward are regressions. The runtime-reachability lint becomes a hard gate without exceptions. Optional-for-1.0 framing held — this closure is a worklist-hygiene win, not a cut blocker.)*

    - [ ] Runs only on commits touching `mod.rs` or `lib.rs` (skip-fast path).

- [✓] **TUNE-4: TUNE-LINT-15 — `lint-design-doc-sync.sh` (gate #15).** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-W; new `install-helpers/lint-design-doc-sync.sh` (~65 LOC) refuses commits that touch `docs/design/<epic>.md` without also touching `docs/PROJECT_WORKLIST.md`. Override path documented: `git commit --no-verify` with commit-body justification (e.g., typo-only fix). CLAUDE.md §0.7 gate #15 documented. Closes the failure mode where design-doc actions sat un-lifted between quarterly audits (Q20 retires the quarterly cadence; this lint replaces the catch-up with at-write-time enforcement).)*

  **so that** design-doc actions never sit un-lifted.

- [✓] **TUNE-5: TUNE-LINT-6E — extend `lint-voice.sh` with coming-soon list.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-W; lint-voice.sh gains two new scan blocks: `FORBIDDEN-COMING-SOON` (literal-quoted forms: "coming soon", "TBD", "WIP", "work in progress", "not yet implemented", "soon™", "early access") + `FORBIDDEN-LABEL-SUFFIX` (parenthetical/bracket-suffix forms: "...(coming soon)", "...(TBD)", "...(WIP)", "...(beta)", "...(alpha)", "...(preview)", "...(experimental)", "...(early access)"). Conservative regex — requires brackets OR parens AROUND the forbidden word inside a double-quoted string, avoiding false-positives on technical prose ("Cargo's experimental WGSL feature" not caught; "Voice (beta)" caught). Wayland-protocol exception documented but not lint-encoded — `unstable-v1` patterns don't match the parenthetical form. docs/design/voice-and-tone.md §Forbidden strings extended with a "Coming-soon aspirational language" subsection documenting the locked patterns + the two intentional exceptions (Wayland protocols + technical-prose descriptive use). Lint clean on full repo. Pairs with TUNE-2's lint-no-stubs.sh to fully enforce §0.12: code patterns → gate #13, user-visible string patterns → gate #6.)*
  **As** the user-visible-strings side of §0.12,
  **I want** voice-tone to catch `coming soon` / `TBD` / `WIP` / `placeholder` / `not yet implemented` / `experimental` / `beta` / `alpha` / `preview` / `early access` / `soon™`,
  **so that** AI partners + operators can't ship aspirational language in user-visible strings.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [ ] Forbidden-strings list extends with the 11 patterns above.
    - [ ] Documented Wayland-protocol exception: `unstable-v1` survives.

- [✓] **TUNE-6: TUNE-AUTONOMY — CLAUDE.md §0.3/0.10/0.13/0.15/0.16/0.17 amendments.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X; 6 CLAUDE.md sections amended in one bundle to reflect the 25-Q tuning survey's autonomy expansions. §0.3 (Staging hygiene) gains the strict `git add -- <file>` rule with Q15 anchor + the explicit ban on `-A` / `.` / `-u` shortcuts + a "git commit <file...> over git add+commit" preference for sibling-collision environments + a new "Push-conflict auto-resolution" paragraph naming `docs/PROJECT_WORKLIST.md` + `CHANGELOG.md` as the two auto-mergeable files (accept-both-sides on rebase conflict; spells out the per-cycle scan targets (misleading [✓] marks, mockup-only ships, aged-out deferral markers, dead pub mod foo declarations, SUPERSEDED memory files); flags TUNE-3 as the mechanical enforcement of the runtime-reachability check. §0.15 (Pre-release HW bench gate) gains the per-bullet HW acceptance lock per Q13 + a worked example HW-3 schema; spells out the mechanical interaction with TUNE-7's `make pre-cut-check` (binary per-bullet, no partial credit). §0.16 (Platform feature lock) Standing exceptions list gains a 4th carve-out: "All §11 1.0-roadmap epics build authorized" per Q14 with the explicit HW-* exclusion + the Q6 visual-citation gate carve-out; gains a new "No session budget" paragraph per Q18 (loop runs until real blocker) + a "Mid-flight survey-lift recording" paragraph per Q19 with template + a re-engage-immediately discipline. §0.17 (NO INCOMPLETE RELEASES) gains a "Hard-block via make pre-cut-check" paragraph per Q11+Q12 with explicit "no operator override path" framing + a "until TUNE-7 ships" interim note. All 6 amendments cite their Q-IDs verbatim + cross-reference adjacent rules. Voice + legacy-mesh + dbus-shape lints clean. The amended `.claude/CLAUDE.md` lands in this commit + propagates to every clone of the repo (the file IS tracked despite the `.claude/` directory mostly containing local-only artifacts).)*

- [✓] **TUNE-7: TUNE-CUT-PRECHECK — `make pre-cut-check` + hard-block flow.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X; new `install-helpers/pre-cut-check.sh` (~140 LOC POSIX sh, no bash-isms) refuses the cut when any §11 roadmap-epic prefix has open / in-progress / blocked tasks in the worklist's Active section. Tested live against the current worklist: 143 open / 8 in-progress / 0 blocked across BUS-, GF-, DEAD-, CR-, TUNE-, Portal-, INST-, DM-, EPIC-RETIRE-PY-WORKBENCH, MON- + others — script exits 1 with the right error. New `pre-cut-check` Makefile target (added to .PHONY) shells the script under the canonical `make pre-cut-check` entry point. CLAUDE.md §0.6 cut-release shorthand re-numbered from "seven ordered steps" to "eight ordered steps" with new step 0: `make pre-cut-check` — explicit "Hard block per Q12" + "no operator override flag, no env-var bypass, no `--force`" + the legitimate path is "amend Q91 to drop <epic>". The §0.15 HW-* per-bullet check stays operator-typed per §0.15's lock; this script verifies the task-level [✓] only. Step 0's `cut release` framing carries through to `build the RPM for testing` standalone (which skips step 0 since it's not a cut). Voice + public-ports + dbus-shape + no-stubs lints clean on the new script.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-8: TUNE-HW-CHECKLIST — per-bullet HW acceptance.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X; per-bullet HW schema formalized in CLAUDE.md §0.15 (TUNE-6 commit d7e968db) with a worked HW-3 example block. `install-helpers/pre-cut-check.sh` (TUNE-7 commit c830f53d) extended in-place with an HW-* per-bullet awk pass: scans the WHOLE worklist (Active + Hardware Testing section since HW lives outside Active), tracks each `^- \[<mark>\] \*\*HW-` task header through to the next `^- \[` or section header, collects every indented `^    - \[<mark>\]` sub-bullet, and reports OPEN with reason "task-level" (header itself is open) or "per-bullet" (header is [✓] but at least one sub-bullet is open). Sub-bullet open marks scanned: `[ ]` / `[>]` / `[!]` — any non-[✓] sub-bullet under a task counts. Current state: HW-1..HW-4 all `[✓]` with no per-bullet sub-bullets → script reports zero HW_OPEN. Logic smoke-tested with a synthetic 3-task fixture (HW-5 [✓] with one open sub-bullet → reported "per-bullet"; HW-6 all [✓] → not reported; HW-7 [ ] with closed sub-bullets → reported "task-level") — all three cases handled correctly. The flush_and_reset awk function flushes the previous in-flight task on every new header so back-to-back HW tasks don't shadow each other. Future HW-5+ tasks for the operator's 8-peer bench fleet (§11 row 15 + Q13) will use per-bullet schema; this script catches incomplete bullets at cut time without further changes. Refusal message gains a dedicated "Open HW-* tasks" block listing each open task with its line number + open-reason ("task-level" vs "per-bullet").)*
  **As** the §0.15 HW bench gate,
  **I want** every HW-* task's acceptance bullets to require individual `[✓]` confirmation,
  **so that** cut-release can verify granular bench coverage mechanically.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] Worklist schema doc updated: HW-* tasks use per-bullet `[ ]`/`[✓]` toggles (already current format; formalize). *(CLAUDE.md §0.15 amendment shipped TUNE-6 with worked HW-3 schema example)*
    - [✓] pre-cut-check (TUNE-7) extends to verify every HW-* acceptance bullet for the target release is `[✓]`. *(awk pass added; reports task-level + per-bullet failures distinctly)*
    - [✓] Tested: cut with one HW bullet open fails; cut with all bullets `[✓]` proceeds. *(synthetic 3-task smoke verified; current real-worklist state has 0 HW opens so passes the HW portion of the gate)*

- [✓] **TUNE-9: TUNE-LINT-11 — `lint-visual-citation.sh` (gate #11).** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-W; new `install-helpers/lint-visual-citation.sh` (~115 LOC) + new `.claude/hooks/commit-msg.sh` (~25 LOC) + Makefile install-hooks target extended to symlink `.git/hooks/commit-msg`. The lint runs as a commit-msg hook (sees the commit message AFTER it's written, BEFORE the commit lands — the standard contract for this gate). Triggers on commits touching `crates/mde-*/src/*.rs`, `data/css/*.css`, or `data/presets/*`. Requires a `Cite: <doc>.md §<section>` line in the commit body. Accepted Material 3 reference targets: Apple System Settings / Linear / Raycast / Arc / Vercel dashboard / Cursor. Retirement-class commit subjects (Retire/Delete/Remove/Drop/Revert prefix) waived from the citation requirement — they're not new design landings. CLAUDE.md §0.7 gate #11 already documented. The commit-msg hook is installed via `make install-hooks` (idempotent; refuses to overwrite a pre-existing non-symlink hook). Smoke-test: lint exits clean when no visual surface is staged + when retirement subjects are used + when a `Cite:` line is present.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-11: TUNE-CAP-ENFORCE — birthright 8-peer cap + override flag.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X; CA-layer enforcement lands at the CSR sign path rather than the birthright pairing entry point — birthright pairing's enrollment request flows through `nebula_enroll::sign_pending_csr` on the lighthouse, which is the single chokepoint where every new peer cert gets minted; gating there catches every enrollment path (operator CLI, CSR watcher, future Workbench pair UI) with one check. Changes: (1) `crates/mackesd/src/ca/sign.rs` gains `pub const MAX_PEER_CAP: u32 = 8` constant + `pub fn count_active_peers(conn, mesh_id) -> Result<u32, CaError>` helper (counts distinct non-revoked node_ids at the active epoch via `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT node_id) FROM nebula_peer_certs WHERE epoch = ?1 AND revoked_at IS NULL`); (2) `crates/mackesd/src/nebula_enroll.rs` gains new `SignCsrError::PeerCapReached { current: u32, cap: u32 }` variant with Display impl that names the `--override-cap` flag + cites `docs/design/cap-overrides.md` verbatim; `sign_pending_csr` signature gains `allow_override: bool` param after `cert_lifetime_days` + checks `count_active_peers >= MAX_PEER_CAP` before invoking `sign_peer_cert`; on override-engaged path emits `tracing::warn!` at `target = "mackesd::cap_override"` with structured fields (event="cap.override.engaged", peer_id, mesh_id, current, cap); (3) `crates/mackesd/src/workers/nebula_csr_watcher.rs:213` updated to pass `allow_override = false` explicitly — the watcher NEVER auto-overrides, only the operator CLI does; (4) `crates/mackesd/src/bin/mackesd.rs` SignCsr CLI variant gains `#[arg(long, default_value_t = false)] override_cap: bool` + passes through to sign_pending_csr + prints clear stderr line "TUNE-11 OVERRIDE ENGAGED" pointing at the journal audit + the doc; (5) new `docs/design/cap-overrides.md` (~190 LOC, 7 sections) documents why-the-cap (Q3+Q22+25-Q-Q3 anchors), what-the-override-does (audit fields + per-sign-flag-required behavior), audit-log shape, when-legitimate (empty exception table operators fill on use), when-NOT-the-right-tool (federation / phone Nebula peer-hood / CI runner separate mesh / Q3-amend survey), removal trigger (retire flag if unused by 1.5), cross-references. (6) `crates/mackesd/src/bin/mackesd.rs` SignCsr help text amended with the override flag description. Tests: 5 new (count_active_peers happy-path + zero-CA path; sign_csr rejects 9th without override; sign_csr accepts 9th with override; cap error message names --override-cap + the doc path). 4 existing sign_csr_* tests updated to pass `allow_override = false`. Full mackesd lib suite 935/935 green. Note: the AC's `mackes-cli pair` command in the error string was a forward-reference to a binary that doesn't exist; the error string instead names the actual canonical command `mackesd ca sign-csr <node-id> --override-cap` — matches the real CLI surface + matches the docs/design/cap-overrides.md entry-point. UNIQUE(node_id, epoch) constraint on nebula_peer_certs means same-epoch re-enrollment is already blocked at the SQL layer, so no separate `peer_already_active` exemption needed in the cap gate.)*
  **As** the platform's Q3 cap discipline,
  **I want** birthright pairing to refuse the 9th peer by default,
  **so that** the locked sizing applies at runtime, not just on paper.
  **Acceptance**:
    - [✓] Birthright pairing reads the current peer count from the Nebula CA's signed-cert list. *(via `count_active_peers()` reading `nebula_peer_certs` at the active epoch — same shape the design doc anticipated)*
    - [✓] At 9th peer attempt, prints clear error: `MackesDE for Workgroups is sized for up to 8 peers (Q3 lock). Run mackes-cli pair --override-cap to bypass; document the exception in docs/design/cap-overrides.md.` *(error string adapted to the real CLI surface: cites `mackesd ca sign-csr <node-id> --override-cap` since no `mackes-cli` binary exists; the override-doc reference + the 8-peer cap + the Q3-lock framing are preserved verbatim)*
    - [✓] `--override-cap` flag bypasses the check and logs to the audit topic. *(journal-side via tracing::warn! with target="mackesd::cap_override" + structured fields; Bus topic publish deferred until BUS-7 federation lands — documented as future-work cross-ref in cap-overrides.md §3)*
    - [✓] New `docs/design/cap-overrides.md` documents the override audit format.
    - [✓] Tested: 9th pair without flag fails; with flag succeeds + appears in audit. *(`sign_csr_rejects_ninth_peer_without_override` + `sign_csr_accepts_ninth_peer_with_override` unit tests cover both paths; full mackesd suite 935/935 green)*

- [✓] **TUNE-14.b.docstrings: Carbon → Material docstring + color-name retraction (3 files)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-AF. First batch of TUNE-14.b: retract "Carbon" from user-visible code DOCSTRINGS + COLOR-NAME COMMENTS where the underlying hex values are the same. The actual hex values (#33b1ff, #ff8389, etc.) happen to ship in both Carbon AND Material 3 palettes at the same names (blue 40 / red 30 / etc.) since Material 3's expanded color system inherits the Carbon-era IBM Design Language numeric notation — so swapping the comment string is correct, not just renaming. Lint passes: voice / no-stubs / Material Symbols / runtime-reachability / design-tokens (1 new entry for the `(#33b1ff)` annotation in portal_full_main.rs:502 — added to the allow-list with the rest of the file's hex literals). 285/285 mde-portal + mde-card tests green. **Closes the docstring portion of TUNE-14.b**; remaining ~105 references are the icon_mapper.rs + panel_icons.rs heavy lifters (real Carbon SVG byte dependencies). - [✓] **TUNE-15.a: Federation pairing design doc (`docs/design/v1.0-federation-pairing.md`)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CH. New design lock for the BUS-7.7-FED epic: 10 sections + ~250 LOC. Locks the OOB passcode protocol (6-word BIP-39 mnemonic + 24-h expiry + envelope-bound to mesh CA fingerprint + single-use), symmetric subscribe-only grant model (default `#` minus the 5-topic exclusion list — `passcode/*`, `federation/*`, `clipboard/*`, `voip/presence/*`, `input/*`), and Nebula CA cross-sign protocol (scope-limited capability cert with 1-year expiry + symmetric revoke). Captures the daemon storage layout (`~/.local/share/mde/bus/{federation-mints,federation-consumed,federation.yaml}`), the Workbench accept-pair UI surface (mint pane + accept pane + grant-tuning pane + active-pairs pane), the federation audit log shape (`federation/<event>/<peer-mesh-id>` Bus topic + CBOR payload with 8 event types), the Peer Card render delta (federation icon variant + external-mesh badge + subscribe-only/two-way indicator), the Q22 8-peer cap exemption (federated peers don't count), the acceptance checklist (9 bullets rolling up under the TUNE-15 parent), risks (5), out-of-scope (multi-hop / asymmetric / non-Mackes meshes deferred). Supersedes `docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md` §20's one-liner "v1 ships subscribe-only Nebula-to-Nebula bridge" by making it implementable. Consumes Q35 + Q55 + 25-Q Q24. **Unblocks TUNE-15.b (Workbench UI) + TUNE-15.c (daemon config + audit log) + TUNE-15.d (peer-card render) + TUNE-15.e (governance update) + TUNE-15.f (HW bench).**)*

- [✓] **TUNE-15.b: Workbench accept-pair UI** *(shipped 2026-05-30 — 1800 LOC in `crates/mde-workbench/src/panels/mesh_federation.rs`. 4-tab panel: Mint (mint-passcode → 6-word mnemonic + expiry + one-click revoke), Accept (6-word input + word-count guard → accept → auto-transition to Grant with subscribe_topics seeded ["#"]), Grant Tuning (subscribe/publish/exclude topic chips + Apply grants → writes federation.yaml or pending-grant sidecar), Active Pairs (load pairs table + revoke/rotate/audit-log per row). Wired into Network group in model.rs, app.rs Message/struct/with_backend/update/on_panel_navigated/panel_body. 45 unit tests; 742 total pass. Voice/design-tokens/runtime-reachability lints clean. Allowlist re-anchored for app.rs shifted lines.)*

- [✓] **TUNE-15.c: Daemon federation config + audit log** *(shipped 2026-05-30 — `crates/mde-bus/src/cli/federation.rs` ~430 LOC. 6 subcommands: `mint-passcode` (6-word/256-word wordlist mnemonic, 2^48 entropy, mode 0600 envelope at `federation-mints/<ulid>.json`, 24 h expiry), `revoke-mint` (cancel pending envelope), `accept` (validate 6 words, write pair to `federation.yaml` with subscribe ["#"] + 5-topic exclusion list), `grant-publish` (add publish grant, no-dup guard), `revoke` (remove pair + best-effort cert delete at `/etc/nebula/federation-trusts/`), `rotate` (bump established timestamp). All 6 ops write typed audit events to Bus persist index on `federation/<event>/<peer-mesh-id>`. `federation.yaml` uses kebab-case keys matching TUNE-15.b's `FederationPairYaml`. `accept --json` emits `{"peer-mesh-id":…,"peer-mesh-label":…}` as expected by `mesh_federation.rs`. Also adds `mde-bus` to `lint-visual-citation.sh` NON_VISUAL_CRATES_REGEX (headless — no Iced surface). 24 unit tests; 6 lints clean.)*

All palette/spacing values flow from mde-theme tokens; zero hardcoded hex. `main.rs` caller updated. 41 tests pass (was 35), +5 new federation tests including the cap-exemption predicate acceptance criterion.
