Bench verify the 5 surviving KDC2 plugins (clipboard / SMS / battery / mpris / ping) continue to function alongside the Nebula overlay; no double-routing, no protocol conflict. Per the §5 lock.

- [✓] **TUNE-16.g: Voice-and-tone — "Mesh peer (phone)"** *(shipped 2026-05-30 — mde-peer-card + mde-workbench; mde-portal deferred until sibling session clears)*. `hero.rs` extended: `peer_kind: Option<PeerKind>` param; when Phone/Tablet renders "Mesh peer (phone)" / "Mesh peer (tablet)" subtitle below hostname via new `peer_kind_label()` helper. `home.rs` capability row: `"Phone Pairing"` → `"Mesh peer (phone)"`; description updated to "Add a phone to your mesh…". Voice lint clean. 3 new hero tests (phone/tablet/non-handheld label assertions). mde-portal sweep to follow when Portal-* siblings clear.

- [ ] **TUNE-16.h: HW bench — phone joins mesh + Bus round-trip** *(opens 2026-05-27 from TUNE-16 split per §0.12, operator-typed per §0.15 + 25-Q Q14)*. Operator-typed acceptance: pair operator's phone via TUNE-16.c flow; phone shows up in Peer Card list; `mde-bus publish test/hello` from phone lands on every desktop within 5 s; `mde-bus publish fdo/test-app/hello` from a desktop surfaces as a phone notification within 5 s.

---

### v6.x BUS-1..7 — Mackes Bus (locked 2026-05-25 via 104-Q poll across 26 rounds)

> **v6.x = Mackes Bus** — single mesh-wide notification + clipboard
> bus on self-hosted ntfy over Nebula, with persistence on GlusterFS
> mesh-home. Hard cut: replaces v5.1 GF-17 mesh notification bus +
> v2.6 MON alert routing (parallel-write window for external
> consumers) + standalone FDO notifications. Folds into v6.0 Portal
> epic.
>
> **30 candidate features brainstormed.** 24 survived the poll; **7
> cut** with rationale in the design doc: #11 cron (systemd timers),
> #13 tier-2 ACL passcode (flat-trust only), #16 Piper TTS, #18
> geofence, #23 calendar digest, #25 SMS escalation (KDC2 phone push
> is enough), #26 E2E body encryption (Nebula transport is enough).
>
> **Design lock:** `docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md`.
> **Extended memory:** `~/.claude/projects/-home-mm-Desktop-files-mackes-shell/memory/project_v6_x_mackes_bus.md`.
>
> **Architecture highlights:**
> - Per-peer ntfy broker, gossip-synced, with shared SQLite-on-GFS
>   queryable index + per-topic file-tree authoritative store at
>   `~/.local/share/mde/bus/<topic-path>/<ulid>.json`
> - mDNS-on-Nebula discovery (`_mackes-bus._tcp`), <30 s failover
> - Slash-hierarchy topics, MQTT wildcards (`+` / `#`), self-serve
>   creation
> - Tera templating with mesh vars + `{{exec 'cmd'}}` shell exec
>   (flat-trust amplifier — documented in design doc §10)
> - Full CommonMark + media via GFS reference
> - 4 priority levels → surfaces: `min` silent log / `default` tray +
>   badge / `high` status-zone strip + sound persistent until ack /
>   `urgent` Theater takeover + wallpaper stripe + phone push
> - Notifications + clipboard adds land as Dock Breadcrumb
>   left-of-buttons segments (existing §2.9–2.10 of v6 Portal spec),
>   tinted by type (priority accent vs neutral grey)
> - Global clipboard sync via custom `mde-clipd`
>   (wlr-data-control-unstable-v1) — all-peer auto-sync, all MIME
>   types, Super+Shift+C to skip, Super+V centered popover with
>   type-to-filter + j/k nav
> - Phone reach via **dual path** (KDC2 + ntfy mobile app,
>   deduplicated by ULID)
>
> **Sub-epics run in parallel** (R24 lock — no enforced order):
> - **BUS-1** Foundation — crate + broker + persistence + CLI + templating
> - **BUS-2** Surfaces — Breadcrumb / tray / strip / Theater / wallpaper / DND
> - **BUS-3** Webhooks — ntfy publisher + YAML rules + 6 built-in adapters
> - **BUS-4** Migration — GF-17 hard cut + MON parallel-write + FDO bridge
> - **BUS-5** Clipboard — mde-clipd + Super+V + KDC2 round-trip + tag pinning
> - **BUS-6** Advanced routing — rooms + first-to-ack + correlation + DM + broadcast snooze
> - **BUS-7** Federation + audit + Workbench Mesh > Bus subpage
>
> **Each commit ships END-TO-END** per [[feedback_no_stubs]] + §0.12.
> **Build authorization NOT yet granted** — design is locked; awaiting
> user "execute" / "iterate" / "ship the Bus" per §0.5 before
> implementation begins. Bound by
> [[feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty]] — drain BUS-* before any cut.

#### BUS-1: Foundation (mde-bus crate + broker + persistence + CLI)

- [✓] **BUS-1.1: Scaffold `crates/mde-bus/` Rust crate.** Cargo.toml + lib.rs structure + supervised by mackesd's `bus_supervisor` worker (file-list authoritative; design doc said "mded" but the supervisor pattern lives in mackesd::workers::*). Scope: workspace member + `mde-bus` binary entry. Files: `crates/mde-bus/Cargo.toml`, `crates/mde-bus/src/lib.rs`, `crates/mde-bus/src/main.rs`, workspace `Cargo.toml`, `crates/mackesd/src/workers/bus_supervisor.rs`. Exit met: `cargo build -p mde-bus` succeeds; `cargo build -p mackesd --features async-services` includes the `BusSupervisor` worker spawn in `mackesd serve`. Tests met: `cargo test -p mde-bus` (24 passing) + bench smoke (`mde-bus --version`, `mde-bus topic list`, `mde-bus render`).
- [✓] **BUS-1.2: Per-peer ntfy broker, gossip-synced over Nebula.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-D; new `crates/mde-bus/src/broker.rs` (~390 LOC + 10 unit tests, all green) with pure helpers (`render_config` / `materialize_config` / `evaluate_prereqs` / `spawn_ntfy`) + async `start_if_ready` supervising an upstream `ntfy` subprocess bound to the Nebula overlay IP; reads overlay-IP from the same publish file NF-21.1's sshd_overlay_bind worker uses; new `data/ntfy/server.yml.tmpl` Tera template (rendered to `<cache>/mde/bus/ntfy/server.yml` at runtime) installs to `/usr/share/mde/ntfy/server.yml.tmpl` via spec catch-all; plain HTTP per design doc line 210 lock ("Encryption: None — Nebula transport is enough"); graceful degradation with 4 distinct `BrokerSkipReason` variants for pre-enrollment / missing-binary / missing-template / empty-publish-file paths; daemon `run_daemon` wires the broker startup + waits on child exit so outer `mackesd::bus_supervisor` respawns with fresh prereq checks when ntfy crashes; cargo check (lib + bin) clean, 10/10 broker tests green, `topic list` smoke + heartbeat unchanged, make rpm green after spec-hygiene fix for DEAD-2.11 dangling %files entries (mesh.mount + mackes-mesh.thumbnailer retired in same commit). Integration test with 2 peers in containers is HW carve-out per design.)*
- [✓] **BUS-1.3: mDNS Nebula-only service registration (`_mackes-bus._tcp`).** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-F; new `crates/mde-bus/src/discovery.rs` (~370 LOC + 5 unit tests, all green) with `DiscoveryConfig` / `BusPeer` / `PeerRegistry` / `DiscoveryHandle` types + pure helpers (`build_service_info`, `peer_from_service_info`); registers `_mackes-bus._tcp.local.` via `mdns_sd::ServiceDaemon` carrying the overlay-IP + TXT records `host=<hostname>` + `proto=mackes-bus/1`; browses the same service type into a `PeerRegistry`; self-announcement filtered; `DiscoveryHandle::shutdown` unregisters cleanly so peers see drops in real time; daemon `run_daemon` wires registration AFTER the broker confirms running + unregisters on shutdown; missing overlay-IP / failed mdns-sd init / unparseable IP all log a single skip line and continue; new `hostname_for_discovery()` resolves instance name via `$MDE_BUS_INSTANCE` → `$HOSTNAME` → `/proc/sys/kernel/hostname` → `"mde-bus"` fallback (no libc dep); cargo check (lib + bin) clean, 5/5 discovery tests green, legacy-mesh + dbus-shape lints clean. Integration test with 3 peers in CI is HW carve-out per the task body.)*
- [✓] **BUS-1.4: Persistence layer — SQLite index + per-topic file tree.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-M; new `crates/mde-bus/src/persist.rs` (~480 LOC + 11 unit tests, all green) `Persist` struct manages the per-topic JSON file tree + SQLite index. `write(topic, priority, title, body)` atomic-renames a ULID-named JSON file under `<bus_root>/<topic>/<ulid>.json` THEN INSERTs an index row — file is authoritative, index is the queryable view. `list_since(topic, since_ulid)` is an index-range scan via the `(topic, ulid)` SQLite index (10k-replay test green in 2.25s). `count()` for tests + BUS-1.8's `history --count` verb. `detect_divergence()` is the audit safety net: walks the file tree, queries the index, returns `(files_without_rows, rows_without_files)` so retention + audit passes can reconcile. Topic validation rejects empty / leading-slash / trailing-slash / `..` / `//` / wildcard chars. Decision: SQLite is **per-peer** (`<bus_root>/index.sqlite`, NOT on GFS) because SQLite + networked-FS is a known footgun (lock-stealing + WAL-replay edge cases); the file tree IS on GFS so cross-peer replay still works. Cross-peer index aggregation is BUS-7 federation territory. New `crates/mde-bus/migrations/0001_init.sql` embedded via `include_str!` (no migration framework — one schema). Schema: `messages(ulid PK, topic, priority, title, body, ts_unix_ms, file_path UNIQUE)` + two indexes (topic+ulid for `list_since`, ts_unix_ms for BUS-1.9 retention). New deps: `rusqlite 0.32` (workspace version — inherits the bundled link from mackesd's existing pull, no conflict), `ulid 1` (with serde feature). Wired into `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/server.rs` `handle_hook` so every successful webhook match → publish ALSO writes to the per-topic tree + index BEFORE the outbound POST — broker failure doesn't lose a matched message. Listener config gains `bus_root: Option<PathBuf>` (default `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/bus`) so tests can opt out of persistence. cargo check (lib + bin) clean; 120/120 mde-bus lib tests green; 10k-message replay completes in 2.25s; lint-legacy-mesh + lint-dbus-shape + lint-public-ports + lint-voice all clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-1.5: Topic registry — slash hierarchy + MQTT wildcards + self-serve creation.** `+` single-level, `#` multi-level. Scope: parser + matcher + creation. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/topic.rs`, `crates/mde-bus/src/wildcard.rs`. Exit met: `Registry::ensure(name)` auto-creates on first publish (Round 3 self-serve lock); `Registry::match_pattern(glob)` returns matching topics. Tests met: 11 unit tests including the exhaustive MQTT wildcard `match_table` covering exact-match / `+` / `#` / combinations / empty-input edge cases. CLI publish/tail verbs land in BUS-1.8.
- [✓] **BUS-1.6: Seed the 12 curated default topics on first run.** `fleet/announce`, `fleet/sec`, `peer/$hostname/{alerts,system}`, `mon/{cpu,memory,disk,network}`, `mesh/{peers,leader}`, `fdo/system`, `clipboard/sync`. Scope: first-run hook + idempotent seed. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/seed.rs`. Exit met: `mde-bus topic list` shows all 12 with hostname substituted (verified: `peer/fedora/alerts`, `peer/fedora/system`); `seed_defaults_with_hostname` returns `Ok(0)` on second call. Tests met: 4 unit tests including idempotency, hostname expansion, class coverage, and `clipboard/sync = Min priority` (Round 11 silent-by-default lock). The auto-`fdo/$app` topics from FDO bridge land in BUS-4.4; v1 seeds only `fdo/system`.
- [✓] **BUS-1.7: Subscription manifest at `~/.local/share/mde/bus/subs.yaml` + inotify watcher.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-G; new `crates/mde-bus/src/subs.rs` (~430 LOC + 15 unit tests, all green) with `SubsManifest` schema (topics + mute + quiet_hours), `parse_yaml` / `to_yaml` round-trip via serde_yaml 0.9, three pure predicates (`is_subscribed` / `is_muted` / `should_deliver`) using MQTT-style wildcard matching from `crate::wildcard`, quiet-hours window with non-wrapping (09:00..17:00) + wrapping (22:00..07:00) semantics + malformed-time fail-open; `load_or_seed` atomically seeds the per-peer file from the template via temp+rename; `SubsWatcher` polls mtime every 100ms (well inside the 200ms exit ceiling), re-parses on mtime advance, broadcasts via `tokio::sync::watch` channel; daemon `run_daemon` spawns the watcher with a held shutdown_tx so dropping it on function exit triggers clean watcher shutdown; new `data/bus/subs.yaml.tmpl` ships under `/usr/share/mde/bus/subs.yaml.tmpl` via new spec install line; mtime-polling chosen over notify/inotify because the manifest is operator-edited (rare deliberate writes) — simpler implementation wins; cargo check (lib + bin) clean, 15/15 subs tests green, legacy-mesh + dbus-shape lints clean. The "live delivery" wiring lands when BUS-1.8 (CLI tail filter) + BUS-4 (FDO bridge) consume the watch::Receiver; this commit ships the data plane.)*
- [✓] **BUS-1.8: `mde-bus` CLI binary — publish / tail / sub / mute / history / topic verbs.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-N; new `crates/mde-bus/src/cli/` submodule — `mod.rs` (top-level `Cli` + `Cmd` enum, ~70 LOC) + `publish.rs` (~250 LOC + 7 tests — three publish forms: positional body, `--body-flag`, piped stdin) + `tail.rs` (~210 LOC + 5 tests — follow-mode polls the SQLite index every 250ms, count-mode prints last N + exits, wildcards via `crate::wildcard::matches`) + `sub.rs` (~220 LOC + 5 tests — add/remove/list against `subs.yaml` with atomic temp+rename) + `mute.rs` (~140 LOC + 3 tests — same shape as sub) + `history.rs` (~95 LOC + 2 tests — list_since with optional count limit) + `topic.rs` (~75 LOC + 3 tests — list + match against seeded registry). `main.rs` reduced from ~330 LOC to ~280 LOC (dispatch only; per-verb logic moved). `Persist::list_topics()` added to support wildcard expansion. The publish verb writes through `Persist` FIRST (durable record), then attempts the ntfy POST — broker failure leaves the message safely indexed with a ULID the operator sees in the verb's stdout for retry. Smoke-tested: `mde-bus --help` lists all 8 verbs, `mde-bus publish --help` shows all flags. 145/145 mde-bus lib tests green (120 pre-existing + 25 new CLI tests including the publish→persist→read round-trip in `cli::publish::tests::publish_no_broker_writes_to_persist`); lint-* all clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-1.9: Retention engine — 7d default + per-priority overrides + GFS quota.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — commit `b14ceb0b` BUS-1.9 retention engine + GFS quota enforcement; worklist mark flipped 2026-05-26 by session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X after taking sole ownership per operator directive. `crates/mde-bus/src/retention.rs` (526 LOC, 10 unit tests) ships the per-priority TTL machinery (urgent=forever, high=30d, default=7d, min=24h), the soft/hard GFS quota (500 MB / 2 GB), and the `bus/sys/quota` warning publish path via `publish_quota_warning`. Pure helpers `policy_ttl` / `ttl_cutoff_unix_ms` / `find_expired_in_index` / `disk_usage_bytes` are exposed for deterministic stub-clock tests. The GC loop runs as `run_loop` on a 1-hour cadence wired into `crates/mde-bus/src/main.rs::run_daemon` next to the broker supervisor. Every test passes including the time-travel sequence (`min_messages_expire_after_24h`, `default_messages_expire_after_7d`, `high_messages_expire_after_30d`, `urgent_messages_never_expire`, `pass_removes_both_file_and_row`, `quota_soft_breach_surfaces_in_report`). 171/171 mde-bus tests green. Closes BUS-1.x — broker, mDNS, persistence, registry, seeds, manifest, CLI, retention, templates ALL [✓]; BUS-1 sub-epic complete.)*
- [✓] **BUS-1.10: Tera templating engine with `{{exec}}`, `{{include_file}}`, and curated mesh vars.** Renders at publish time. Vars: `peer.{hostname,overlay_ip,uptime_s}`, `mesh.size`, `time.{iso8601,unix}`, `system.{load_1,mem_used_mb}`. Custom Tera function renamed from `include` → `include_file` to avoid collision with Tera's reserved `include` template tag. Path escapes (`..`, leading `/`) rejected; reads scoped to configured include-root. `exec()` truncates at 4 KB and times out at 5 s. Scope: engine integration + variable resolvers + sandbox warnings. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/template.rs`. Exit met: `mde-bus render 'host={{peer.hostname}} time={{time.iso8601}} size={{mesh.size}}'` returns `host=fedora time=2026-05-26T... size=1`. `{{exec(cmd="uptime -p")}}` and `{{include_file(path="hello.txt")}}` render correctly in tests. Tests met: 9 unit tests with tempdir-fixture `/proc` so the suite runs hermetically.

#### BUS-2: Surfaces (Breadcrumb / tray / strip / Theater / wallpaper / mde:// / DND)

- [✓] **BUS-2.1: Priority → surface mapping module.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-P; new `crates/mde-bus/src/surface.rs` (~240 LOC + 7 unit tests, all green) — `Surfaces` trait declares four methods (`log_silent` / `tray_and_badge` / `status_strip_and_sound` / `theater_wallpaper_phone`) keyed to the four priority classes per design-doc §6. `dispatch(msg, surfaces)` is the pure router — match on `Priority` (after a permissive `parse_priority` that falls back to `Default` on unknown strings), fire the corresponding surface method. `LogOnlySurfaces` ships as the daemon's default impl until BUS-2.2..2.8 land the real Iced surfaces — each dispatch emits a `tracing::info!` line with the surface name + ULID + topic, making the table observable in `journalctl -u mde-bus`. `RecordingSurfaces` (thread-safe via `Arc<Mutex<…>>`) is the test stub — snapshot-asserts the call sequence in `dispatch_table_snapshot_in_call_order` cover all four priorities in declaration order. Wired into `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/server.rs` `handle_hook` so every successful webhook match → persist write → fires `dispatch_surface(&stored, &LogOnlySurfaces)` for runtime reachability per §0.12. Implementations of `Surfaces` in `crates/mde-portal/` + `crates/mde-popover/` + `crates/mde-kdc-proto/` land in BUS-2.2..2.8 + BUS-6.8 — this module stays GUI-free. 162/162 mde-bus lib tests green; lints clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-2.2: Dock Breadcrumb wire-up** *(closed 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. All four sub-tasks shipped: BUS-2.2.a (fleet/announce + priority palette), BUS-2.2.b (clipboard/sync + neutral grey), BUS-2.2.b.peer (peer/<hostname>/{alerts,system}), BUS-2.2.typewriter (char-by-char reveal via Portal-14.a). End-to-end bench-observable across all four topics + typewriter polish. Tick audio (R4-Q28) defers to Portal-14.b once Portal-38 sound design lands.)*
- [✓] **BUS-2.2.a: Dock Breadcrumb wire-up — fleet/announce single-topic + priority-accent tint** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. mde-portal gains a `bus_announce_subscription` (same poll-based pattern as Portal-57.b's pulse subscriber) that watches `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/bus/fleet/announce/` for new ULID-named JSON files every 500 ms. `parse_announce_file` reads the BUS-1.4 StoredMessage envelope's outer fields directly — `priority` + `title` + `body` are top-level (vs Portal-57.b's pulse payload which nests JSON inside `body`). `min` priority filtered at the parser per the design lock (silent — no segment emitted). `DockApp::bus_announce_segments: Vec<BusAnnounceSegment>` carries the active set with 6 s TTL (`BUS_ANNOUNCE_TTL_SECS`); buffer caps at 8 with FIFO eviction. `build_bus_announce_segments` renders one priority-tinted pill per alive segment in a horizontal row, placed between `layout_prompt_seg` and `host_seg`. Tints: `urgent` = Carbon red 30 (0.91/0.30/0.36), `high` = Carbon orange 30 (0.93/0.55/0.21), `default` = Carbon blue 40 (0.20/0.69/1.0), unknown → indigo fallback. Label format `<title>: <body>` with optional-field graceful degradation + 60-char truncation. 11 new tests: TTL gate, priority→tint lookup (4 classes + fallback), label-formatting (5 input combinations + truncation), Message append + sweep, 8-buffer cap, envelope parse, min-filter, default-priority fallback, malformed-JSON None. Plain-text rendering for now; typewriter entry ships as BUS-2.2.typewriter when Portal-14 typewriter primitive lands. `cargo test -p mde-portal bus_` 11/11 green. All applicable lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §2 (breadcrumb segments); ref: Linear (priority pill stack).)*
- [✓] **BUS-2.2.b: Dock Breadcrumb multi-topic subscriber (clipboard/sync; peer/{alerts,system} follow-up)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. `BusAnnounceSegment` gains a `topic: BusSegmentTopic` field discriminating Announce vs Clipboard. New `bus_clipboard_subscription` polls `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/bus/clipboard/sync/` with the same 500-ms cadence as `bus_announce_subscription`; emits `Message::BusAnnounceSegment` tagged with `BusSegmentTopic::Clipboard`. New generic helper `parse_breadcrumb_file(path, ulid, topic)` extracted from `parse_announce_file` so both subscriptions share parser logic. `bus_segment_color(topic, priority)` overrides priority lookup for Clipboard → neutral Carbon-grey-50 (0.55/0.55/0.55) regardless of priority per the design lock; Announce delegates to `bus_priority_color`. Renderer (`build_bus_announce_segments`) swaps `bus_priority_color` for `bus_segment_color` so Clipboard pills render grey even when priority is `urgent`. 4 new tests: clipboard-always-grey across 4 priorities, announce-delegates-to-priority, parse_breadcrumb_file Clipboard tag, clipboard-segment append. peer/{alerts,system} per-host subscriber deferred — needs hostname resolution + dynamic topic-path generation; opens as BUS-2.2.b.peer. `install-helpers/lint-design-tokens.allowlist` re-anchored for the +1 new clipboard-grey Color::from_rgb + struct-field-additions shifting pre-existing lines. `cargo test -p mde-portal bus_ + clipboard_segment + parse_breadcrumb_file` 13/13 green (all of session's bus tests now pass). All applicable lints clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-2.2.b.peer: Dock Breadcrumb peer-alerts + peer-system topic subscribers** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. New `bus_peer_topics_subscription` polls BOTH `<XDG_DATA_HOME>/mde/bus/peer/<hostname>/alerts/` AND `peer/<hostname>/system/` in one loop (single tokio task, shared seen-ULID set) — saves a task vs two separate subscriptions. Both topics tag emissions with `BusSegmentTopic::Announce` so the priority palette wins; clipboard-grey is reserved for the literal `clipboard/sync` topic. 4 new tests: `local_hostname_never_empty`, `peer_alerts_topic_dir_composes_path`, `peer_system_topic_dir_composes_path`, `peer_topic_dirs_handle_dashes_and_dots`. `cargo test -p mde-portal local_hostname/peer_alerts/peer_system/peer_topic_dirs` 4/4 green. All applicable lints clean. **BUS-2.2 stack now fully shipped end-to-end: fleet/announce + clipboard/sync + peer/<hostname>/{alerts,system}** all surface as priority/topic-tinted breadcrumb pills in the Dock.)*
- [✓] **BUS-2.2.typewriter: Dock Breadcrumb typewriter entry** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-Z. Portal-14.a's typewriter primitive landed in the same session + was wired into `build_bus_announce_segments` (which renders both fleet/announce + clipboard/sync + peer/{alerts,system} pills) as part of Portal-14.a's initial consumer integration. Pill labels reveal char-by-char from segment.spawned_at at 60 chars/sec via `typewriter_visible_text`. Tick audio (R4-Q28) ships separately as Portal-14.b once Portal-38 sound design lands.)*
- [✓] **BUS-2.3: Tray popover — priority bucket → chronological grouping.** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — BUS Messages section grafted below FDO groups in `mde-popover notifications` popover. Pure `serde_json::Value` parse of the GFS file tree; no mde-bus dep. Three priority buckets: urgent (red) / high (orange) / default (blue), newest-first within each (ULID descending). AckBusMessage(ulid) moves to acked-list; min-priority + audit/ topic files filtered. 7 new tests: parse, filter-min, malformed, 3-bucket grouping, ack exclusion, newest-first sort, nested-dir + audit-skip. 156/156 tests green. All lints clean.)* *(NEXT FOCUSED BUILD — session=opus-2026-05-28-ship-portRP recon: home resolved by the BUS-2.4 precedent — use the EXISTING `mde-popover notifications` popover (the notification center), not a new `tray/bus_popover.rs`. Scope: that 715-LOC popover currently reads the FDO cache `~/.cache/mackes/notifications.json` peer-grouped (via `mde_applet_notifications`); BUS-2.3 adds a Bus-message source (GFS-poll the bus store, mirror `parse_breadcrumb_file`) rendered as a priority-bucketed section (urgent / high / default, newest-first within bucket) + ack-moves-to-acked-list. A substantial but buildable graft — deferred from this loop only because it's a focused 715-LOC-file build, not a turn-tail addition.)* Tray dropdown groups urgent / high / default; within bucket, newest first. Exit: 3 messages at 3 priorities show in 3 buckets; ack moves to acked-list. Test: Iced widget test.

- [✓] **BUS-2.8.watcher: mtime-poll watcher for dnd.yaml + tokio::watch broadcast** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BF. **Closes BUS-2.8 in full.** `crates/mde-bus/src/dnd.rs` gains a `DndWatcher` struct + `DndTickOutcome` enum mirroring the BUS-1.7 SubsWatcher pattern: 1-second `DEFAULT_WATCH_TICK`; pinned to `<bus_root>/dnd.yaml`; on construction loads current state (default DND off when missing/corrupted); each `tick_once()` polls mtime, re-reads on advance, broadcasts via `tokio::sync::watch::Sender<DndState>`. Outcomes: FileMissing / Idle / Reloaded / Unchanged / ReadOrParseFailed (cached state preserved). `subscribe()` clones the Receiver for future BUS-2.x surfaces; `current()` for cheap reads. `run(shutdown)` interleaves ticks with the shutdown signal. Wired into `run_daemon` alongside the SubsWatcher startup; skips cleanly when no XDG data dir. 5 new tests cover starts-with-default-when-file-missing, starts-with-existing-file-state, tick-file-missing, tick-reloads-on-mtime-advance, subscribe-emits-clone. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 200 tests green. **BUS-2.8 epic complete** — .data + .cli + .topic-hours + .watcher all shipped. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (DND watch-channel cadence); ref: Linear (live-updated DND state).)*

- [✓] **BUS-2.8.topic-hours: Hook config schema gains per-topic `quiet_after` + `quiet_until`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BE. Third of the BUS-2.8 sub-tasks (BUS-2.8.watcher remains as final follow-on). `PublishSpec` gains `quiet_after`/`quiet_until` HH:MM string fields; `PublishSpec::quiet_hours()` resolves to a typed `crate::dnd::TopicQuietHours` (one-sided or unparseable → no-window default; fail-open). `RenderedPublish` carries `quiet_hours` through `render_rule`; `handle_hook` swaps the prior hardcoded default for `rendered.quiet_hours`. 4 new tests: default-when-unset, parses-hhmm-pair (22:00+07:00), one-sided-falls-through, unparseable-falls-through. 2 stale RenderedPublish test fixtures + 1 PublishSpec fixture updated to keep literal-syntax safe. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 195 tests green. **BUS-2.8 nearly complete** — only inotify watcher remains. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (DND cadence + per-topic quiet hours); ref: Linear (per-channel notification scheduling).)*

- [✓] **BUS-2.8.cli: `mde-bus dnd on/off/status` operator verbs** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BD. Second half of BUS-2.8 (BUS-2.8.watcher remains as final follow-on for inotify push-vs-pull). New `crates/mde-bus/src/cli/dnd.rs` (~180 LOC + 5 unit tests, all green) ships the `mde-bus dnd <op>` operator-facing verbs: `on` writes `<bus_root>/dnd.yaml` with active=true + since-now-timestamp + local-hostname; `off` writes the same envelope with active=false; `status` reads the live state + prints in human-readable form (`DND: on/off\nSince: <timestamp>\nBy: <peer>`). All three accept `--bus-root <path>` override for tests + integration smokes. Default bus_root resolves via `crate::default_data_dir()` (the standard XDG path the rest of mde-bus uses); `--bus-root` override is required when neither $HOME nor $XDG_DATA_HOME is set. `local_hostname()` helper resolves via `$HOSTNAME` → `/proc/sys/kernel/hostname` → `"unknown-host"` fallback (mirrors the chain used by `crate::publisher_id`). `format_status` handles the "never toggled" path (default state with since_unix_ms=0 + empty set_by_peer) by appending a clarifying line so operators don't see a misleading "Since: 1970" timestamp. Wired through `cli::mod.rs` `Cmd::Dnd` + `main.rs::main` dispatch. 5 new unit tests cover: set_state(on) round-trip (writes active=true + non-zero timestamp + non-empty peer + load-back round-trips), set_state(off) writes false after a prior on (validates the "off path doesn't no-op when prior=on" failure mode), format_status for default state ("never toggled" line present), format_status for active state (DND: on + Since + By: <peer> all present), local_hostname never empty. **Bench-verified end-to-end on this commit**: `mde-bus dnd status --bus-root /tmp/foo` → `DND: off\n(never toggled; using default off)`; `mde-bus dnd on --bus-root /tmp/foo` → `DND: on\nSince: 2026-05-27 12:11:05 -04:00\nBy: fedora`; `mde-bus dnd off` → flips back. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib dnd` 20/20 tests green (15 lib + 5 CLI). All applicable lints clean. **Remaining BUS-2.8 sub-tasks**: BUS-2.8.watcher (inotify on dnd.yaml so the hook handler doesn't re-read on every fire — small perf win + cross-peer propagation via GFS heal), BUS-2.8.topic-hours (extend hook config schema with per-topic quiet_after/quiet_until + thread through to dispatch_with_dnd's `topic_hours` arg). Cite: motion-language.md §6 (DND cadence); ref: Linear (DND-respecting CLI tool).)*

- [✓] **BUS-2.8.data: DND state machine + decision logic + GFS persistence** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BC. First half of BUS-2.8 (full mesh-sync integration ships as `.watcher`). `crates/mde-bus/src/dnd.rs` (~330 LOC + 15 unit tests, all green) ships the data model + decision logic + persistence: `DndState { active, since_unix_ms, set_by_peer }` (the mesh-synced state envelope, with safe default = DND off), `TopicQuietHours { quiet_after: Option<u32>, quiet_until: Option<u32> }` (seconds-of-day window per topic), pure helpers `is_quiet_hour(now_local_seconds, hours) -> bool` (handles same-day + overnight wrap-midnight + zero-length + one-sided-None edge cases) + `is_suppressed(state, hours, tags, now) -> bool` (override=dnd tag bypass first, then DND toggle, then quiet-hour window) + `parse_hhmm(s) -> Option<u32>` (24-hour HH:MM string → seconds-of-day) + filesystem helpers `load_default(bus_root) -> DndState` (missing file → default; corrupted YAML → default; safe-fail) + `save_default(bus_root, &state) -> Result<()>` (atomic temp+rename write). New `crates/mde-bus/src/surface.rs::dispatch_with_dnd` wrapper consumes the state + suppresses by log-silent when DND/quiet-hour gates fire; falls through to standard `dispatch()` otherwise. `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/server.rs` `handle_hook` swaps `dispatch_surface` for `dispatch_with_dnd` — loads DND state from `<bus_root>/dnd.yaml` on each hook fire (cheap; < 1 KB file). New `current_local_seconds_of_day()` helper uses chrono::Local. `ListenerState` gains `bus_root: Option<PathBuf>` carried from config; tests without persistence get None → falls back to DndState::default(). 15 new unit tests cover default state, YAML round-trip, same-day quiet window, overnight wrap window, zero-length window, one-sided None window, override=dnd bypass for both DND + quiet-hour paths, DND-off-outside-quiet-hours-delivered, HH:MM parse + reject, file-missing returns default, save+load round-trip, corrupted-YAML returns default. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 186 tests green (171 pre-existing + 15 new). All applicable pre-commit lints clean (runtime-reachability clean — `dnd::` referenced from surface.rs + hooks/server.rs). **End-to-end bench-observable**: `mde-bus dnd on` (CLI verb ships separately as BUS-2.8.cli) writes dnd.yaml with active=true → next hook publish → dispatch_with_dnd routes to log_silent surface (DND on). **Remaining sub-tasks**: BUS-2.8.watcher (inotify watcher for the dnd.yaml file so toggle is propagated within the GFS heal window WITHOUT every hook re-loading), BUS-2.8.topic-hours (extend hook config schema with per-topic quiet_after/quiet_until fields + thread through to dispatch_with_dnd), BUS-2.8.cli (`mde-bus dnd on/off/status` verbs). All three ship as follow-ons. Cite: docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md §6 (DND routing surface); ref: Linear (DND-respecting notification).)*

- [✓] **BUS-2.8: Single DND toggle + per-topic global quiet hours + `override=dnd` bypass + fleet-wide DND sync.** *(closed 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BX. Epic-level closeout: all four sub-tasks are individually [✓] (`.data` + `.cli` + `.topic-hours` + `.watcher`), plus two extension verbs (`.cli.status.json` + `.cli.toggle`). The full DND state machine ships: `DndState { active, since_unix_ms, set_by_peer }` mesh-synced via GFS at `<bus_root>/dnd.yaml`; per-topic `quiet_after`/`quiet_until` HH:MM windows in hook config flow through to dispatch; `override=dnd` tag bypasses every gate; CLI verbs `mde-bus dnd on/off/toggle/status` (with `--json` on status); `DndWatcher` (mtime-poll, 1s tick) broadcasts state changes via `tokio::sync::watch::Sender<DndState>` so all future BUS-2.x surfaces subscribe to live state instead of polling. The original "mesh DND propagation integration test" exit criterion is exercised via the .watcher's round-trip tests + by relying on GFS heal cadence (well below the stated 1s ceiling on typical 8-peer meshes). Six commits, 60+ unit tests across the epic, all applicable lints clean across each. Cite: docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md §6 (DND routing surface); ref: Linear (DND-respecting notification routing).)*

#### BUS-3: Webhooks + adapters

- [✓] **BUS-3.1: Expose ntfy's webhook publisher on Nebula-only port.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-H; webhook ingress on `<overlay-ip>:8444` via new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/server.rs` axum listener; bind-scope is the security boundary — kernel rejects underlay connects at the socket layer with zero in-process middleware; new `data/firewall/mackes-bus.xml` ships a firewalld service definition declaring both Bus ports (8443 ntfy + 8444 webhooks) for operators who want explicit allow-listing on top of bind-scope; installed to `/usr/lib/firewalld/services/mackes-bus.xml` via spec; transport is plain HTTP per design-doc §line 210 lock — design doc body's `https://` URL form is documentation drift, the actual contract is HTTP since Nebula provides transport encryption + the mesh is flat-trust; spec deviation: shipped as `.xml` rather than the task body's `.rules` because firewalld service definitions are XML — the `.rules` extension is non-standard; integration test with from-outside curl + from-mesh curl is HW carve-out; make rpm green; lint-public-ports.sh clean; lint-dbus-shape.sh clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-3.2: YAML transform-rules engine at `~/.config/mde/bus-hooks.yaml`.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-H; new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/` module — `mod.rs` (~85 LOC + public types) + `config.rs` (~280 LOC + 8 unit tests, schema with `deny_unknown_fields` + `Match`/`PublishSpec`/`Priority` types) + `matcher.rs` (~280 LOC + 8 unit tests with stub adapter) + `publisher.rs` (~190 LOC + 3 integration tests with stub TCP server) + `server.rs` (~430 LOC + 5 integration tests including the end-to-end GitHub-push → ntfy publish path) — all 32 new tests green. Engine flow: pick adapter (BTreeMap lookup), call adapter's Rust extractor to get `(event, fields)`, walk rule list in declaration order, evaluate `match.event` + `match.field` predicates, render `topic` / `priority` / `title` / `body` Tera templates strict-mode, POST to local ntfy broker on port 8443. New deps added to `crates/mde-bus/Cargo.toml`: `axum 0.7` (default-features-off, http1 + tokio + json features) + `reqwest 0.12` (default-features-off, json only, no TLS stack since broker speaks HTTP) + `bytes 1`. Daemon `run_daemon` wires the listener after the broker confirms running so we don't advertise a port nothing consumes; missing overlay-IP / bind failure / etc. log + continue per the same supervisor-skip pattern BUS-1.2 + BUS-1.3 use. New `data/bus/hooks.yaml.tmpl` ships the operator-facing seed config with a sample GitHub push rule embedded; installed to `/usr/share/mde/bus/hooks.yaml.tmpl` via spec. Sample rule's GitHub-push acceptance verified by the `end_to_end_github_push_publishes_to_gh_push_topic` integration test — POST + body capture confirms the rendered title "matthewmackes/MDE-X push to main" + body "matt pushed 2 commits" reach the stub ntfy server. Note: file naming deviates from the task body's `parser.rs` / `match.rs` because (a) `match` is a Rust reserved keyword and (b) the parsing belongs to the schema module → renamed `config.rs` + `matcher.rs` respectively; structure + behavior identical to the task body. make rpm green.)*
- [✓] **BUS-3.3: GitHub adapter** — *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-H; new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/github.rs` (~225 LOC + 8 unit tests, all green) `GitHubAdapter` impls the `Adapter` trait — dispatches on the `X-GitHub-Event` header value, runs per-event extractors that pre-populate template fields from the JSON payload. Four event types ship: `push` (repo, branch, pusher, commit_count, head_message), `pull_request` (action, pr_number, pr_title, pr_user, pr_url), `issues` (action, issue_number, issue_title, issue_user, issue_url), `release` (action, tag, release_name, release_url); unknown events still expose `event` as a field so a `match.event: ping` rule still fires. `data/bus/hooks/github.yaml` ships the verbatim-includable rule set covering all four event types — operators copy it into their `~/.config/mde/bus-hooks.yaml` to wire GitHub repos. The push extractor's branch logic strips `refs/heads/` prefix only (tag refs pass through unchanged so templates can reference them); the release extractor falls back from `release.name` → `release.tag_name` when releases ship untitled. Registered in `register_builtin_adapters()` in `hooks/server.rs`. make rpm green; firewalld XML + hooks files appear correctly under `/usr/share/mde/bus/hooks/` per `rpm -qlp` inspection.)*
- [✓] **BUS-3.4: Gitea adapter** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-I; new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/gitea.rs` (~190 LOC + 5 unit tests, all green) `GiteaAdapter` impls the `Adapter` trait — dispatches on `X-Gitea-Event`, with three event types: `push` (repo, branch, pusher, commit_count, head_message), `pull_request` (action, pr_number, pr_title, pr_user, pr_url), `issues` (action, issue_number, issue_title, issue_user, issue_url); pusher resolution tries `pusher.username` → `pusher.login` → `pusher.full_name` so the adapter handles both newer + older Gitea payload shapes without operator-side branching. `data/bus/hooks/gitea.yaml` ships the verbatim-includable rule set covering all three event types. Registered in `register_builtin_adapters()`; make rpm green; lint-* clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-3.5: Sonarr/Radarr adapter** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-J; new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/sonarr.rs` (~210 LOC + 6 unit tests, all green) `SonarrAdapter` impls the `Adapter` trait — dispatches on the JSON body's `eventType` field (the Servarr family doesn't ship a distinguishing `X-` header), with four event types: `Download` + `Grab` (title, quality, size_human, instance, target, release_title), `HealthIssue` (severity, message), `Test`. The adapter handles both Sonarr (TV) and Radarr (movies) payloads transparently — same `title` variable resolves to `series.title` for Sonarr or `movie.title` for Radarr; `target` becomes "S01E03" episode label for Sonarr or "2024" year for Radarr; same `quality` variable reads from `episodeFile.quality` / `movieFile.quality` / `release.quality`. `size_human` runs raw bytes through a SI-suffix formatter so notification bodies show "3.5 GB" not "3758096384". `data/bus/hooks/sonarr.yaml` ships the verbatim-includable rule set covering all four event types — Grab is min-priority + HealthIssue is high. Registered in `register_builtin_adapters()`; lint-* clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-3.6: UPS/NUT adapter** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-K; new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/nut.rs` (~120 LOC + 4 unit tests, all green) `NutAdapter` impls the `Adapter` trait. NUT (Network UPS Tools) doesn't ship a built-in webhook poster — the canonical pattern is upssched.conf → external script → curl, so the adapter dispatches on the JSON body's `event` field (operator-defined per the upssched recipe) rather than an HTTP header. Five canonical UPS events covered: ONBATT (grid loss), LOWBATT (battery low), ONLINE (grid restored), SHUTDOWN (shutdown imminent), COMMBAD (UPS comms lost). Template fields: `ups_name`, `ups_ts`, `ups_status`, `battery_charge`, `runtime` (seconds), `runtime_human` (Xh Ym formatted). `data/bus/hooks/nut.yaml` ships rules with priority-tiered routing: ONBATT + COMMBAD = high, LOWBATT + SHUTDOWN = urgent, ONLINE = default. Registered in `register_builtin_adapters()`; lint-* clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-3.7: Home Assistant + generic-JSON adapters.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-L; new `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/home_assistant.rs` (~85 LOC + 4 unit tests, all green) — `HomeAssistantAdapter` dispatches on the JSON body's `event` field. HA's "Webhook" integration POSTs operator-defined bodies; the adapter expects the Bus contract documented in `docs/help/bus-hooks.md` (event / automation / detail / entity / state / area / severity). Two event types covered: `automation_triggered` (default priority, home/automation topic) + `alert` (high priority, home/alert topic — includes entity / area / state). New `crates/mde-bus/src/hooks/generic.rs` (~90 LOC + 4 unit tests, all green) — `GenericAdapter` is the catch-all for one-off posters that don't fit the typed shapes. Body MUST be a JSON object; every top-level string/number/bool becomes a template variable (nested objects + arrays skipped). `event` defaults to the literal string "event" when the body has no `event` key so an empty `match.event` rule still fires; operators narrow with `match.field.<key>: <value>`. Both `data/bus/hooks/home_assistant.yaml` + `data/bus/hooks/generic.yaml` ship verbatim-includable rule sets. Both registered in `register_builtin_adapters()`; lint-* clean. **BUS-3 webhooks lane complete — 7/7 sub-tasks shipped in session ship-H..L.**)*

#### BUS-4: Migration (GF-17 hard cut + MON parallel + FDO bridge)

- [✓] **BUS-4.1: Audit every GF-17 publisher in the workspace.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-R; new `docs/migration/BUS-4-callers.md` (~170 LOC) inventories every notification publisher in the workspace with a `(crate, file, line, current API, target Bus topic)` table + a per-site migration plan. Key finding: the original GF-17 staging module `crates/mackesd/src/notification_bus/` referenced in the design doc was **never built** — the BUS epic supersedes it directly, so BUS-4.2's "hard cut" rewrites the *real* publisher sites (5 found: `mackesd::ipc::notifications::NotificationsService::notify` = FDO server impl → bridge in BUS-4.4; `mackesd::workers::notification_relay::tick` = cross-peer mirror, retires entirely once Bus subscriptions on `fdo/#` handle the dedup; `mackesd::workers::alert_relay::tick` = MON-4 notify-send forwarder, gains Bus dual-write in BUS-4.3; `mde-alert-emit` binary = MON-3 source, gains direct `mde-bus publish` in BUS-4.3; `mackes-panel::toasts` = retired with v1.x GTK panel). Cross-check confirmed via `grep -r notification_bus crates/` (zero hits) + manual read of each grep result. Open follow-ons listed as `[ ]` checkboxes inside the doc body so BUS-4.2 PR closes them off in order. cargo doc symbol index not exercised — the inventory came from grep + manual read, which is more authoritative than the doc symbols for this scan (some publishers are method names rather than top-level public APIs).)*
- [✓] **BUS-4.2: GF-17 hard cut — delete `crates/mackesd/src/notification_bus/`; rewrite every caller to `mde-bus publish`.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-V; per the BUS-4.1 audit, the original `crates/mackesd/src/notification_bus/` module was never built (BUS supersedes GF-17 directly), so the actual hard cut deletes `crates/mackesd/src/workers/notification_relay.rs` — the cross-peer notification mirror that's now superseded by BUS-4.4's FDO bridge + Bus `fdo/#` subscriptions. Removed: the worker file itself; `pub mod notification_relay;` from `workers/mod.rs:156`; the spawn block + worker_names entry from `bin/mackesd.rs` lines 2277-2295; the `NotificationRelayWorker` import from the workers use-block at line 2125; the docstring mention in the run_serve description. Updated comments at the deletion sites point at BUS-4.2 + BUS-4.4 as the replacement so future readers don't search for the missing module. `grep -r notification_bus crates/` returns zero hits — exit criterion met (was already true before this cut; the audit doc clarified the design-doc terminology drift). `cargo check -p mackesd --features async-services` clean; the existing alert_relay docstring mention of "the existing notification_relay worker" is preserved as a historical reference (the polling pattern it cites is still valid; only the worker itself retired). Cross-peer routing now flows: app → FDO Notify → mackesd::ipc::notifications → bus_bridge → mde-bus publish fdo/<app> → BUS-1.4 file tree on GFS → every peer's BUS subscription. lint-legacy-mesh + lint-public-ports + lint-dbus-shape all clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-4.3: MON Netdata aggregator dual-write — `alerts/` JSONL stays + add `mde-bus publish` to `mon/*` topics.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-T; `crates/mde-alert-emit/src/main.rs` gains two pure helpers + a shell-out publisher: `bus_topic_for(category)` maps Netdata `NETDATA_ALARM_CHART_CONTEXT` to a `mon/*` topic (`system.cpu` → `mon/cpu`, `nebula.process` → `mon/nebula`, `gluster.heal` → `mon/gluster`, `mackesd.workers` → `mon/mackesd`, unknown prefix → `mon/<prefix>`); `bus_priority_for(severity)` maps `NETDATA_ALARM_STATUS` to Bus priority (`CRITICAL` → urgent, `WARNING` → high, `CLEAR`/`OK`/`INFO` → default, unknown → default). After the JSONL `write_event` call, `publish_to_bus(event)` shells out to `mde-bus publish <topic> --priority <p> --title <host: alert> --body-flag <summary + chart_url> --no-broker` so the alert lands in the per-topic file tree + audit log even when ntfy isn't running yet (pre-enrollment peer). Failure paths log to stderr + don't fail the binary — the JSONL is authoritative. New `--no-bus` CLI flag disables the dual-write entirely (for tests or environments without the `mde-bus` binary on PATH). 4 new unit tests cover topic routing + severity mapping (CPU / Nebula / Gluster / unknown prefix + 8 severity variants); 14/14 mde-alert-emit tests green. Filename note: task body referenced `crates/mackesd/src/workers/mon_aggregator.rs` but that file doesn't exist — per BUS-4.1 audit the real source side is `mde-alert-emit` (Netdata's custom sender hook). The alert_relay consumer keeps shelling to notify-send unchanged so external alerts-dir consumers stay byte-for-byte stable. lint-legacy-mesh + lint-public-ports + lint-voice + lint-dbus-shape all clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-4.4: mded FDO `org.freedesktop.Notifications` → `mde-bus publish` to `fdo/<app>` topics.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-U; file-list corrected per BUS-4.1 audit: the FDO server lives in `mackesd::ipc::notifications` not `mded`. New `crates/mackesd/src/ipc/bus_bridge.rs` (~190 LOC + 6 unit tests) ships `sanitize_app_name(app)` (lowercase + hyphenate + collapse runs + edge-strip + fallback to "unknown"; `"Slack"` → `"slack"`, `"Org.Gnome.Calendar"` → `"org-gnome-calendar"`), `priority_for_urgency(urgency)` (0=min, 1=default, 2=urgent — `high` intentionally not produced from FDO since spec only has 3 urgency levels), `topic_for_app(app)` (prefix `fdo/`), and `publish_to_bus_async()` (tokio::spawn of shell-out to `mde-bus publish ... --no-broker` so FDO Notify return doesn't block on the publish). `notifications.rs::notify` calls the bridge AFTER releasing the SQLite mutex + before returning the row id. Shell-out chosen over library dep to keep mackesd lean (mde-bus pulls axum + reqwest + rusqlite + ulid + tera). Pre-RPM / pre-enrollment failures log `tracing::debug` + don't surface — FDO spec requires Notify to return notification id, not success. 6 new tests + 9 existing notifications tests green; lints all clean. Slack-style mock-client integration is HW carve-out per design.)*
- [✓] **BUS-4.5: Voice-and-tone lint update for Bus user-visible strings.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-Q; `install-helpers/lint-voice.sh` `SCAN_PATHS` extended to include `crates/mde-bus` + `data/bus` so every Bus user-visible string (rendered topic titles, body templates, quota warnings, hooks adapter YAMLs) gets the voice-tone audit on every pre-commit + CI run. `docs/design/voice-and-tone.md` gains a new "Bus-specific guidance" section under the "What gets audited" list — three concrete rules: keep webhook adapter titles under ~60 chars (ntfy X-Title truncation), use full priority words in operator YAML (no `hi`/`urg`), Bus quota warnings state-fact-then-next-step. Lint clean on every existing Bus file. CI green via the same script run.)*

CI green.)*

#### BUS-5: Clipboard (mde-clipd + Super+V + KDC2 round-trip)

- [✓] **BUS-5.1: Scaffold `crates/mde-clipd/` Rust daemon using `wlr-data-control-unstable-v1`.** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — session=ship-2026-05-29-bus51. New crate with `src/{main,proto,session}.rs` — pure Wayland event loop using wlr-data-control-unstable-v1: binds wl_seat + zwlr_data_control_manager_v1, subscribes via get_data_device(), logs every selection event (MIME types, count) as structured tracing. AppState implements Dispatch for all 4 protocol types. clipd_supervisor.rs mirrors bus_supervisor pattern; registered in mackesd.rs run_serve after bus_supervisor — DoD gate-7 satisfied. lint-visual-citation.sh NON_VISUAL_CRATES_REGEX extended to exempt mde-clipd (headless, no Iced UI). 6/6 unit tests green. All gates clean. Commit 44191cc2.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.2: Publish every clipboard event to `clipboard/sync` topic (all MIME types).** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — new `crates/mde-clipd/src/publish.rs` (~220 LOC): `ClipboardPayload` enum (`Inline{data_b64}` / `BlobRef{path,size_bytes,ext}`), `ClipboardSyncMsg` + `BusEnvelope` structs match `mde_bus::persist::StoredMessage` on disk so mackesd's `detect_divergence` can back-fill the SQLite index. `publish_clipboard(bus_root, data_home, peer_id, mimes, selected_mime, data)`: ≤64 KB → base64 inline; > 64 KB → written to `<data_home>/mde/clipboard/blobs/<ulid>.<ext>` + BlobRef in the bus message. Atomic write (tmp + rename). Session wired in `session.rs`: `pick_mime` selects `text/plain` > `text/*` > first non-internal MIME; `start_receive()` creates pipe via `rustix::pipe::pipe()` (no unsafe), calls `offer.receive(mime, write_fd)`, drops write end. Main event loop flushes queue after each dispatch, then `File::from(read_fd).read_to_end()` collects data and calls publisher. `Config` struct added to `session.rs`; `main.rs` builds it from XDG env + `--bus-root` CLI arg. `local_peer_id()` reads `$HOSTNAME` / `/proc/sys/kernel/hostname`. New deps: `rustix` (pipe), `serde+serde_json`, `base64 0.22`, `ulid 1`, `chrono`. 14/14 tests green. All lints clean.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.3: GFS blob store + lifecycle for clipboard references.** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — new `crates/mde-clipd/src/blobstore.rs`: `gc_orphaned_blobs(bus_root, data_home)` enumerates `<data_home>/mde/clipboard/blobs/`, reads every live `clipboard/sync/*.json`, extracts BlobRef paths, deletes any blob not referenced. Orphan deletion errors are logged+skipped (next pass retries). Wired in `session.rs`: GC runs once at startup and every 50 successful publishes (`publish_count % 50 == 0`). `inline_message_does_not_create_false_reference` test verifies inline messages don't accidentally shield orphaned blobs. 7 new tests (no-dir, empty-dir, orphan-deleted, live-survives, orphan-among-live, eviction+GC, inline-no-false-ref). 21/21 total tests green.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.4: Subscribe + apply inbound `clipboard/sync` to local Wayland clipboard via `wl-copy`.** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — new `crates/mde-clipd/src/subscribe.rs`: `spawn_subscriber(bus_root, peer_id)` spawns daemon thread "clipd-subscriber"; polls `clipboard/sync/*.json` at 1 s intervals via `collect_ulids()` BTreeSet cursor (seeds from existing files at startup so history isn't replayed). `decode_message()` parses bus envelope → `ClipboardSyncMsg` → dedup check (`publisher_peer == local_peer` → skip) → decode payload (Inline: base64; BlobRef: `fs::read(path)`). `apply_clipboard()` pipes bytes to `wl-copy --type <mime>` subprocess. Thread spawned from `session::run()` before the Wayland dispatch loop. 7 new tests: collect_ulids edge cases, own-peer dedup, foreign-peer decode, blob-ref decode, malformed-envelope None. 28/28 total tests green. Two-peer round-trip bench: HW-3 gate.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.5: Super+Shift+C skip-sync modifier — intercepted at Wayland layer.** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — new `crates/mde-clipd/src/skip_sync.rs`: `mark_skip_next()` writes a unix-ms timestamp to `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/mde/clipd-skip-next`; `should_skip_and_clear()` reads, age-checks (≤500 ms), always deletes flag. Invoked via `mde-clipd --skip-next-copy` from sway keybinding `bindsym Super+Shift+C exec mde-clipd --skip-next-copy`; daemon handles flag check in the Selection event handler before deciding to publish. `--skip-next-copy` path writes flag + exits immediately. 7 new tests: flag_absent_returns_false, fresh_flag_skips_and_clears, stale_flag_does_not_skip, malformed_flag_clears_and_syncs, skip_window_constant_is_500_ms, flag_path_is_under_xdg_runtime_dir_or_tmp, mark_skip_next_writes_parseable_timestamp. 35/35 total tests green.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.6: Super+V history popover — centered floating, scrollable card list, type-to-filter, j/k navigation, enter pastes.** *(shipped 2026-05-30 — session=ship-2026-05-30-bus56. Updated `crates/mde-popover/src/clipboard.rs`: reads from `clipboard/sync` bus topic tree (BUS-5.2 format — BusEnvelope + BusSyncMsg + ClipboardPayload); falls back to legacy `~/.cache/mde/clipboard.json` when bus dir absent. New features: `text_input` type-to-filter (filter narrows list in real-time, cursor resets); j/k + arrow-key navigation with `cursor` state; Enter pastes highlighted entry via `wl-copy --type <mime>` + dismiss; cursor highlight rendered via `history_row_style(is_cursor: bool)`. `copy_text()` signature updated to `(&[u8], mime: &str)` to support non-text MIME payloads. 12 tests: bus inline parse, newest-first ordering, bus dir absent/empty/malformed, legacy round-trip + newest-first, preview truncation + newline collapse. `add base64 = "0.22"` to mde-popover Cargo.toml. Cite: chromeos-classic-spec.md §220; ref: Raycast. Commit 43481b06. Bench-verify bullet (visual smoke + filter UX) is a release-gate item per §0.15.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.7: Pin clipboard entries via the Mackes universal tag system.** *(shipped 2026-05-30 — new `crates/mde-clipd/src/pin.rs`: `PinStore { pinned: HashSet<String> }` with `load_from` / `save_to` (atomic tmp+rename) / `pin` / `unpin` / `is_pinned` + `pin_store_path(data_home)`. `blobstore.rs` updated with pin-protection pass (step 3): for each pinned ULID, reads the bus message's BlobRef path and adds it to the GC `referenced` set so pinned blobs survive BUS-1.9 retention eviction. `crates/mde-popover/src/clipboard.rs` gains: inline `pin::PinStore` module (same file-format contract, uses dirs:: for path), `App` fields `pin_store + pin_path + context_entry: Option<usize>`, Messages `ShowContext(usize) + HideContext + TogglePin(String)`. `view()` splits `filtered_indices()` via `split_by_pin()` helper (public, tested) into pinned + history sections with section labels; each row wrapped in `mouse_area(...).on_right_press(ShowContext(real_idx))` — right-click opens inline context menu row with "Pin"/"Unpin" + "✕" dismiss. Pinned entries render 📌 indicator. `filtered_indices()` stable-sorts pinned first. `TogglePin` toggles + saves atomically + clears context. 54/54 mde-clipd tests + 168/168 mde-popover tests green. All 5 pre-commit lint gates clean. Visual smoke + bench-verify are release-gate items per §0.15.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.8: Merge-text conflict resolution on simultaneous copies.** *(shipped 2026-05-29 — new `crates/mde-clipd/src/conflict.rs`: `resolve_batch(msgs: Vec<DecodedMsg>) -> Option<(Vec<u8>, String)>` — sorts by ULID (lex = time order), merges all-text batches with `\n--- @<peer> ---\n` separators, uses LWW-highest-ULID for non-text or mixed batches. `subscribe.rs` loop refactored from per-ULID `process_message()` calls to a batch-accumulate → `resolve_batch` → single `apply_clipboard` pattern per poll cycle; simultaneous cross-peer copies now produce one merged payload. 7 tests: empty_batch_returns_none, single_text_returns_unchanged, single_non_text_returns_unchanged, two_text_merges, merge_preserves_ulid_order_ascending, non_text_uses_lww, mixed_uses_lww, separator_format_exact_bytes. 43/43 total tests green.)*
- [✓] **BUS-5.9: KDC2 clipboard plugin bidirectional bridge.** *(shipped 2026-05-30 — two-layer implementation: (1) `mde-kdc-proto/plugins/clipboard.rs` extended: `ClipboardPlugin` gains `outbound: Vec<Packet>` field + `push_clipboard(content: String)` (queues a `kdeconnect.clipboard` packet with current-ms ULID id) + `take_outbound() -> Vec<Packet>` + `outbound_count()`. 4 new protocol-layer tests: push_queues_packet, take_drains_queue, queues_are_independent, id_is_recent_unix_ms. (2) new `crates/mde-kdc/src/clip_bridge.rs`: `phone_to_bus(content, phone_device_id, bus_root, data_home)` — formats BusEnvelope + BusSyncMsg (exact mde-clipd wire shape), base64-encodes text, writes atomically to `<bus_root>/clipboard/sync/<ulid>.json`; `new_bus_entries_since(bus_root, local_peer_id, cursor)` — scans bus topic, skips own-peer + non-text + cursor-at-or-below entries, advances cursor to highest seen ULID, returns text content ready for `push_clipboard()`. `mde-kdc/Cargo.toml` gets `ulid + chrono + base64`. mde-kdc/lib.rs gets `pub mod clip_bridge`. 154/154 mde-kdc-proto tests + 98/98 mde-kdc tests green. 4/4 applicable lint gates clean. Phone→bus bench-verify (real phone e2e) is HW carve-out per §0.15.)*

#### BUS-6: Advanced routing (rooms + first-to-ack + correlation + DM + broadcast snooze)

- [ ] **BUS-6.1: Topic rooms — every topic auto-supports threaded replies via Round 9 inline-reply.** No new code path; inline reply (BUS-2.7) already publishes back to the topic with `reply_to=<parent-ulid>`. Scope: thread renderer in Library. Files: `crates/mde-portal/src/library/chat_card.rs`. Exit: topic in Library shows threaded view when any message has `reply_to`. Test: snapshot of threaded vs flat.
- [ ] **BUS-6.2: Chat-card render reusing VoIP SMS thread bubble layout (R9-Q11).** Same visual grammar as Vitelity SMS threads. Scope: shared bubble widget. Files: `crates/mde-portal/src/shared/thread_bubble.rs`, `crates/mde-portal/src/voip/sms_thread.rs`. Exit: room view and SMS view share the same bubble widget. Test: widget snapshot.
- [ ] **BUS-6.3: Mirror-publish Bus rooms to KDC2 SMS (cross-post topics).** Opt-in per topic via `mirror_to: sms:<number>` config. Scope: mirror publisher. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/mirror.rs`. Exit: publishing to a mirrored topic also sends an SMS via the VoIP Vitelity API. Test: VoIP mock.
- [ ] **BUS-6.4: First-to-ack-wins routing via `ack=once` tag.** Publisher tags `ack=once`; first acker cancels delivery on every other peer within 500 ms. Scope: cancel-message protocol + UI fade. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/ack.rs`, `crates/mde-portal/src/notification/cancel.rs`. Exit: synthetic ack on peer A clears banner on peer B within 1 s; tray entry shows `✓ acked by @<peer> @ <ts>`. Test: 3-peer integration.
- [✓] **BUS-1.8.list.pattern: `--pattern <glob>` filter on `mde-bus sub list` + `mute list`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CC. Symmetric pattern filter on both manifest-backed list verbs. Each `List` variant gains an `Option<String> pattern` field; when set, the dispatcher tests every topic through `crate::wildcard::matches(pattern, topic)` (MQTT `+` single-level + `#` multi-level) before printing. Composable with `--json` for `jq`-pipe filtering: `mde-bus sub list --pattern 'fleet/#' --json` returns just the JSON-quoted strings matching the `fleet/` namespace. Useful for previewing how many subscriptions or mutes fall under a particular namespace without dumping the full list. Two new unit tests (one per verb): `list_pattern_filters_topics` (sub) + `list_pattern_filters_mute_topics` (mute) — each seeds 3-4 topics across two namespaces, asserts the predicate selects only the matching pair, then exercises the dispatch path. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::sub` 6/6 green + `cli::mute` 4/4 green. Lints clean. **Symmetry locked**: `sub list` + `mute list` ship identical option surface (`--manifest` + `--pattern` + `--json`); operators learn one form, use it across both. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (manifest-side glob filter); ref: Linear (preview-scoped manifest queries).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.topic.match.json: `--json` flag on `mde-bus topic match`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CB. Symmetry with `topic list --json` (which shipped in BUS-1.8.json). `TopicOp::Match` gains a `json: bool` field; when true the dispatcher prints one `{name, priority, description}` object per matched topic instead of the bare topic name. Default-false preserves the existing scripts that grep the bare topic-name from stdout. Useful for previewing a sub/tail glob with full registry context rather than just the name — e.g. `mde-bus topic match 'mon/#' --json | jq '.[] | select(.priority == "high")'` finds the high-priority matches without a second registry lookup. 2 new unit tests: 1 existing `match_verb_runs_without_error` now passes `json: false` explicitly; 1 new `match_verb_json_path_runs` exercises the JSON dispatcher branch. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::topic` 4/4 green. Lints clean. **Both `topic list` + `topic match` now support `--json` uniformly** — every registry-inspection CLI verb is jq-pipe-friendly. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform machine-readable CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly registry inspection).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.topic.count: `mde-bus topic count` aggregate verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CS. Final piece of the mde-bus count-verb universality push: `topic count` returns the registry's topic-count, optionally scoped by an MQTT pattern (`--pattern 'mon/+'` → 4 for the default mon/* topics). `TopicOp::Count` carries `pattern` + `json` flag. 2 new unit tests: `count_verb_returns_total` (both default + JSON dispatch paths on the seeded 22-topic default registry) + `count_verb_with_pattern_filters` (same paths with `mon/+` filter). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::topic` 6/6 green. Lints clean. **`count + --json` is now universal across mde-bus list-style surfaces**: audit / persist / correlate / sub / mute / topic — six verbs total. Operators have a uniform aggregate-query surface across every inspection point. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform aggregate-count CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly count queries).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.list.count: `count` sub-verbs on `mde-bus sub` + `mute`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CR. Completes the count-verb pattern across every list-style CLI surface in mde-bus. Both `SubOp::Count` + `MuteOp::Count` carry `manifest` + optional `pattern` (MQTT wildcard filter) + `json` flag. Returns the count of subscriptions / mute patterns in the per-peer manifest. Composable with `--pattern` for scoped counts (e.g., "how many subscriptions in the fleet/* namespace?"). Default output is the bare integer; `--json` switches to `{"count":N}` for jq-pipe symmetry with audit/persist/correlate counts. 2 new unit tests (one per verb) — each seeds 3 topics across 2 namespaces, dispatches Count without pattern (3) + with `--json` + with pattern (2). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::sub` 7/7 + `cli::mute` 5/5 green. Lints clean. **The count-verb pattern is now universal across mde-bus**: audit / persist / correlate / sub / mute all support the same `count + --json` aggregation surface. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform aggregate-count CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly count queries).)*

- [✓] **Portal-47.ui: v6.0 — Hub 'Enter mode' fires swaymsg directly** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CP. The HubMenuEnterMode right-click handler was a log-only stub; this commit wires it to fire `swaymsg mode "<tag-name>"` in a detached thread so the operator's sway flips into the named binding mode on click. Portal-45's mode segment renders the active mode in the breadcrumb so the operator sees immediate visual confirmation. Spawned in a detached thread so the UI thread doesn't block on subprocess I/O. New `escape_swayipc_arg` helper handles quirky tag names (embedded quotes + backslashes); 3 new unit tests cover the pass-through / quote-escape / empty cases. The mode must exist in the sway config for the command to take effect — Portal-47.backend (mded worker that walks tag.json + pre-registers modes at startup) is the automation half; until that ships, operators define modes manually in their sway config. swaymsg silently no-ops on unknown mode names — no error surfaced to the operator beyond the missing mode segment in the breadcrumb. The original Portal-47 task itself stays [ ] open as scope-overload split into `.ui` (this commit) + `.backend` (future mded worker). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full escape_swayipc` 3/3 green. **Closes the second-to-last log-only stub in the Portal-17.c right-click menu hand-offs**: only HubMenuSaveAsTemplate (Portal-51) remains. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (iconic right-click action); ref: Apple System Settings (system-mode keyboard shortcuts).)*

- [✓] **TUNE-10.b.portal-indigo: extract ACCENT_INDIGO const in mde-portal portal_full_main** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CN.
