The Q2-locked indigo accent `Color { r: 0.357, g: 0.416, b: 0.961, a: 1.0 }` (#5b6af5) appeared as an inline literal in 5 sites in `crates/mde-portal/src/portal_full_main.rs` — Apply button background (3 modals: Edit-tag, Edit-window-rule, Hub menu pill), active-state layout-chooser option, and tristate-button enabled state. Extracted to a new module-level `const ACCENT_INDIGO: Color = ...` next to the existing CHARCOAL/FG/FG_DIM block. All 5 inline literals replaced with the const identifier; only the const declaration itself is now a hex Color literal. `install-helpers/lint-design-tokens.allowlist` re-derived — portal_full_main.rs entries shrunk from ~31 to 19 (saved ~12 stale entries that pointed at literals that had moved or been replaced). Net allow-list lines: 163 (was 175). `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full` 83/83 green (no behavior change). All 8 applicable lints clean. Per TUNE-10.b's "drop matching allow-list lines" directive — each cleanup commit shrinks the allow-list. **Next batches** can promote ACCENT_INDIGO to `mde_theme` so other crates (mde-workbench, mde-popover, mde-applets) can import it; this commit is the first step. Cite: docs/design/chromeos-classic-spec.md §Iconography (Q2 indigo lock) + motion-language.md §3 (primary-action accent); ref: Apple System Settings (system-wide accent color reuse).)*

- [✓] **Portal-44.b: v6.0 — Hub right-click 'Layout chooser…' → Edit-tag modal redirect** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CM. The HubMenuLayoutChooser right-click action was a log-only stub since Portal-17.c shipped — Portal-44's backend (TagLayoutWorker) already enforces per-tag `default_layout` server-side but the UI surface defaulted to "operators hand-edit tags.json." This commit wires the menu item to open the existing Portal-18.b Edit-tag modal (which already includes the 5-option layout chooser row), seeded with the right-clicked tag's current values. Re-uses the existing modal infrastructure rather than building a separate stand-alone layout-only modal — the menu item is just a faster gesture to the same surface. Two-line handler change + 1 new unit test `hub_menu_layout_chooser_opens_edit_tag_modal` asserting modal state transition + right-click target clear. `cargo test -p mde-portal --bin mde-portal-full hub_menu_layout` 1/1 green. Lints clean. **Closes the last log-only stub in the Portal-17.c right-click menu hand-offs**: only HubMenuEnterMode (Portal-47) + HubMenuSaveAsTemplate (Portal-51) remain as log-only stubs, both blocked on their own backend workers. Cite: visual-identity.md §3 (iconic right-click fast-path); ref: Apple System Settings (context-menu shortcut to detail view).)*

- [✓] **BUS-6.5.cli.count: `mde-bus correlate count` aggregate verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CL. Completes the aggregate-count trifecta across mde-bus's inspection surfaces: `audit count` + `persist count` + `correlate count`. Returns the number of configured correlation rules from `bus-correlate.yaml`. Default output is the bare integer; `--json` switches to `{"count":N}` for jq-pipe symmetry. New `CorrelateOp::Count` variant carries `config` + `json` flag. Missing config file → 0 (empty rules vec; load_default handles missing-file as default-empty-config). 2 new unit tests: `count_on_missing_config_returns_zero` (both default + JSON paths) + `count_with_existing_config_succeeds` (2-rule YAML fixture, dispatches both paths). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::correlate` 5/5 green (3 pre-existing + 2 new). Lints clean. **mde-bus CLI count-verb pattern is now uniform across every list-style inspection surface**: audit / persist / correlate all ship `list` + `count` with matching `--json` flags. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform aggregate-count CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly count queries).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.4.cli.count: `mde-bus persist count` aggregate verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CK. Symmetric with the just-shipped `audit count` — returns the total number of indexed messages in the SQLite store. Wraps the existing `Persist::count()` helper (was previously test-only + used by `history --count`). Useful for monitoring + dashboards ("how many bus messages persist right now?") + capacity planning ("at the current pace, when will we hit the retention quota?"). Default output is the bare integer; `--json` switches to `{"count":N}` for jq-pipe symmetry with `audit count --json`. New `PersistOp::Count` variant carries `bus_root` + `json` flag. 2 new unit tests: `count_on_empty_persist_returns_zero` (exercises both default + JSON dispatch paths) + `count_returns_actual_message_count` (writes 3 messages, asserts `count()` == 3, then dispatches the verb). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::persist` 5/5 green (3 pre-existing + 2 new). Lints clean. **`mde-bus persist` now ships both `verify` (divergence audit) + `count` (aggregate); symmetry with `audit` (list + count) locks the inspection-verb pattern across both diagnostic surfaces.** Cite: motion-language.md §6 (persist diagnostics surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly count queries).)*

- [✓] **BUS-7.1.cli.count: `mde-bus audit count` aggregate verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CJ. New sub-verb that returns the count of audit entries matching the same five filters as `list` (publisher / topic / priority / since-date / until-date). Useful for monitoring + dashboards where the full list would be too noisy ("how many urgent publishes happened today?"). Default output is a bare decimal integer; `--json` switches to `{"count":N}` for jq-pipe symmetry with `list --json`. New `Count` variant on `AuditOp` carries the same 6 args + json flag; dispatcher reads entries via `audit::read_entries`, runs `apply_filters` with the same 5 predicates, then prints `filtered.len()`. 1 new unit test exercising both default-and JSON dispatch paths on an empty bus_root. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::audit` 11/11 green (10 pre-existing + 1 new). Lints clean. **`mde-bus audit` now ships every operator-facing aggregate-and-list verb**: `list` (with 5 filters + tail + json) + `count` (with the same 5 filters + json). Cite: motion-language.md §6 (operator-facing audit-aggregate); ref: Linear (jq-friendly count queries).)*

- [✓] **BUS-7.1.cli.date-filter: `--since-date` + `--until-date` on `mde-bus audit list`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-CA. Date-window filters for the per-peer audit log. Both flags take `YYYY-MM-DD` strings; predicate is a pure string prefix comparison against `ts_iso[..10]` (ISO 8601 dates are sort-friendly so this works without parsing). Inclusive semantics on both sides: `--since-date 2026-05-24 --until-date 2026-05-26` returns entries from 2026-05-24 00:00:00 through 2026-05-26 23:59:59. Composable with every existing audit filter (publisher / topic / priority / tail / json). Malformed `ts_iso` rows (shorter than 10 chars) silently drop when either date filter is active — operators relying on date scoping won't see corrupt rows. `apply_filters` signature grew from 4 args to 6; all 5 existing call sites updated. 4 new unit tests: since-date-keeps-at-or-after, until-date-keeps-at-or-before, date-range-combines-since-and-until, malformed-ts-drops. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::audit` 10/10 green (6 pre-existing + 4 new). Lints clean. **`mde-bus audit list` now ships every operator-facing audit-scan filter**: `--publisher` + `--topic` + `--priority` + `--since-date` + `--until-date` + `--tail` + `--json` + `--bus-root` — 8 knobs total. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (operator-facing audit-scan filters); ref: Linear (date-bounded audit queries).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.history.reverse: `--reverse` flag on `mde-bus history`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BZ. Output-ordering toggle: by default `mde-bus history` iterates rows in ULID-ascending (chronological / oldest-first) order; with `--reverse` the row Vec is reversed before printing so newest-first lands at the top. The reverse is applied AFTER the `--count` slice — `--count 5 --reverse` still returns "the last 5 messages, newest first", not "the first 5 reversed". Composable with every other history flag (`--since` / `--before` / `--count` / `--json` / `--bus-root`). Useful when scanning a noisy topic in an interactive terminal: the most recent message lands at the top instead of scrolling off-screen. Pure-fn: relies on `Vec::reverse` on the already-materialized row set; ULID monotonicity guarantees the reverse-iterated order matches publish timestamp descending. 1 new unit test `reverse_flag_runs_without_error` asserts the `Vec::reverse` invariant (asc[0] = desc[2], asc[2] = desc[0]) + exercises the dispatch path. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::history` 4/4 green (3 pre-existing + 1 new). Lints clean. **`mde-bus history` now ships every operator-facing pagination/ordering knob**: `--since` (start cursor) + `--before` (end cursor) + `--count` (window size) + `--reverse` (print order) + `--json` (machine-readable). Cite: motion-language.md §6 (operator-facing ordering control); ref: Linear (terminal-friendly history scan).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.history.before: `--before <ulid>` end-cursor on `mde-bus history`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BW. Pagination affordance: pairs with the existing `--since <ulid>` start-cursor so operators can read a half-open `(since, before)` window of stored messages without dumping the full topic. `HistoryArgs` gains a `before: Option<String>` field; the verb applies the cursor as an exclusive in-memory `retain(|m| m.ulid.as_str() < before)` filter AFTER `list_since` but BEFORE the `--count` slice — so `--count` limits the size of the filtered window, not the unfiltered tail. Useful for "show me the 50 messages older than ULID X" / "page backwards through a noisy topic" patterns. Composable with `--since` for explicit windows (e.g., `--since 01H... --before 01J...` returns exactly the messages in that ULID range). The in-memory filter is correct given `list_since` already materializes the full topic; a future SQL-side `list_before` predicate is the optimization path if topic sizes grow past tens of thousands of messages. **Bench-verified** via new `before_cursor_excludes_rows_at_or_after` test: 5 publishes → cursor at ULIDs[3] → `retain(< cursor)` returns exactly 3 rows (ULIDs[0..3]), every retained ulid is strictly less than the cursor, run() completes Ok. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib cli::history` 3 tests green (2 pre-existing + 1 new). Lints clean. **mde-bus history now ships both cursor directions** — operators can paginate forward (`--since`) and backward (`--before`) through any topic's persistent log. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (operator-facing pagination); ref: Linear (jq-friendly cursor verbs).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.9.cli: `mde-bus retention status` policy + quota inspector verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BV. New CLI verb wrapping the BUS-1.9 retention engine's constants + `disk_usage_bytes` helper for operator-facing diagnostics. `crates/mde-bus/src/cli/retention.rs` ships `RetentionOp::Status { bus_root, json }` — prints per-priority TTLs (min=1d / default=7d / high=30d / urgent=forever), GFS quota tiers (soft=500MB / hard=2GB), and current `<bus_root>` disk usage. Triggers an `!` warning line when usage exceeds the soft quota, `***` callout when over the hard quota. Default human-readable output is multi-line for terminal readability; `--json` flag emits a flat object with every TTL + quota + usage field for jq-pipe / monitoring. Read-only — the daemon's tick loop owns retention GC; this verb intentionally has no `pass` sub-verb to avoid concurrent-GC race conditions. New `format_bytes` + `format_ttl` pure-fn helpers handle the human-readable conversion. 4 new unit tests: status-runs-on-empty-bus-root, status-json-runs-on-empty-bus-root, format-bytes-handles-all-orders, format-ttl-handles-all-units. **Bench-verified end-to-end** on empty bus_root: human-readable mode prints all 4 TTL lines + quota tiers + "used: 0 B"; JSON mode prints `{"bus_root":..., "quota_hard_bytes":2147483648, ..., "ttl_urgent":"forever", "used_bytes":0}`. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 240 tests green (236 pre-existing + 4 new). Lints clean. **mde-bus CLI now ships 11 operator-facing verbs** (publish + tail + sub + mute + topic + history + dnd + correlate + audit + persist + retention). Cite: motion-language.md §6 (retention diagnostics surface); ref: Linear (operator-facing storage telemetry).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.4.cli: `mde-bus persist verify` divergence audit verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BU. New CLI verb wrapping the existing `Persist::detect_divergence` helper for operator-facing diagnostics on the BUS-1.4 persistence layer. `crates/mde-bus/src/cli/persist.rs` ships `PersistOp::Verify { bus_root, json }` — opens the persist layer, walks the SQLite index + per-topic file tree, flags any `files_without_rows` (file tree has it, index doesn't — external write or index corruption) or `rows_without_files` (index has it, file tree doesn't — external delete or retention bug). Default human-readable output: `OK — persist clean (0 divergent rows, 0 orphan files)` or `DIVERGENT — N file(s) without rows, M row(s) without files` + bulleted detail per divergent path. `--json` flag emits a `{"files_without_rows": [...], "rows_without_files": [...]}` object for jq-pipe / CI parsing. Returns `Err(anyhow!)` (nonzero exit) when divergence is detected so CI / pre-commit hooks can gate on it. **Bench-verified end-to-end**: clean persist → exit 0 with OK message; dropping `orphan/01XYZ.json` into the bus_root → exit 1 with `file-without-row: orphan/01XYZ.json` + matching JSON output. 3 new unit tests: verify-empty-bus-root-clean, verify-with-orphan-file-returns-err, verify-json-path-runs. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib persist` 15 tests green (12 pre-existing + 3 new). Lints clean. **--json now covers BOTH read + write + diagnostic mde-bus CLI verbs**: 9 prior + 1 new = 10 verbs total with uniform `--json` surface. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (persist diagnostics surface); ref: Linear (CI-gateable verify verbs).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.json.publish: `--json` flag on `mde-bus publish`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BT. Extends --json to the write side. `PublishArgs` gains a `json: bool` field; when true the publish output switches from the bare ULID line to a full `serde_json::to_string(&stored_message)` — operators get topic + ulid + priority + title + body + ts_unix_ms + file_path in one machine-readable line. Default-false preserves existing scripts that grep the bare ULID from stdout. **Bench-verified end-to-end**: default mode prints `01KSN77EVBYW721AETNS1270AK`; --json mode prints the full StoredMessage object; piping `--json | python3 json.loads` extracts `topic` + `ulid` correctly. 1 existing test updated to include `json: false`. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 233 tests green. Lints clean. **--json now covers BOTH read-style + write-style mde-bus CLI verbs**: 8 read verbs (audit list + correlate list + topic list + dnd status + sub list + mute list + history + tail) + 1 write verb (publish) = 9 total. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform machine-readable CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.json.tail: `--json` flag on `mde-bus tail`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BS. Final read-style mde-bus verb to gain `--json`. `TailArgs` gains a `json: bool` field. New `format_emit(msg, json)` helper centralises the formatter so both emit-sites (count-mode pre-loop + follow-mode poll loop) stay in sync — neither emit site can drift from the other on future formatter changes. When `--json` is true, output switches to JSONL (full StoredMessage object per line); otherwise the existing tail-format summary (`<ulid>  <topic>  <priority>  <title-summary>`). 2 new unit tests: format_emit-json-round-trips-full-envelope (JSON output deserializes back to the same StoredMessage) + format_emit-tsv-matches-format_line (TSV mode is byte-for-byte identical to format_line). Existing tail-count test extended to include `json: false`. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 233 tests green. Lints clean. **--json sweep now covers EVERY read-oriented mde-bus CLI verb (8 verbs)**: audit list + correlate list + topic list + dnd status + sub list + mute list + history + tail. Operators have a uniform jq-pipe surface across the entire bus inspection toolkit. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform machine-readable CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.json.history: `--json` flag on `mde-bus history`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BR. Final piece of the --json sweep — all 7 read-oriented CLI verbs now support `--json`. `HistoryArgs` gains a `json: bool` field; when true output switches from the tail-format summary (`<ulid>  <topic>  <priority>  <title-summary>`) to JSONL — each line is a full `StoredMessage` object (StoredMessage already derives Serialize) suitable for piping to `jq`. JSON output exposes the full envelope including `ts_unix_ms` + `file_path` fields that the tail summary omits, so operators can pipe `mde-bus history fleet/announce --json | jq 'select(.ts_unix_ms > 1700000000000)'` to filter by timestamp. **Bench-verified end-to-end** with 2 publishes into a tmpdir + a history read: default mode prints tail-format lines; `--json` mode prints valid JSONL with all 7 StoredMessage fields. 2 existing tests updated to include `json: false` in HistoryArgs literals. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 231 tests green. Lints clean. **--json sweep complete across all 7 read-oriented mde-bus CLI verbs**: audit list + correlate list + topic list + dnd status + sub list + mute list + history. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform machine-readable CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.json.subs-mute: `--json` flag on `mde-bus sub list` + `mde-bus mute list`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BQ. Extends the --json sweep to the two remaining list-style verbs (sub list + mute list). Both `SubOp::List` and `MuteOp::List` gain a `json: bool` field; when true output switches from plain-text per-line to JSON-quoted strings per line (using `serde_json::to_string(&topic_str)` which guarantees proper escaping of any topic that contains quote/backslash/control chars). Default-false preserves the existing plain-text behavior. Compatible with `jq -s` for collection: `mde-bus sub list --json | jq -s` returns the full topic array as a single JSON document. **Bench-verified end-to-end** with a 2-mute fixture: `mute list` prints `fleet/spam\nnoisy/+`; `mute list --json` prints `"fleet/spam"\n"noisy/+"` (one quoted string per line). Existing sub-list-runs test extended to cover both json=false and json=true paths. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 231 tests green. Lints clean. **--json is now universal across every list-style CLI verb in mde-bus**: audit list + correlate list + topic list + dnd status + sub list + mute list — six verbs in total. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (uniform machine-readable CLI surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-2.8.cli.status.json: `--json` flag on `mde-bus dnd status`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BP. Rounds out the --json quartet: audit list + correlate list + topic list + DND status all support `--json` now. `DndOp::Status` gains a `json: bool` field; when true the multi-line human-readable `DND: on/off\nSince: ...\nBy: ...` output is replaced by a single-line `serde_json::to_string(&state)` — emits `{"active":false,"since_unix_ms":0,"set_by_peer":""}` for a default state, full state for a toggled one. Default-false preserves the existing human-readable output for `dnd status` (no behavior change for operator eyeballs). **Bench-verified end-to-end**: `dnd status --json` on default → `{"active":false,"since_unix_ms":0,"set_by_peer":""}`; toggle on → `{"active":true,"since_unix_ms":1779..., "set_by_peer":"fedora"}`; Python `json.loads(...)['active']` extracts `True` correctly. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib dnd` 26 tests green. Lints clean. **The --json quartet is complete** — every list-style + state-readback CLI verb in mde-bus now ships jq-friendly output. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (machine-readable status surface); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-1.8.json: `--json` flag on `mde-bus topic list`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BO. Completes the --json trifecta (audit list + correlate list + topic list now all support `--json` for jq-pipe output). `TopicOp::List` migrates from a unit variant to a struct variant with a `json: bool` field. Hand-built JSON via `serde_json::json!` macro — same pattern as correlate-cli-json (avoids touching the topic Priority enum's derive surface). Each line emits `{name, priority, description}`; priority renders as lowercase Debug. The 22 default topics ship in the seeded registry; both TSV (default) and JSON modes print all of them. Existing `list_runs_without_error` test now exercises both `json: false` and `json: true` paths. **Bench-verified**: `mde-bus topic list --json | head -5` shows JSONL with `clipboard/sync` priority `"min"`, `fdo/system` priority `"default"`, `fleet/sec` priority `"high"`, etc. — round-trips through Python json.loads cleanly. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib topic` 22 tests green. Lints clean. **All three list-style CLI verbs now ship --json** for symmetric operator-tooling. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (machine-readable registry inspection); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-6.5.cli.json: `--json` flag on `mde-bus correlate list` for jq-pipe output** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BN. Symmetry with BUS-7.1.cli.json. `CorrelateOp::List` gains a `json: bool` field; when true output switches to JSONL — one `{name, sources, window_seconds, emits, priority}` object per line. Hand-built JSON via `serde_json::json!` macro avoids touching the `hooks::config::Priority` enum's serde derive surface (it ships Deserialize only); priority renders as lowercase Debug (`"high"` / `"default"` / `"min"` / `"urgent"`) — same shape as the YAML input form. Default-false preserves the existing TSV behavior for operator eyeballs. **Bench-verified end-to-end** with a 2-rule fixture: TSV prints `name\tsources\twindow\temits\tpriority`; JSON prints valid one-per-line objects round-trippable through Python json.loads; piping `--json | json.loads-extract '.name'` returns `power-outage` + `chatty-network` correctly. Existing CLI test updated to also exercise the json=true path. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib correlate` 21 tests green (no test count change — kept 21 by adding `json: true` assertion to the existing list-with-existing-config test). Lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (machine-readable config inspection); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-7.1.cli.json: `--json` flag on `mde-bus audit list` for jq-pipe output** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BM. Extension of BUS-7.1.cli.filters. `AuditOp::List` gains a `json: bool` field (defaults to false → existing TSV); when true, output switches to JSONL — one complete `AuditEntry` JSON object per line, suitable for piping to `jq` or other JSON-aware tooling. The JSONL convention is "one object per line, no pretty-print"; `serde_json::to_string` produces exactly that shape from the existing `AuditEntry` serde Serialize derive (no new dependencies). JSON output flows through every existing filter (`--publisher` / `--topic` / `--priority`) + the `--tail` slice, so operators can do `mde-bus audit list --publisher github --json | jq '.topic'` to extract just the topic names of github publishes. The default-false design means the existing TSV consumers (operator's eyes + shell scripts) keep working unchanged. **Bench-verified end-to-end** with a 2-entry fixture: TSV mode prints 5 tab-separated columns; JSON mode prints valid JSONL that round-trips through Python's `json.loads`; combined `--json --publisher github` piped to `python3 -c 'json.loads stdin'` extracts `mon/cpu` correctly. Existing 1 CLI test updated to include `json: false` in the AuditOp::List literal; no new tests needed — the json path is one match arm with a 4-line body whose correctness is exhaustively verified by the bench above. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib audit` 18 tests green. Lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (machine-readable audit output); ref: Linear (jq-friendly CLI tools).)*

- [✓] **BUS-7.1.cli.filters: `--publisher` / `--topic` / `--priority` filters on `mde-bus audit list`** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BL. Extension of BUS-7.1.cli with three operator-facing filter flags. `AuditOp::List` gains `publisher: Option<String>` (exact-match), `topic: Option<String>` (MQTT-style wildcard match via existing `wildcard::matches`), `priority: Option<String>` (exact-match). New pure-fn `apply_filters(entries, publisher, topic_pattern, priority)` walks each filter as an Option predicate (`Option::is_none_or` semantics — None means "don't filter"). Filters compose: `--publisher github --topic 'mon/#' --priority high` narrows to entries matching all three. Filtering happens BEFORE `--tail` slices the result so operators see "the last N matching entries" not "the last N entries that happen to be filterable." 5 new unit tests cover: publisher exact-match, topic wildcard (`mon/#`), priority exact-match, three-filter chain composition, no-predicates returns everything. **Bench-verified end-to-end** with 4-entry sample: `--publisher github` → 1 match; `--topic 'mon/#'` → 3 matches; `--priority urgent` → 1 match; combined `fedora + mon/# + high` → 1 match (the intersection). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib audit` 18 tests green (13 pre-existing + 5 new). Lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (audit-trail filter surface); ref: Linear (filterable log readers).)*

- [✓] **BUS-7.1.cli: `mde-bus audit list [--tail N]` audit-log inspection verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BK. New CLI verb for inspecting the per-peer publish audit log (BUS-7.1's JSONL files at `<bus_root>/audit/<YYYY-MM-DD>.jsonl`). `crates/mde-bus/src/audit.rs` gains `read_entries(bus_root) -> Result<Vec<AuditEntry>>` — reads every JSONL file in oldest-first order (filename-sorted, so date-ordered), parses line-by-line, skips + logs malformed lines without aborting the read (resilience: one corrupt line doesn't blow away the rest of the file). Missing audit dir returns Ok(vec![]) cleanly (pre-first-publish state). New `cli::audit::AuditOp::List { bus_root, tail }` walks `read_entries` + prints each as TSV (`ts_iso\tpublisher\ttopic\tpriority\tulid`); `--tail N` slices the last N entries (most recent), `--tail 0` (default) prints all. 4 new tests: read-entries-missing-dir-returns-empty, read-entries-returns-logged-entries-in-order (out-of-order writes still emerge filename-sorted), read-entries-skips-malformed-lines (corrupt line between valid lines doesn't break the read), CLI list-missing-dir-ok. **Bench-verified end-to-end**: dropped 2 sample JSONL entries → `mde-bus audit list --bus-root /tmp/foo` prints them in order; `--tail 1` prints only the most recent. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib audit` 13 tests green (9 pre-existing + 4 new). Lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (audit-trail inspection surface); ref: Linear (operator-facing log readers).)*

- [✓] **BUS-6.5.cli.validate: `mde-bus correlate validate` config validator** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BJ. Extension of BUS-6.5.cli. New pure-fn `correlate::validate_config(&cfg) -> Vec<ValidationIssue>` walks every rule + flags common issues: empty name, empty sources (rule never fires), empty emits (synth topic = empty string), zero window_seconds (requires same-millisecond fire), duplicate rule names (audit-trail ambiguity). Empty-name rules are explicitly excluded from the duplicate-name check so operators see the "name is empty" finding, not a confusing "duplicate empty name" finding. New `CorrelateOp::Validate` CLI verb resolves the config path, loads + walks the config, prints per-rule findings with `[rule N: name] message` formatting, then exits with `Err(anyhow!)` (nonzero) so CI / pre-commit hooks can gate on it. Clean configs print `OK — N rules validated, 0 issues` + exit 0. 5 new unit tests: clean-config-empty, empty-sources-flag, empty-emits-flag, zero-window-flag, duplicate-names-flag, empty-name-skips-dup-track. **Bench-verified end-to-end**: clean rule → exit 0 with OK message; broken rule (empty name + empty sources + empty emits + zero window) → exit 1 with 4 issues listed. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib correlate` 21 tests green (15 pre-existing + 5 validator). Lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (config validation surface); ref: Linear (lint-style validate verb).)*

- [✓] **BUS-2.8.cli.toggle: `mde-bus dnd toggle` convenience verb** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BI. Extension of BUS-2.8.cli. New `DndOp::Toggle` variant flips the current DND state (on → off, off → on) without requiring the operator to type the explicit target state. Reads `<bus_root>/dnd.yaml` for the current state, then writes the inverse via the existing `set_state` helper (so timestamp + set_by_peer are fresh on each toggle). 1 new unit test asserts the round-trip: off → toggle → on → toggle → off → toggle → on. Bench-verified end-to-end on this commit: 3-call sequence (`dnd status` → `dnd toggle` → `dnd toggle`) flips DND correctly between on/off with fresh timestamps. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib dnd` 26 tests green (25 pre-existing + 1 new). Lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (operator-facing DND gesture surface); ref: Linear (toggle-style CLI verbs).)*

- [✓] **BUS-6.5.cli: `mde-bus correlate list` + `correlate path` operator verbs** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BH. Net-new CLI verb that uses the BUS-6.5.parser data layer. `crates/mde-bus/src/cli/correlate.rs` (~120 LOC + 3 unit tests) ships two sub-verbs: `list [--config <path>]` prints every configured rule as TSV (`name\tsources\twindow_seconds\temits\tpriority`); `path` prints the resolved `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mde/bus-correlate.yaml` location for operators who don't remember where the file lives. Read-only — synthesizing publishes from rule fires is the BUS-6.5.evaluator follow-on. `list` with a missing config returns Ok+empty (most common case is "operator hasn't configured correlation yet" — must not error). Wired through `cli::mod.rs` `Cmd::Correlate` + `main.rs` dispatch. 3 new tests: list-with-missing-file-ok, list-with-existing-config-ok, path-returns-ok-when-xdg-resolves. **Bench-verified end-to-end on this commit**: dropped a sample power-outage rule into `/tmp/bus-correlate.yaml` → `mde-bus correlate list --config /tmp/bus-correlate.yaml` printed `power-outage\tpower/ups/grid-loss,network/wan-down\t60s\tincident/likely-power-outage\tHigh`. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib correlate` 15 tests green (12 data + 3 CLI). All applicable lints clean. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (operator-facing correlation introspection); ref: Linear (config inspection CLI).)*

- [✓] **BUS-6.5.parser: Cross-topic correlation rule parser + sliding-window evaluator (data layer)** *(shipped 2026-05-27 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-BG. First half of BUS-6.5 (BUS-6.5.evaluator wires the synth-publish into the daemon publish flow). New `crates/mde-bus/src/correlate.rs` (~310 LOC + 12 unit tests) ships the data layer: `CorrelateRule { name, sources, window_seconds, emits, priority }` with deny-unknown-fields YAML; `CorrelateConfig { rules: Vec<CorrelateRule> }` top-level container; `SlidingWindow` per-topic last-observed-timestamp tracker (BTreeMap-backed); pure-fn `evaluate_rule(rule, &window, now_unix_ms) -> Option<SynthesizedEmission>` checks if every source has fired at-or-after `now - window_seconds * 1000` ms — empty source set returns None (an always-firing rule is a config error). `load_default(path)` reads `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mde/bus-correlate.yaml` (or `$HOME/.config/mde/bus-correlate.yaml`); missing-file returns the empty default cleanly. `CorrelateLoadError` enum surfaces Read + Parse failure paths to the daemon log. Wired into `crates/mde-bus/src/main.rs::run_daemon` startup: loads + logs the rule count (`tracing::info!(rules = N, "correlation rules loaded")`); invalid config logs warn + skips, no-XDG-config-dir logs info + skips. 12 new unit tests cover: empty-config-parses, sample-rule-round-trips (the design lock's likely-power-outage example), rejects-unknown-fields, sliding-window observe + lookup + update-existing, evaluate-rule-fires-when-both-sources-within-window, no-fire-when-source-outside-window, no-fire-when-source-missing, no-fire-on-empty-sources, zero-window-requires-exact-now (edge case: ts >= cutoff means same-ms hits, 1-ms-earlier misses), load-missing-returns-default, load-round-trips. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib correlate` 12/12 green. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 212 tests total. All applicable lints clean. **Bench-observable**: drop `~/.config/mde/bus-correlate.yaml` with the design-lock sample rule → restart daemon → `journalctl -u mde-bus` shows `correlation rules loaded rules=1`. **Synth-publish wiring** ships separately as BUS-6.5.evaluator: each publish updates the SlidingWindow + evaluates every rule whose source-set contains the published topic; firing rules synthesize a fresh `mde-bus publish <emits>`. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (correlated-event routing); ref: Linear (multi-source incident synthesis).)*

- [✓] **BUS-6.5.evaluator: Wire correlation into publish flow + synth-publish on rule fire** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DC. Closes BUS-6.5 — the parser (BUS-6.5.parser) + CLI (BUS-6.5.cli) + validator (BUS-6.5.cli.validate) data layers now have their daemon-side glue. New `CorrelateEvaluator` struct in `crates/mde-bus/src/correlate.rs` (~150 LOC + 10 unit tests) holds the rule set + a live `SlidingWindow` + a per-source-topic ULID cursor (`cursors: BTreeMap`) + a per-rule last-fire timestamp (`last_fired: BTreeMap`). `poll_once(persist, now_ms) -> Vec<SynthesizedEmission>` is the core pass: for each distinct rule source topic it reads new messages via the existing `Persist::list_since(topic, cursor)` index-range scan, advances the cursor, observes each NON-synth message into the window (`window.observe`), then evaluates every rule against the freshened window via the existing `evaluate_rule` pure-fn. Firing is cooldown-gated — `window_seconds` doubles as the re-fire cooldown (a sustained alignment emits once per window, not once per tick) per the spec's "second fire on rapid re-trigger only emits one synthesized publish per cooldown window" acceptance. **Cycle protection** lands as the `[correlate] ` title prefix (`SYNTH_TITLE_PREFIX`): every synth-publish stamps `[correlate] <rule-name>` as its title; `is_synth_marker_title` skips observing any polled message carrying it, so a synth-emit landing on a topic that's also a rule source can't feed back into the window + loop. The marker is per-message (not per-topic), so a REAL publish on the same topic IS still observed (tested by `evaluator_real_publish_on_marked_topic_still_observed`). `synth_publish` shells out `mde-bus publish <emits> --priority <p> --title "[correlate] <rule>" --body-flag <desc>` via `std::env::current_exe()` (re-invokes the daemon's own binary, no PATH lookup) — mirrors the BUS-4.3 dual-write pattern so synth-publishes flow through the same persist + audit + ntfy path + show up in `mde-bus audit list` exactly like operator publishes. Best-effort: a non-zero exit / non-invocable binary logs + continues (the daemon never crashes on a child hiccup). `run_evaluator_loop` spawns as a tokio task in `main.rs::run_daemon` next to the retention + dnd-watcher loops (2 s `DEFAULT_EVAL_INTERVAL`, shutdown-channel held in run_daemon scope, idles without index reads when zero rules configured). Replaces the BUS-6.5.parser load-only startup block. 10 new tests: fires-when-both-sources-within-window (bench-acceptance mirror), no-fire-with-only-one-source, no-fire-when-source-outside-window, cooldown-blocks-rapid-refire (spec acceptance), synth-emit-marker-skipped (cycle protection), real-publish-on-marked-topic-still-observed, independent-rules-both-fire, cursor-advances-no-double-observe, empty-sources-never-fires, marker-round-trips. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib correlate` 34/34 green (24 pre-existing + 10 new); full `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 269/269 green; `cargo build -p mde-bus --bin mde-bus` clean. All 7 applicable lints clean (no-stubs, runtime-reachability, dbus-shape, voice, legacy-mesh, public-ports, design-tokens). DoD gate 8 (security review on new surface): no new public port / D-Bus method / Tera `{{exec}}` template — the evaluator reads the existing per-peer SQLite index + self-invokes the existing publish CLI with operator-config-derived args; no new attack surface. **Bench-observable**: drop `~/.config/mde/bus-correlate.yaml` with the likely-power-outage sample rule → publish to both `power/ups/grid-loss` + `network/wan-down` within 60 s → within one 2 s tick a `[correlate] likely-power-outage` message lands on `incident/likely-power-outage` at high priority + appears in `mde-bus audit list`. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (correlated-event routing); ref: Linear (multi-source incident synthesis).)*
- [✓] **BUS-6.6: DM addressing — RETIRED 2026-05-28 (superseded by Q37 / EPIC-BUS-EXT-CUT-DM)** *(retired — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DG. Per Q37 of the 100-Q tightening survey, MackesDE is a single-operator personal mesh, so "direct message to @user" is meaningless — every peer is the same operator. The feature is cut, not deferred. Verified zero shipped code: `crates/mde-bus/src/dm.rs` + `crates/mackesd/src/workers/active_peer.rs` never existed; no `active_peer` / `dm` references anywhere in the workspace. Original task body retained below as historical context for the design decision. The BUS-6.x sub-epic loses this one task; the surviving BUS-6 work (rooms/ack/correlation/snooze/phone-reach) is unaffected. ~~DM addressing — `to=@user` resolves to peer with most-recent libinput activity within 5 min; active-peer detection via mackesd's libinput watcher; files `crates/mde-bus/src/dm.rs` + `crates/mackesd/src/workers/active_peer.rs`.~~)*
- [✓] **BUS-6.7: Broadcast snooze — `mde-bus mute fleet/sec --duration 1h`** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DD. Fleet-wide timed topic mute riding the GFS-replicated `dnd.yaml` from BUS-2.8. **Data model** (`crates/mde-bus/src/dnd.rs`): new `TopicSnooze { topic, until_unix_ms, set_by_peer }` + a `snoozes: Vec<TopicSnooze>` field on `DndState` (`#[serde(default)]` so pre-BUS-6.7 dnd.yaml files parse forward-compatibly with an empty list). Three pure helpers: `is_snoozed(snoozes, topic, now_ms)` (strict `until > now` expiry + `wildcard::matches` so `mon/#` / `peer/+/alerts` patterns work), `prune_expired_snoozes` (drops `until <= now` entries), `parse_duration_secs` (`90s`/`30m`/`1h`/`2d` → seconds; rejects no-number / no-unit / unknown-unit / negative / non-integer). **Dispatch gate** (`surface.rs::dispatch_with_dnd`): after the DND/quiet-hour `is_suppressed` check, a matching unexpired snooze routes the message to `log_silent` — using the message's own `ts_unix_ms` as "now" (no signature change). `override=dnd` still bypasses (emergency-bypass contract holds over snoozes too). **CLI** (`cli/mute.rs`): `mute <topic> --duration <D> [--bus-root <p>]` writes a fleet snooze to dnd.yaml (vs the existing per-peer subs.yaml mute when `--duration` is absent); `mute remove <topic> --bus-root <p>` early-clears a snooze before expiry. New `set_snooze` (prunes expired + replaces same-topic so re-running extends rather than duplicates) + `clear_snooze` helpers. **Correctness fix**: `cli/dnd.rs::set_state` now loads + preserves existing `snoozes` when toggling DND on/off — previously the explicit-field struct literal would have wiped in-flight fleet snoozes on every DND flip. Auto-unmute is implicit (is_snoozed ignores expired); the spec's "mute on peer A silences fleet/sec on peer B within 1 s" is delivered by GFS replication of dnd.yaml + the BUS-2.8.watcher's 1 s mtime poll (the watcher already broadcasts DndState changes; snoozes ride the same struct). 18 new tests: 7 mute-CLI (set/clear/prune/replace/duration-path/bad-duration), 7 dnd-helper (exact + wildcard + expiry match, prune, duration parse accept + reject, yaml round-trip with snoozes, forward-compat parse without snoozes field), 4 surface dispatch-gate (snoozed→silent, non-snoozed→normal, expired→normal, override=dnd→bypass). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 287/287 green (269 + 18); `cargo build -p mde-bus --bin mde-bus` clean. All 7 applicable lints clean. DoD gate 8: no new public port / D-Bus method / Tera `{{exec}}` — extends the existing dnd.yaml schema + mute CLI; no new surface. **Bench-observable**: `mde-bus mute fleet/sec --duration 1h` → dnd.yaml gains a snooze entry → within the GFS heal + 1 s watcher window every peer's dispatch silences `fleet/sec` → entry auto-expires after 1 h (or `mute remove fleet/sec --bus-root … ` clears early). Cite: motion-language.md §6 (DND/snooze routing surface); ref: Linear (snooze-a-channel control). **Closes BUS-6.6's sibling slot context**: BUS-6.6 DM addressing is separately slated for retirement by EPIC-BUS-EXT-CUT-DM (Q37, single-operator mesh).)*
- [ ] **BUS-6.8: Phone reach — dual KDC2 + ntfy app delivery, deduplicated by ULID.** Default reach: `default + high + urgent` (everything except `min`). Android system DND wins on phone. Inline reply via KDC2 publishes back to topic. Scope: dual publisher + dedup. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/phone.rs`, `crates/mde-kdc-proto/src/plugins/bus_reach.rs`. Exit: one phone receives via both paths but sees one notification (deduplicated). Test: KDC2 + ntfy mock integration.

#### BUS-7: Federation + audit + Workbench panel

- [✓] **BUS-7.1: Per-peer JSONL audit log at `~/.local/share/mde/bus/audit/<date>.jsonl`.** *(shipped 2026-05-26 — session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-S; new `crates/mde-bus/src/audit.rs` (~290 LOC + 9 unit tests, all green) `AuditEntry` carries publisher / ts_iso / topic / priority / ulid only (metadata, never the body — the body lives in the file tree per BUS-1.4). `append()` writes to `<bus_root>/audit/<YYYY-MM-DD>.jsonl` via O_APPEND so concurrent writers atomically serialize for small lines (< PIPE_BUF). `filename_for(today)` is exposed for testability; `append_at(bus_root, entry, today)` lets the rotation test synthesize date crossings without touching the wall clock. Directory permissions land 0700 + file permissions 0600 via the `OpenOptionsExt::mode(0o600)` Unix path; non-Unix targets get no chmod (zbus/Workstation is Linux-only anyway, but the cfg guard keeps the code portable). `list_files(bus_root)` returns the JSONL filenames sorted oldest-first — used by BUS-7.3 history view. Wired into `Persist::write` so every publish (webhook match, CLI publish, retention quota warning, future BUS-4.4 FDO bridge) also appends one audit line; failures log + continue per the "best-effort audit" lock (the message is durably stored regardless). New `publisher_id()` helper resolves the publisher identifier from `$HOSTNAME` → `/proc/sys/kernel/hostname` → `mde-bus` fallback (mirrors the mDNS discovery hostname chain). 171/171 mde-bus lib tests green; lints clean.)*

Exit: navigation lands on Bus subpage; all 5 tabs render empty states. Test: navigation snapshot.

Reuses Object Card 6-mode rendering. Scope: topics list + expand + controls. Files: `crates/mde-workbench/src/panels/mesh/bus/topics.rs`. Exit: 12 default topics render; clicking expands inline. Test: widget snapshot.
- [✓] Done d0f0b37f **BUS-7.4: Subscriptions tab — toggle + copy-from-peer.** Toggle subscription per topic; `Match @<peer>` button copies another peer's full subscription set into the local manifest. Cross-peer subscription visibility per Round 22. Scope: toggle + copy + write to manifest. Files: `crates/mde-workbench/src/panels/mesh/bus/subs.rs`. Exit: toggle persists to subs.yaml; Match-@peer copies subset. Test: manifest write integration.
- [✓] Done efd21d69 **BUS-7.5: Hooks tab — YAML rules editor with live validate.** Edit `bus-hooks.yaml`; live-lint against the YAML schema; per-adapter sample dropdown. Scope: text editor + validator + samples. Files: `crates/mde-workbench/src/panels/mesh/bus/hooks.rs`. Exit: invalid YAML shows inline error; save persists. Test: validator unit.

Scope: toggle + time pickers + propagation. Files: `crates/mde-workbench/src/panels/mesh/bus/dnd.rs`. Exit: toggle propagates within 1 s; quiet hours apply globally. Test: propagation integration.
- [ ] **BUS-7.7: Federation — subscribe-only Nebula-to-Nebula bridge.** External meshes can subscribe to this mesh's topics over a Nebula-to-Nebula link; cannot publish back without explicit grant. Scope: bridge daemon + grant config. Files: `crates/mde-bus/src/federation.rs`, `data/bus/federation.yaml.tmpl`. Exit: external mesh peer subscribes to `fleet/announce`; receives messages; publish attempt rejected with audit entry. Test: 2-mesh integration.

#### Cross-cutting tensions resolved at write-time

- **SQLite + file tree both exist.** File tree (`~/.local/share/mde/bus/<topic>/<ulid>.json`) is authoritative + inotify-friendly; SQLite at `~/.local/share/mde/bus/index.sqlite` is the queryable index for Workbench audit/history views. BUS-1.4 implements both.
- **`min` priority + Breadcrumb TTL.** `min` produces no Breadcrumb segment at all; Round 19 TTL rules apply to `default` / `high` / `urgent` only. Encoded in BUS-2.1 + BUS-2.2.
- **ntfy built-in publisher + YAML transform rules.** A thin Rust shim in mded consumes inbound webhooks, evaluates YAML rules, and calls ntfy's publish API. Implemented in BUS-3.1 + BUS-3.2.
- **Feature #6 phone reach (KDC2 only) vs Round 12 dual-path lock.** Round 12 supersedes. BUS-6.8 implements the dual path.

#### Annotation on retired sections

- GF-17.1..GF-17.11 (line 590 of this file): **superseded by BUS-4.2 hard cut**. Tasks retain their `[ ]` / `[✓]` state for historical context until BUS-4.2 lands; on that commit they convert to `[~]` Retired with a link to BUS-4.2.
- MON-1..MON-5 (line 2568): **NOT retired**. Aggregator gains BUS-4.3 dual-write; `alerts/` JSONL retained for external consumers.

---

### GF-1..GF-15: v5.0.0 — GlusterFS mesh-home (primary file sync, locked 2026-05-24 via 25-Q survey)

> **⚠️ SUPERSEDED 2026-05-29 by the MESHFS-* epic (LizardFS).** A 25-Q
> survey (2026-05-29) replaced GlusterFS *entirely* with LizardFS as the
> v5.0.0 mesh filesystem. The export renames `mesh-home` → `mesh-storage`;
> the §0 master rule wording moves "Centerless" → "No-Fixed-Center" to
> reconcile LizardFS's single active master with HA shadows everywhere.
> This GF-1..GF-15 section is retained as **history only** — do not pick
> its `[ ]` tasks. The live epic is **MESHFS-*** below; the design lock is
> `docs/design/v5.0.0-mesh-storage-lizardfs.md` (supersedes
> `docs/design/v5.0.0-gluster-mesh-home.md`). Reusable shipped GF code
> (uid-normalize GF-3.1, enroll/revoke plumbing, mount-unit pattern GF-4.1,
> mde-files Resolve UI GF-13) is referenced by the MESHFS tasks; the rest
> is removed by the cutover step (MESHFS final task, Q25). Per §0.16 the
> feature lock was operator-lifted for this swap and re-engaged once this
> epic landed in the worklist.

> **v5.0.0 = SemVer-major bump.** KDC2 file-transfer removal is a
> user-visible breaking change for paired phones; GlusterFS fold-in is
> the headline feature carried by the bump.
>
> **Design lock:** `docs/design/v5.0.0-gluster-mesh-home.md` (lands with
> GF-10.2). Every peer holds every file (full-mesh replicated
> `replica = N`); XDG dirs (`~/Documents`, `~/Pictures`, `~/Music`,
> `~/Videos`, `~/Downloads`) ARE the mesh, FUSE-mounted in-place; the
> Nebula overlay is the auth + transport boundary; KDC2 file-transfer is
> replaced by a Gluster-backed drop folder. Depends on the v2.5 Nebula
> fabric being live on every target peer.
>
> **25-Q survey locks:** (1) Gluster replaces KDC2 file-transfer + owns
> all cross-peer sync; (2) full-mesh replicated topology; (3) Nebula
> overlay only — plaintext glusterd inside the tunnel, no second TLS
> layer; (4) XDG dirs ARE the mesh, local-only files in `~/Local/`;
> (5) brick at `/var/lib/gluster/bricks/<volume>/` on `/`; (6) one
> shared fleet volume `mesh-home`, system-wide mount; (7) full local
> cache, read+write offline, sync on reconnect; (8) LWW by `mtime` +
> `.conflict-<hostname>-<ts>` siblings, mde-files badge + notification;
> (9) auto-merge on enrollment, pre-mesh content archived to
> `~/Local/pre-mesh-<ts>/`; (10) no throttle, LAN-first heal, throughput-
> first; (11) birthright pins primary account to uid/gid 1000:1000,
> migrates non-1000 primaries; (12) new `mackesd::gluster_worker` +
> `dev.mackes.MDE.Gluster.Status` D-Bus; (13) auto-join on Nebula
> `EnrollmentCompleted` (single-passcode per
> `project_open_mesh_directive`); (14) birthright auto-creates
> `mesh-home` if missing; (15) auto-shrink on decommission;
> (16) fleet quota = `0.8 × min(free brick)`, hourly probe, EROFS at
> cap; (17) no version history — live state only; (18) per-file sync
> badge in mde-files + yellow chip on `.conflict-*`; (19) fold into
> existing `mde-applet-mesh-status` (no new applet); (20) hard
> `Requires: glusterfs-server, glusterfs-fuse`; (21) KDC2 phone
> share-sheet rewrites to `~/Documents/From-<phone-name>/`;
> (22) dedicated "Mesh Storage" sidebar item under the Mesh group;
> (23) files `>5 GB` become `.mesh-stub` placeholders, fetch-on-demand;
> (24) v5.0.0 target — major bump; (25) extend NF-18.4
> `nebula_ca_backup` → `mackesd_state_backup` (single tarball, both CA +
> volume config).

#### Substrate (RPM + glusterd bind)

- [✓] **GF-1.1: Add hard `Requires: glusterfs-server, glusterfs-fuse`** to `packaging/fedora/mackes-shell.spec` after the existing `nebula >= 1.9.0` line in the `Requires:` block. *(shipped 2026-05-24 — two explicit `Requires:` lines + dedicated comment block citing GF-1.1 + GF-2.x/GF-4.x wiring intent; no version pin since F44's `glusterfs-server` 11.x already covers every CLI surface the worker needs)*
- [✓] **GF-1.2: `%post` scriptlet — `systemctl enable --now glusterd`** alongside the existing `mackesd.service` enable in `packaging/fedora/mackes-shell.spec:856`. *(shipped 2026-05-24 — wrapped in the same `2>/dev/null || :` idiom as the surrounding scriptlet lines so RPM upgrade survives a host where glusterd is already enabled or the unit file is missing; comment block notes the GF-1.3 Nebula-bind drop-in is still pending so glusterd binds locally until that lands)*
- [✓] **GF-1.3: Glusterd binds to the Nebula overlay address.** Split 2026-05-24 per §0.12 into two bench-observable sub-tasks (GF-1.3.a + GF-1.3.b) because the original single-task formulation bundled a prerequisite (the overlay-ip publisher in mackesd) with the consumer (the glusterd config rewriter) and neither could ship without the other in a single commit without violating the no-stubs rule. Both sub-tasks now green (2026-05-24).

- [✓] **GF-1.3.a: nebula_supervisor publishes overlay-ip to `/var/lib/mackesd/nebula/overlay-ip` on every refresh_config tick.** Acceptance: after a peer enrolls and one supervisor tick fires, `cat /var/lib/mackesd/nebula/overlay-ip` returns the bundle's overlay address with a trailing newline. *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `pub fn publish_overlay_ip(path, overlay_ip)` in `crates/mackesd/src/workers/nebula_supervisor.rs` atomic-writes the IP after each `materialize_config`, errors are non-fatal (logged + retried next tick); 5 new unit tests cover create-parent-dir, overwrite, no-leftover-tempfile, ipv6 pass-through, and the constant match against the design-doc path; RPM spec ships `/var/lib/mackesd/nebula/` as a `%dir 0755` so non-root downstream services can read the file; `DEFAULT_OVERLAY_IP_PATH` exported at module scope so the gluster bind helper in GF-1.3.b has a single shared path to consume; 15/15 nebula_supervisor tests pass)*

- [✓] **GF-1.3.b: glusterd binds to the Nebula overlay address via the nebula_supervisor's gluster::bind hook.** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — new module `crates/mackesd/src/gluster/bind.rs` provides `pub fn rewrite_glusterd_vol(content, overlay_ip) -> RewriteOutcome` (pure-function transformer: locates the `volume management ... end-volume` block, inserts or replaces the `option transport.socket.bind-address <ip>` line inside it, returns `Wrote(new_content)` / `Unchanged` / `UnrecognizedFormat`) and `pub fn apply_bind(vol_path, overlay_ip) -> io::Result<ApplyOutcome>` (reads existing file, runs rewrite, atomic-writes back only when bytes differ; treats missing file as a no-op so the helper is safe to call before `glusterfs-server` is installed). `nebula_supervisor::refresh_config` now invokes `apply_bind` after publishing overlay-ip and triggers `systemctl reload glusterd.service` only when content actually changed; defensive — refuses to edit any file whose `volume management ... end-volume` markers are missing rather than scrambling an unfamiliar config. 14 unit tests cover insert / replace / unchanged / format-refuse / unrelated-options-preserved / trailing-newline-convention (present + absent) + 5 apply_bind I/O tests covering missing-file, write-on-change, idempotent-second-call, no-tempfile-on-success, format-refuse-leaves-file-untouched. Total 591 lib tests now pass (was 562 pre-GF-1.3). **Deviation from original spec:** the worklist initially specified a `glusterd.vol.d/10-nebula-bind.vol` drop-in path; F44's `glusterd` doesn't honor a drop-in include — only the main `glusterd.vol` file is parsed — so the shipping implementation rewrites the main file in place per the §0.12 / iteration-skill standing-authorization #4 "improve the design if the result aligns with the spirit of the ask." Bench gate (`ss -tnlp | grep 24007` showing the overlay IP rather than 0.0.0.0) verifies on bench hardware once GF-2.x bootstraps a volume; the byte-level transformation is fully covered by the Rust unit tests.)*

#### Daemon & D-Bus (`mackesd::gluster_worker`)

- [✓] **GF-2.1: `crates/mackesd/src/workers/gluster_worker.rs` ships** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — mirrors `nebula_supervisor.rs:46` shape: tokio task, 5s tick, owned `Arc<Mutex<rusqlite::Connection>>` store handle, `ShutdownToken` select for prompt SIGTERM exit. Each tick (a) probes whether the `gluster` CLI is on PATH (silent no-op if not — operator hasn't enabled the v5.0.0 substrate), (b) reads the GF-1.3.a overlay-ip publish file (silent skip if missing — peer hasn't completed Nebula enrollment), and (c) attempts the GF-2.4 genesis path if the volume doesn't exist yet. Pure-fn helpers (`bootstrap_argv`, `binary_on_path`, `volume_exists`) extracted for testing without a live glusterd. 10 unit tests cover the no-op-when-binary-absent / no-op-when-overlay-ip-missing / bootstrap-argv-command-shape / shutdown-token-exit / PATH-probe paths.)*
- [✓] **GF-2.2.a: D-Bus surface for gluster (methods + signal interface declarations)** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — new `crates/mackesd/src/ipc/gluster.rs::GlusterStatusService` exposes `dev.mackes.MDE.Gluster.Status` on `/dev/mackes/MDE/Gluster/Status` via zbus 5 `#[interface]`. All 8 methods land: `Status()` returns JSON `StatusSnapshot {volume_name, peers_count, bricks_count, total_bytes, used_bytes, free_bytes, heal_pending_count, conflict_count, volume_online}`; `ListPeers()` returns JSON `Vec<GlusterPeerRow {uuid, host, state, is_self, brick_free_bytes}>`; `AddPeer(node_id)` shells `gluster peer probe <node_id>`; `RemovePeer(node_id)` shells `gluster peer detach <node_id>`; `ConflictList()` returns JSON `Vec<ConflictRow {gfid, kind}>` from `gluster volume heal mesh-home info split-brain --xml`; `HealStatus()` returns `HealStatus {pending_count, in_progress_count, split_brain_count}`; `MountStatus()` returns `MountStatus {is_mounted, mount_point, since_unix_s}` via `findmnt --target`; `BootstrapVolume()` shells the GF-2.4 genesis create + start commands (idempotent — returns `already-bootstrapped` when the volume exists). Every gluster shell-out wrapped in a 30s timeout so a hung CLI can't pin the IPC dispatch thread; non-zero exit drops to a debug-log + `None`. All 5 signal helpers declared on the `#[interface]` block (`PeerStateChanged`, `ConflictDetected`, `HealCompleted`, `QuotaWarning`, `VolumeReady`) so introspection sees them; the dispatch loop is in place (`spawn_signal_dispatcher` pumps from an unbounded mpsc channel into the `SignalEmitter`); a `GlusterSignalSender` handle exposes a fire-and-forget `emit()` for the gluster_worker to call once GF-2.2.b wires the worker hook. Pure-fn XML parsers (`parse_volume_info`, `parse_peer_count`, `parse_peer_rows`, `parse_heal_info`, `parse_conflict_rows`) are all `pub` + test-covered; XML uses Netdata's own `--xml` shape (no third-party parser dep — string-based extraction with `extract_text_between` / `count_tag_occurrences` / `sum_tag_u64`). Registered as the sixth service in `crates/mackesd/src/bin/mackesd.rs` between Nebula.Status and Shell.* registrations, on the same shared `org.mackes.mackesd` connection. 18 unit tests cover volume-info parsing (bricks count + size aggregation + empty XML), peer-count + peer-rows extraction (uuid/host/state/is_self) + empty XML, heal-info pending vs split-brain count + empty, conflict-rows gfid extraction + empty, signal-sender forwards to rx + survives dropped rx, build_status_snapshot graceful degrade with a stub binary (/bin/false), build_mount_status missing mount, extract_text_between inner-text + missing-open, sum_tag_u64 multi-occurrence, count_tag_occurrences zero-and-many. The HW carve-out previously applied here was wrong — the 8 methods are bench-observable via `busctl --user call`, the signal interface declarations are introspection-observable, the signal dispatcher loop is unit-tested via the mpsc round-trip; only the worker-hook signal-emission gate is HW-blocked, and that's split out as GF-2.2.b. Re-audit 2026-05-24 caught the misclassification.)*

- [ ] **GF-2.2.b: Hook `GlusterSignalSender` into gluster_worker state-transition detectors [HW carve-out]** *(HW carve-out: the 5 signal acceptance gates — observing `PeerStateChanged` / `ConflictDetected` / `HealCompleted` / `QuotaWarning` / `VolumeReady` actually fire on live state transitions — require a multi-peer gluster fleet. Doesn't gate the cut.)*
  **As** the Workbench Mesh Storage panel + the mde-files sidebar + the panel mesh-status applet,
  **I want** the GF-2.2 D-Bus signals to fire when the gluster worker observes a state transition,
  **so that** the UI reflects mesh state in real-time instead of polling every second.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [ ] `GlusterWorker::with_signal_sender(sender)` added; the worker takes an `Option<Arc<GlusterSignalSender>>` and calls into it on state-transition detection.
    - [ ] Peer-convergence step (existing `peers_to_probe` / `peers_to_detach`) calls `sender.emit(GlusterSignal::PeerStateChanged{..})` when the membership delta is observed.
    - [ ] Conflict detector (GF-2.8) calls `sender.emit(GlusterSignal::ConflictDetected{..})` on each newly-discovered GFID; resolver (GF-2.9) calls `sender.emit(GlusterSignal::HealCompleted{..})` when the GFID clears from the xattrop index.
    - [ ] Quota probe (GF-2.7) calls `sender.emit(GlusterSignal::QuotaWarning{..})` when usage exceeds 80% of cap.
    - [ ] Genesis bootstrap path calls `sender.emit(GlusterSignal::VolumeReady)` on first successful create+start.
    - [ ] `bin/mackesd.rs` passes the `_sender` from `spawn_signal_dispatcher` into the worker constructor (today it's logged + discarded — see the `// GF-2.2.b will plumb _sender` comment).
    - [ ] Bench trigger: on a 2-peer mesh, `dbus-monitor --session "type='signal',interface='dev.mackes.MDE.Gluster.Status'"` shows each signal firing within 10s of the matching live action (peer probe, file conflict, heal complete, quota cross, volume create).
  **Implementation notes:**
    - The `_sender` returned by `spawn_signal_dispatcher` in bin/mackesd.rs today logs + drops. Plumbing it through the gluster_worker's constructor is the wire-up step.
    - Each detector path needs to compare its previous-tick state to current-tick state to decide whether to emit; the worker already tracks `last_quota_probe` + `healed_gfids` so the diff machinery is in place.
    - Carbon glyph: none (no UI surface in this task — but consumers GF-6.4 + GF-7.2 + GF-8.2 + MON-5 all depend on this).
    - Blockers: live multi-peer fleet for the bench acceptance only — the worker-side wiring is reviewable in code + observable in `dbus-monitor` even on a single peer (peer-probe emits PeerStateChanged on the local-peer registration).
- [✓] **GF-2.3: `GlusterWorker` spawned in `run_serve()`** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `sup.spawn(Spawn::new(GlusterWorker::new(gluster_store), RestartPolicy::Always))` lands just after the nebula_ca_backup spawn site. Opens its own SQLite handle via `mackesd_core::store::open(&db_path)` so the future GF-2.7 quota probe + GF-2.8 conflict detector can audit-log without contending with other workers' locks. Registered in `worker_names` for the Shell.Workers D-Bus surface (v4.1 wiring). `RestartPolicy::Always` since the tick is purely passive — a crash is a fault we want auto-recovered without operator intervention. Binary builds clean under `--features async-services`.)*
- [✓] **GF-2.4: Genesis path — bootstrap `mesh-home` on first tick** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `tick_once()` probes `gluster volume info mesh-home`; on `does not exist` stderr, runs the genesis argv: `gluster volume create mesh-home replica 1 transport tcp <overlay-ip>:<brick-path> force` per design doc § 3.4. Idempotent — once the volume exists every tick is a no-op for this step. Bench-observable: after one tick on a peer with glusterd live + the GF-1.3.a overlay-ip file populated, `gluster volume list` shows `mesh-home`. The peer-probe + add-brick paths (subsequent peers joining the existing volume) defer to GF-2.5 (the `nebula_supervisor::EnrollmentCompleted` subscription that knows when a new peer is reachable to probe).)*
- [✓] **GF-2.5: Polling-based peer-probe (replaces the `nebula_supervisor::EnrollmentCompleted` subscription)** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — instead of building an event-bus the worklist body sketched, gluster_worker now polls `<qnm_root>/*/mackesd/nebula-bundle.json` on every tick via pure-fn `peer_probe_targets(qnm_root, self_node_id)` (skips self, sorted by node_id, skips dirs lacking a parseable bundle); diffs against `current_gluster_peers(binary)` which shells `gluster pool list` + `parse_gluster_pool_list(text)` (skipping the `localhost` row); `peers_to_probe(desired, current)` returns `(node_id, overlay_ip)` pairs missing from the pool; for each, runs `peer_probe_argv(binary, ip)` = `gluster peer probe <overlay-ip>`. Auto-joins the moment a new peer's Nebula bundle replicates via QNM-Shared — single-passcode flat-trust per [[project_open_mesh_directive]]. Worker opts in via `with_qnm_peer_discovery(qnm_root, self_node_id)` (called from run_serve); without the opt-in the step is a silent no-op. 8 new unit tests cover missing-qnm-root / skips-self / sort-determinism / skips-bundle-less-dirs / pool-list parsing (2 cases) / probe-diff / probe-argv. The deviation from the worklist body (poll instead of event-subscribe) is documented in the source-block comment.)*
- [✓] **GF-2.6: Polling-based peer-detach (replaces the `ca_revoke` subscription)** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `peers_to_detach(desired, current)` returns every IP in the gluster pool that no longer has a matching bundle in QNM-Shared (operator removed it via `mackesd ca revoke` → revoked-peer's bundle disappears from `<qnm_root>/<peer-id>/mackesd/`). For each stale IP, `peer_detach_argv(binary, ip)` = `gluster peer detach <ip> force` (force flag required because the peer may still own a brick contributing to the volume — auto-shrink per Q15). 2 new unit tests cover detach-diff + detach-argv-uses-force. Polling-based (5s cadence) instead of event-subscribe; documented in the source-block.)*
- [✓] **GF-2.7: Hourly free-space probe + quota cap** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — gluster_worker tick now ends with a `quota_probe_due()` gate (Mutex-guarded last-fire Instant + `QUOTA_PROBE_INTERVAL = 3600s`); when the gate fires, `run_quota_probe()` shells `gluster volume info mesh-home --xml`, parses every `<sizeFree>` element via `min_brick_free_bytes(xml)` (regex-free scan, integer-parse-tolerant), computes `0.8 × min(free brick)` per Q16, and pushes the cap via `gluster volume quota mesh-home limit-usage / <bytes>` (pure-fn `quota_set_argv`). 5 new unit tests cover smallest-brick pick / empty-volume / unparseable-entry skip / quota-argv command shape / rate-limiter behavior. `QuotaWarning` D-Bus emission defers to GF-2.2; the tracing info-log at quota-set carries the payload until that ships.)*
- [✓] **GF-2.8: Conflict detector — walks `.glusterfs/indices/xattrop/` for pending entries** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — gluster_worker tick now ends with a fifth step: pure-fn `pending_conflict_gfids(xattrop_dir) -> Vec<String>` enumerates the brick's xattrop index dir + filters out glusterd's own `xattrop` / `xattrop-*` placeholder markers; each remaining entry is a GFID symlink for a file with a pending heal / split-brain op. The detector surfaces each pending GFID as a `ConflictDetected` tracing warn event so the operator sees split-brain state without manually running `gluster volume heal info`. Silent no-op when the brick dir is missing (non-storage box). 4 new unit tests cover missing-dir / empty-healthy-brick / 3-GFID-enumeration / placeholder-marker-filter. The `{path, peers}` payload the worklist body sketched needs `gluster volume heal info` parsing for the per-peer attribution — that lands with the GF-2.2 D-Bus service when the structured payload is actually consumed; the current tracing event carries the GFID + brick path which is operator-actionable today.)*
- [✓] **GF-2.9: Conflict resolver — delegates to gluster's native `volume heal split-brain latest-mtime` daemon** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — instead of reimplementing the LWW mtime-comparison + `.conflict-<host>-<ts>` rename in Rust, gluster_worker now delegates to gluster's own self-heal daemon via `heal_split_brain_argv(binary, gfid)` = `gluster volume heal mesh-home split-brain latest-mtime gfid:<uuid>`. The gluster daemon already knows the cross-peer mtime + handles the rename per its own conventions (which DOES produce `.conflict-<host>-<ts>` siblings on identical-mtime ties — same operator-facing behavior the worklist sketched). The detector step (GF-2.8) fires the heal command once per detected GFID; subsequent ticks find the GFID still in the xattrop index but `mark_gfid_heal_requested(gfid)` rate-limits re-issuance until heal completes + the index entry clears. 3 new unit tests cover the heal-argv command shape match + the rate-limiter (first call fires; immediate second is gated; multiple distinct GFIDs each fire once). Deviation from the worklist body (delegate vs reimplement) documented in source-block comment per §0.12 best-choice authorization — re-implementing the LWW logic would have meant maintaining a parallel implementation of glusterd's own resolution algorithm. `HealCompleted` D-Bus signal emission defers to GF-2.2; the tracing info-log at heal-request carries the payload until then.)*

#### Birthright pipeline (Python)

- [✓] **GF-3.1: `apply_uid_normalize()` in `mackes/birthright.py`** — assert primary account is `uid:gid 1000:1000`; if not, run `usermod -u 1000 -g 1000 <user>` + `chown -R` over `$HOME` and `/var/lib/<user>`. Routed through `admin_session.run()`. *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `apply_uid_normalize(_preset)` in `mackes/birthright.py` resolves the primary user from `$SUDO_USER`/`$USER`/`$LOGNAME` (skipping when missing or `root`), reads `pwd.getpwnam` + `pwd.getpwuid(1000)` + `grp.getgrgid(1000)` to detect the four branch states: already-normalized (no subprocess calls); uid-1000-collision (refuses with a clear log line — silent chown over an unrelated existing uid-1000 user would corrupt their session); gid-1000-collision (same shape); happy-path (runs `usermod -u 1000`, `groupmod -g 1000`, recursive chown of `$HOME` + `/var/lib/<user>` when present, all through `_run_root` → `AdminSession`); registered as the "Normalize UID" wizard step between "Thunar on login" and "XDG user dirs" in `mackes/wizard/pages/apply.py`. 9 pytest tests cover every branch (already-normalized, uid-collision, gid-collision, happy-path with $HOME only, happy-path with both $HOME and /var/lib state, missing $USER, root-user skip, user-not-in-passwd skip, usermod failure halts before groupmod). ruff F401/F541/F811/F841 + voice-and-tone lint clean. The remaining GF-3.x steps (GF-3.2 gluster bootstrap, GF-3.3 XDG mesh mount) wire in as they ship — `apply.py` ordering already accommodates them.)*
- [✓] **GF-3.2: `apply_gluster_bootstrap()` in `mackes/birthright.py`** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — operator-visibility step that probes (a) whether the `gluster` CLI is on PATH, (b) `systemctl is-active glusterd.service`, (c) `gluster pool list` succeeds, (d) `gluster volume info mesh-home` reports whether the worker has already bootstrapped. Does NOT bootstrap the volume itself — the worklist body's `mackesd gluster bootstrap-or-join` CLI handoff was retired during implementation because the `mackesd::workers::gluster_worker` daemon (GF-2.4) already owns the bootstrap on every 5s tick, making a parallel operator-typed CLI redundant. The birthright step reports the daemon's expected next-tick action so the operator sees substrate state during the wizard apply rail. Registered as the "Gluster substrate" step between "Normalize UID" and "XDG user dirs" in `mackes/wizard/pages/apply.py`. 5 pytest tests cover every branch (CLI not installed, glusterd inactive, pool-list failure with stderr-tail surfacing, mesh-home exists, mesh-home pending with overlay-ip dependency named). ruff + 278+5 pytest + module-import smoke pass.)*
- [ ] **GF-3.3: `apply_xdg_mesh_mount()` in `mackes/birthright.py` [HW carve-out]** *(HW carve-out tagged 2026-05-24 per `feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty.md`: needs live `mesh-home` FUSE mount + a multi-peer fleet to verify the archive-mount-rsync flow + conflict generation. Doesn't gate the cut.)* — move existing `~/Documents`, `~/Pictures`, `~/Music`, `~/Videos`, `~/Downloads` content to `~/Local/pre-mesh-<ts>/<dirname>/`; mount `mesh-home` at the XDG dirs via per-user systemd unit `mde-mesh-mount@.service`; `rsync --ignore-existing` archived content back into the mesh-mounted dirs. Conflicts handled by GF-2.9.
- [ ] **GF-3.4: Register the three new steps in `mackes/wizard/pages/apply.py`** — insert after `apply_qnm` and before `apply_user_dirs`; amend the latter to skip the dirs that GF-3.3 now owns. *(partial 2026-05-24 — `apply_uid_normalize` registered as the "Normalize UID" step (between "Thunar on login" and "XDG user dirs") in the same commit that shipped GF-3.1; the in-source comment block above the new `_Step` documents the slot where the future GF-3.2 / GF-3.3 steps wire in. Closes fully when those two land and `apply_user_dirs` gets amended to skip the mesh-home XDG dirs.)*

#### FUSE size cap + stub placeholders

- [✓] **GF-4.1: systemd user unit `mde-mesh-mount@.service` ships** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `data/systemd/mde-mesh-mount@.service` is a templated user-unit that `mount -t glusterfs -o _netdev,acl,direct-io-mode=disable localhost:/mesh-home %h/%i` (where `%i` is the XDG subdir name); ExecStop runs `fusermount -u`; not auto-enabled — birthright's GF-3.3 step is what flips `systemctl --user enable mde-mesh-mount@<Documents/Pictures/...>.service` once the operator is on uid 1000:1000 (GF-3.1) AND glusterd has bootstrapped the volume (GF-2.x). Until GF-3.3 lands, operators can manually enable individual instances after running `gluster volume create mesh-home` on their lighthouse — `mount.glusterfs` errors out cleanly with "transport endpoint not connected" when glusterd is down so `systemctl --user status` shows a useful diagnostic. RPM spec installs to `%{_userunitdir}/mde-mesh-mount@.service` + ships in the `%files` block; `rpmspec -P` preprocess clean.)*
- [ ] **GF-4.2: Write-watcher in `gluster_worker` — 5 GB size cap [HW carve-out]** *(HW carve-out tagged 2026-05-24 per `feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty.md`: needs live FUSE mount + a peer that owns the >5 GB origin file for the stub-replacement flow to be verifiable. Doesn't gate the cut.)* — files exceeding 5 GB become a `.mesh-stub` containing `{origin_host, size, sha256, gfid}`. mde-files renders these as placeholders.
- [ ] **GF-4.3: New CLI subcommand `mackesd gluster fetch-stub <path>` [HW carve-out]** *(HW carve-out tagged 2026-05-24 per `feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty.md`: needs `.mesh-stub` files in production (GF-4.2 must run on a bench fleet first) + cross-peer Nebula reach to fetch real bytes. Doesn't gate the cut.)* — pulls the real bytes from the origin host over Nebula on demand.

#### KDC2 phone-bridge rewrite (breaking change for v5.0.0)

- [ ] **GF-5.1: Rewrite KDC2 file-transfer destination in `crates/mde-kdc/src/` [HW carve-out]** *(HW carve-out tagged 2026-05-24 per `feedback_no_cut_until_worklist_empty.md`: needs the KDC2 inbound file-handler infrastructure (no `kdeconnect.share.request` dispatch path in `crates/mde-kdc/src/dispatch.rs` yet — only outbound packet builders exist) + a paired Android phone for end-to-end verification. Doesn't gate the cut.)* — received files land in `~/Documents/From-<phone-name>/` (folder created idempotently on first receive). Mesh replicates from there to every peer.
- [✓] **GF-5.2: Remove every KDC2 file-share UI surface** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — `send_file` D-Bus method retired from `crates/mde-kdc/src/dbus.rs:296`; top-level doc comment updated to flag the GF-5.2 retire alongside the `SendFile` mention in the KDC2-3.6 list. mde-workbench's connect panel (`crates/mde-workbench/src/panels/connect.rs`) drops the `ConnectSection::Share` enum variant + `render_share_section` helper + visibility predicate arm + render-card branch + the 3 unit tests targeting them; 13/13 remaining connect tests pass, 71/71 mde-kdc tests stay green. The `kdeconnect.share.request` packet kind stays in `build_packet`'s vocabulary since the GF-5.1 inbound receive handler (HW-carve-out) may emit it as an acknowledgement; only the OUTBOUND operator-typed surface retired.)*

#### Backup worker extension (subsumes NF-18.4)

- [✓] **GF-9.1: state-backup constant + path change** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — file rename retired in favor of an in-place constant change to avoid 14 unrelated git history churn points; `crates/mackesd/src/workers/nebula_ca_backup.rs:BACKUP_FILENAME` now reads `state-backup.enc` (was `ca-backup.enc`); added `LEGACY_BACKUP_FILENAME` constant for the operator-runbook upgrade path; module name kept as `nebula_ca_backup` because every consumer references it that way + the type/worker name are still semantically the CA backup with an optional gluster section folded in; deviation documented per iteration-skill standing-authorization #4)*
- [✓] **GF-9.2: 24h tick folds a `gluster volume info` / `peer status` / `volume status` XML snapshot into the same encrypted tarball** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — new `crate::gluster::snapshot::collect(&SnapshotConfig)` shells `gluster volume info --xml`, `gluster peer status --xml`, and `gluster volume status all clients --xml`, folds the three XML payloads into a `GlusterSnapshot { volume_info_xml, peer_status_xml, volume_status_xml }` struct (each field `Option<String>`); returns `None` when the `gluster` binary isn't on PATH (peer-only role) and `Some` with per-field `None` for failed subcommands when the binary is present; `BundlePlaintext` gains a `#[serde(default)] gluster_snapshot: Option<GlusterSnapshot>` field so v1 readers ignore it forward + v2 readers tolerate a missing field on legacy bundles; the worker bumps `schema_version` from 1 → 2 only when the snapshot is populated, so CA-only `mackesd ca export` paths stay backward-compatible; 7 new unit tests cover absent-binary / always-failing-binary / always-succeeding-binary / JSON round-trip / legacy-shape deserialization / relative-binary PATH probe / nonexistent-relative-binary; full 583/0 lib suite green. Note: the "brick xattr config" half of the original spec defers to GF-9.3 (restore CLI) — capturing xattrs at backup-time without `gluster` install is a no-op, so the restore path will rebuild xattrs from `volume info` rather than re-applying a frozen snapshot)*
- [✓] **GF-9.3: New CLI `mackesd state-restore <bundle>`** reconstructs both the Nebula CA and the Gluster volume config on a bare peer. *(shipped 2026-05-24 — new `Cmd::StateRestore { bundle, passphrase_env, recovery_dir }` subcommand: reads the armored bundle, calls `ca::backup::dearmor` + `unseal` + `restore_to_store` to put the CA + peer cert rows back into the local SQLite store; when the bundle carries a `gluster_snapshot` (v2 schema bumped by GF-9.2), writes the per-section XML payloads (`volume-info.xml` / `peer-status.xml` / `volume-status.xml`) under `--recovery-dir` (default `/var/lib/mackesd/restore/gluster`) for the operator's manual `gluster volume create --xml-input` replay. Automatic volume replay is intentionally out of scope: replaying a stale `volume info` against a live cluster requires careful peer-by-peer reconciliation that's an operator-driven step, not a silent CLI action — the runbook in `docs/help/mesh-recovery.md` is the canonical replay procedure. Bench-observable: after `MDE_BACKUP_PASSPHRASE=... mackesd state-restore <bundle>`, (a) `mackesd nebula peer-list` shows the restored peer roster, (b) `ls /var/lib/mackesd/restore/gluster/` shows the three XML files (or none, for a CA-only bundle), and (c) the CLI prints a one-line summary of what was restored + what manual operator step remains. Help text + CLI parsing verified end-to-end with `mackesd state-restore --help`.)*
- [✓] **GF-9.4: NF-18.4 worklist entry updated to reflect the rename** *(shipped 2026-05-24 — NF-18.4 retains its `[✓]` status as the historical record of when the daily backup worker first shipped; the file path it wrote to (`ca-backup.enc`) is now superseded by the v5.0.0 `state-backup.enc` via GF-9.1, and the bundle payload is extended with the optional `gluster_snapshot` via GF-9.2. No content edit to NF-18.4's body — closure-as-superseded recorded here in the GF-9.x cluster rather than mutating the historical entry per the newer-wins-silently directive.)*
