#### EPIC-SYNC (storage + sync model)

- [ ] **EPIC-SYNC-MOUNT: Add per-user systemd unit `mde-mesh-mount@.mde-mesh.service` mounting the mesh FS at `~/.mde-mesh/<peer>/`** *(Q14 + Q21)* *(⚠️ RE-POINT 2026-05-29 — §0.13 scan: this task body names retired **GlusterFS** ("mounting gluster", "gluster mesh-home subvolume"). The mount mechanism is now **LizardFS `mesh-storage`** (MESHFS-4.1's `mde-meshfs-mount@.service` pattern), NOT gluster. The `$QNM_SHARED_ROOT/<peer>/` → `~/.mde-mesh/<peer>/` migration also overlaps **EPIC-RETIRE-QNM** (QNM-Shared → MDE-Workgroup). Intent survives; the impl must target LizardFS + coordinate with EPIC-RETIRE-QNM + the MESHFS mount units. Needs a /plan re-point before build — do NOT implement against the gluster wording below.)*
  **As** the platform substrate,
  **I want** QNM-Shared coordination files folded into gluster mesh-home at a dedicated subvolume mount,
  **so that** TWO substrates (gluster + Bus) replace THREE (gluster + QNM-Shared + Bus).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [ ] New unit file `data/systemd/mde-mesh-mount@.mde-mesh.service` ships.
    - [ ] Birthright (GF-3.3 equivalent) enables the unit alongside the XDG mounts.
    - [ ] Per-peer subdirs (`~/.mde-mesh/<self-node-id>/`) populate on first launch.
    - [ ] Workers (`gluster_worker`, `netdata_aggregator`, etc.) updated to read from `~/.mde-mesh/<peer>/` instead of `$QNM_SHARED_ROOT/<peer>/`.

- [✓] **EPIC-SYNC-ARBITER: ~~Configure gluster volume with arbiter brick on lighthouses~~ — RETIRED (obsolete under LizardFS)** *(Q22)* *(RETIRED 2026-05-29 — session=opus-48-2026-05-29-ship-pivot, §0.13 continuous-retirement scan. GlusterFS was replaced wholesale by LizardFS (`mesh-storage`, MESHFS-* epic / `docs/design/v5.0.0-mesh-storage-lizardfs.md`). **LizardFS has no arbiter-brick concept** — there is no "replica N arbiter 1" volume model to update. The underlying concern (keep SBC-lighthouse storage small) is addressed differently in the LizardFS design: lighthouses run the master/shadow metadata role (Q2/Q3) + chunkserver placement is per MESHFS-6/Q6 (every peer is a chunkserver under goal=N; a lighthouse-lean-storage carve-out, if wanted, is a per-CS goal/label decision, NOT an arbiter brick). No gluster-arbiter code should be built. Closed as retired rather than left open so the cut gate + future /ship cycles don't pick it.)*

- [✓] **EPIC-SYNC-APP-CONFIG: Port `media_sync_daemon.py` to Rust as `mde-app-sync` worker** *(Q26)* *(**SHIPPED 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-A.** Discovery decision (operator-delegated via AskUserQuestion "Make the discovery call"): **Option A — faithful mesh-peer TCP-probe discovery in Rust.** Landed: new `crates/mackesd/src/mesh_media.rs` (`MediaServer` + `discover()` enumerates peer overlay IPs from `<qnm_root>/<peer>/mackesd/nebula-bundle.json` + TCP-probes :4040/:8096, deduped on `(kind,ip,port)`) + new `crates/mackesd/src/workers/app_sync.rs` (60 s-tick `Worker` replacing the `media_sync` subprocess: credential load + byte-faithful Sublime/Delfin config writers + `~/Mackes Media/` launcher view + GTK bookmark, behind an extensible `MediaApp` plugin trait — Sublime Music + Delfin out-of-box, add-an-app = one `impl`). `run_serve` now spawns `app_sync::build()` (worker name `app-sync`). **3 files retired** (`git rm`): `workers/media_sync.rs` + `mackes/media_sync_daemon.py` + `mackes/mesh_media.py` — only doc-comment consumers remained. 14 new unit tests (7 mesh_media + 7 app_sync); the two byte-faithful JSON tests assert output identical to the Python `json.dumps(indent=2)`. `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` 1059/1059 green; `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; no-stubs + runtime-reachability + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice lints clean; ruff + `make test-nodeps` (214 pass / 0 fail) green after the `.py` deletions; RPM gate N/A (`setup.py find_packages` auto-discovery — deleting modules doesn't change package structure; no systemd unit referenced the daemon). DoD gate 8: no new public port / D-Bus / Tera-exec — writes local config files + TCP-probes Nebula-overlay peer IPs (never public). **Scope reduction (§0.16): the Python's supplementary mDNS `_subsonic._tcp`/`_jellyfin._tcp` LAN browse is NOT ported** — a media server reachable only via plain-LAN mDNS has no Nebula trust, so auto-pointing clients at it conflicts with §0 "Secure"; tracked as the `.mdns-lan` follow-on below. Cite: docs/design/v2.5-nebula-fabric.md (mesh-peer overlay discovery); ref: the existing `gluster_worker::peer_probe_targets` pattern. — The earlier survey (kept for context, and the basis for choosing Option A) found: the **config-writer half is clean + mechanically portable**, but the **discovery half is architecturally unresolved** and needs a dedicated design decision before any code lands. Findings: (1) **Writers are clean** — `media_sync_daemon.py` (257 LOC) writes Sublime Music `config.json` (`{providers:[…]}`), Delfin `servers.json` (`{servers:[…]}`), one `.desktop` launcher per server under `~/Mackes Media/`, a GTK-3 `bookmarks` line, all atomic-write + idempotent; these port byte-faithfully to Rust with unit tests asserting identical output. (2) **Retirement is clean** — the only remaining references to `media_sync_daemon`/`mesh_media` outside themselves are DOC-COMMENTS (`mesh_nebula.py:65/72/502`, `service_publishing.rs:13`); no functional Python importer, so the 3 files (`media_sync_daemon.py` + `mesh_media.py` + the `media_sync.rs` subprocess wrapper) delete cleanly once the Rust replacement lands. (3) **Discovery is the blocker** — `mesh_media.py` carries an in-source `DeprecationWarning` (its mDNS `_subsonic._tcp`/`_jellyfin._tcp` browse + TCP-probe-of-peer-IPs approach is deprecated) pointing at "per-peer service telemetry" as the replacement, BUT: the referenced docs (`v12.0-enterprise-mesh.md`) are SUPERSEDED (archived per memory); `crates/mackesd/src/telemetry/` exists but covers heartbeat/health, NOT media-server discovery; `service_publishing.rs` still shells to Python `mesh_nebula.published_services_summary()` for its "Media" row; and there is NO Rust-native airsonic/jellyfin discovery anywhere in `crates/`. So the port forces a DESIGN CHOICE that /ship can't make unsupervised: **(A)** reimplement the deprecated mDNS+TCP-probe in Rust (faithful to current runtime behavior, but ports an explicitly-deprecated approach via `mdns-sd 0.11` which IS available) vs **(B)** design a telemetry/service-publishing-sourced media discovery (the intended direction, but net-new design with feature-lock implications + needs the Rust service-publishing model that `service_publishing.rs` currently fakes via a Python shell-out). **Recommend a dedicated session**: resolve A-vs-B first (likely folds into the broader `service_publishing.rs` Python-shell-out retirement + EPIC-RETIRE-PY-DAEMONS), then the writers + worker + plugin-model + 3-file retirement are mechanical. Same caveat applies to the sibling `remmina_sync` port (421 LOC, also probe-based + still references legacy `tailscale_status`).)*
  **As** the platform's app-config sync,
  **I want** the cross-peer app-config sync (Sublime Music + Delfin) ported to Rust,
  **so that** Q15's hard deadline (no Python subprocess-supervised daemons by 1.0) is met for this case.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] New worker `crates/mackesd/src/workers/app_sync.rs` ships (60 s-tick `Worker`, spawned by `run_serve` as `app-sync`).
    - [✓] Discovery + write-config logic ported from `mackes/media_sync_daemon.py` — discovery via mesh-peer TCP-probe (`mesh_media::discover`); all 5 writers (Sublime config.json, Delfin servers.json, `.desktop` launchers, media view, GTK bookmark) byte-faithful. *(mDNS-LAN browse deferred per §0.16 scope reduction — see `.mdns-lan` below)*
    - [✓] Per-app plugin model supports adding new apps — `MediaApp` trait + `apps()` registry; Sublime Music + Delfin out-of-box, extensible by one `impl`.
    - [✓] `media_sync_daemon.py` retired (+ `mesh_media.py` + `media_sync.rs`); `git rm`'d, only doc-comment consumers remained.
    - [ ] **EPIC-SYNC-APP-CONFIG.mdns-lan: (deferred §0.16) re-add the mDNS `_subsonic._tcp`/`_jellyfin._tcp` LAN browse** *(opened 2026-05-28)* — the native port intentionally dropped the Python's supplementary LAN-mDNS discovery (a non-Nebula-peer media server has no mesh trust, conflicting with §0 "Secure"). A later session wanting LAN-bleed back adds an `mdns-sd 0.11` snapshot-browse (2 s window, both service types) to `mesh_media::discover`, unioned + deduped with the existing probe path. `mde-bus/discovery.rs` is the in-repo `mdns-sd` reference pattern. NOT a §11 blocker — the daemon is already retired + mesh discovery works.

- [ ] **EPIC-SYNC-CLIP-BLOBS: Unify clipboard blobs into `~/.mde-mesh/blobs/<ulid>.<ext>` (mesh-replicated)** *(Q29)* *(⚠️ RE-POINT 2026-05-29 — §0.13 scan: body says "gluster-replicated" + "respects gluster's replication". GlusterFS is retired; the blob store now rides **LizardFS `mesh-storage`** (goal=N replication). Concept survives (one canonical mesh-replicated blob store); the impl + GC must target LizardFS, not gluster. The acceptance's `mde-clipd` write-path + GC logic update accordingly.)*
  **As** the platform's blob model,
  **I want** one canonical blob store across the platform,
  **so that** clipboard payloads + Bus references + any future blob refs use the same location.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [ ] BUS-5.3 spec updated: `~/.local/share/mde/clipboard/blobs/` → `~/.mde-mesh/blobs/`.
    - [ ] `mde-clipd` writes to the gluster-replicated path.
    - [ ] GC logic respects gluster's replication (don't delete on local peer if other peers might still reference).

#### EPIC-MESH-PROBE (centralized probe subsystem — one probe source, all tools consume)

> **Locked 2026-05-28 via 10-Q `/plan` survey** (operator lock-lift from §0.16). Design: `docs/design/mesh-probe-subsystem.md`. Memory: `project_mesh_probe`. Big-bang epic (Q10) — lands cohesively, but each task is an independent bench-observable user-story per §0.12. Consolidates/underpins MESH-A-1/2/4/7 (they repoint onto this). Locks: per-peer centerless (Q1); GFS source + Bus `probe/changed` (Q2); nmap-for-everything (Q3) + bundled NSE (Q4); mesh+LAN+arbitrary scope (Q5); two-tier+manual cadence (Q6); mde-card schema (Q7); `mackesd::probe` library (Q8); scan-everything-actively (Q9).

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-1: v1.0 — `mde-card` Host + Service card kinds + probe field set** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. `CardKind` gains `Host` + `Service` (+ `tag()` entries); new `crates/mde-card/src/probe.rs` ships `HostSource` enum (mesh/lan/arbitrary) + `HostFacts`/`ServiceFacts` typed structs + `host_card()`/`service_card()` constructors + `host_facts()`/`service_facts()` accessors, re-exported from `lib.rs`. Probe facts ride the `metadata` bucket under a single `"probe"` key (serde-typed nested object). 8 new probe tests + the extended `card_kind_tag_is_stable`; `cargo test -p mde-card` 39/39 green; `cargo build -p mde-card` clean; `cargo check -p mde-portal` clean (dependent unaffected by the new variants); no-stubs + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice + runtime-reachability lints clean. DoD gate 8: pure library schema, no new port/D-Bus/exec. Reachable via the crate public API (the `pub use probe::*` re-export, consumed by mde-portal's existing `card_index` Card deserializer).)*
  **As** the inventory model,
  **I want** `CardKind::Host` + `CardKind::Service` variants carrying the probe fields (ip, hostname, source mesh/lan/arbitrary, trust-state, last-seen; product, version, fingerprint on services),
  **so that** the probe inventory is a first-class `mde-card` structure that renders via the existing `card_index` (Q7).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] `crates/mde-card` `CardKind` gains `Host` + `Service` (open-set variants — old peers fall back to `Other("host"/"service")`, so NO `schema_version` bump needed). **Best-choice deviation (§0.9) from the task's "12-field schema carries the probe fields, schema_version bumped" wording:** the `Card` field count is a hard R5-Q3 lock (the `card_field_count_is_twelve` structural test), so the probe fields live in the purpose-built `metadata` bucket (its documented role — "drains known fields into typed places") with typed accessors in `crates/mde-card/src/probe.rs`, rather than adding top-level fields + bumping the schema. Same data, respects the lock, no migration entry needed.
    - [✓] A host Card with two service child Cards round-trips through serialize → deserialize unchanged (`full_inventory_card_round_trips_through_json`) — the mechanical half of "card_index load"; the actual Portal render is MESH-PROBE-9 (visual bench, deferred per the no-pre-release-reviews policy).
    - [✓] `mde-card` unit tests cover the new kinds + the host→service `children` composition + metadata-accessor read-back (8 probe tests + extended tag-stability test).

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-2: v1.0 — nmap invocation profiles + `-oX` XML parser** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. New `crates/mackesd/src/probe_nmap.rs`: `Profile` enum (Fast/Deep); pure-fn `fast_argv`/`deep_argv` builders over a `CURATED_PORTS` set (media 4040/4533/8096 + the MESH-A-7 well-known connect-action ports), both `-oX -` + `-T3`-rate-limited (polite default, never `-T4`/`-T5` per design §7); `parse_nmap_xml(xml, source, now)` uses `roxmltree` (with `allow_dtd: true` — real nmap output opens `<!DOCTYPE nmaprun>`) to emit `mde_card` Host cards + Service children via the MESH-PROBE-1 `mde_card::probe` constructors (skips down hosts + closed ports); `scan(binary, profile, targets, nse_dir, source, now)` shells nmap + parses, returning empty+warn on nmap-absent/garbage (never panics). **Runtime entry point (§0.12):** new `mackesd probe scan [--deep] [--source mesh|lan|arbitrary] [--nse-dir] <targets>` CLI prints one JSON host-card line per up-host — verified end-to-end (`--help` wired; nmap-absent → warn + exit 0). Deps added to `crates/mackesd/Cargo.toml`: `roxmltree = "0.20"` + `mde-card` (both already vendored). 10 probe_nmap tests (argv flag-shape, faithful `nmap -sV -oX` fixture → cards with product/version, closed-port skip, down-host skip, garbage→empty, nmap-absent, empty-targets); `cargo test -p mackesd` 1069/1069 green; `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; no-stubs + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice + runtime-reachability lints clean. **nmap binary is needed for real output** — install is the bench's job (MESH-PROBE-10) + the `Requires: nmap` RPM dep is MESH-PROBE-3; the engine + CLI ship complete + graceful-degrade today. **Cargo.lock + workspace Cargo.toml intentionally excluded from the commit** — a sibling session was concurrently retiring `mde-motion` (staged deletion) + editing mde-portal/mde-iced-components, entangling those shared files; `mackesd/Cargo.toml` is the dep source-of-truth, lock reconciles on next build per §0.3. DoD gate 8: outbound nmap only (no new listener/D-Bus/exec-template); the scan reaches mesh/LAN/arbitrary targets per the operator-confirmed Q9 scan-everything lock. Cite: mesh-probe-subsystem.md §2 (engine + Q3/Q4 nmap-for-everything).)*
  **As** the probe engine,
  **I want** a `mackesd` module that shells `nmap -oX -` in a fast profile (curated known-ports) and a deep profile (`-sV --version-all --script <bundled NSE>`) and parses the XML into `Vec<Card>`,
  **so that** every probe goes through one engine (Q3) with rich identification (Q4).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] Pure parser turns a captured nmap XML fixture into host+service Cards (roxmltree; 4 parse tests over a faithful `nmap -sV -oX` sample).
    - [✓] Fast + deep argv builders are pure-fn + unit-tested for flag shape (`-T3` rate-limited, never `-T5`; `-oX -`; deep adds `-sV --version-all` + `--script <nse_dir>`).
    - [✓] `nmap` absent → `scan` returns empty + logs at warn (no panic; verified via the `mackesd probe scan` CLI smoke + a unit test). `Requires: nmap` in the RPM is MESH-PROBE-3.

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-3: v1.0 — bundled NSE scripts + RPM packaging** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. Two NSE scripts under `data/nmap/`: `mde-media-detect.nse` (probes Subsonic `/rest/ping.view` + Jellyfin `/System/Info/Public` to name Airsonic/Navidrome/Jellyfin behind generic http banners, setting precise `service_kind` via `set_port_version`) + `mde-service-fingerprint.nse` (probes Mackes Bus broker `/v1/health` + `/config.js` to mark MDE peers / `mde-bus` service). Both validated against real nmap 7.92 — `nmap --script-help data/nmap/<name>.nse` resolves + parses both. Spec: `Requires: nmap` (verified via `rpmspec -q --requires`) + two `install -D` lines → `%{_datadir}/mde/nmap/` mirroring the proven `data/bus/*.tmpl` pattern (covered by the `%{_datadir}/%{name}/` %files catch-all). Already wired into the deep profile (`probe_nmap::deep_argv` → `--script /usr/share/mde/nmap`). `rpmspec -P` parses clean. **No `make rpm` — RPM builds are deferred to release per the operator's hard rule (2026-05-28); packaging verified via `rpmspec -P` + correct-by-construction install lines.** Cite: mesh-probe-subsystem.md §2 (Q4 bundled NSE).)*
  **As** the deep-identification path,
  **I want** custom NSE scripts (a mesh-media detector + a Mackes-service fingerprinter) shipped to `/usr/share/mde/nmap/` with `Requires: nmap`,
  **so that** identification beats stock `-sV` for platform services (Q4).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] NSE scripts land under `data/nmap/`; spec installs them to `/usr/share/mde/nmap/` + adds `Requires: nmap`.
    - [✓] `nmap --script-help <bundled-name>` resolves against the installed path (validated against real nmap 7.92 for both scripts).
    - [✓] Packaging verified via `rpmspec -P` (parses clean) + `rpmspec -q --requires` (nmap registered); install lines mirror the proven `data/bus` pattern. **`make rpm` deferred to release** per the no-RPM-until-release hard rule (2026-05-28).

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-4: v1.0 — probe worker (two-tier cadence + manual refresh)** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. Thin async `workers::probe::ProbeWorker` (gated `async-services`) wraps the sync orchestration in `probe_nmap::run_probe_cycle` (ungated, so the CLI reaches it without the feature). Worker ticks every 60s (FAST_TICK); each tick runs a Fast cycle EXCEPT every 600s (DEEP_INTERVAL, rate-limited via a `last_deep` mutex mirroring gluster's `quota_probe_due`) it runs a Deep `-sV`/NSE cycle. `run_probe_cycle`: resolves mesh-peer targets (`mesh_targets` → `mesh_media::peer_overlay_ips`), `probe_nmap::scan`, serializes the `Vec<Card>` to `<qnm_root>/<self>/mackesd/probe-inventory.json` (atomic temp+rename via `write_inventory_if_changed`), and publishes `probe/changed` (min priority, `mde-bus` shell-out, graceful-degrade) ONLY when the content changed vs. on-disk. Spawned in `run_serve` (`RestartPolicy::Always`, worker name `probe`) → §0.12 runtime-reachable. Manual refresh: new `mackesd probe refresh [--qnm-root] [--node-id] [--nse-dir]` CLI runs one deep cycle. **Robustness fix surfaced by the e2e test:** `scan()` now drops `--script <nse_dir>` when the dir is absent (nmap aborts exit-1 on a missing `--script` path) so a peer without the bundled scripts installed degrades to plain `-sV` instead of returning zero hosts. **Validated end-to-end against real nmap 7.92:** `probe refresh` with a temp mesh-home bundle (overlay_ip=127.0.0.1) wrote a 1-host inventory (`localhost` → ssh + ipp service children) + published `probe/changed`. 33 probe tests (`probe_nmap` + `workers::probe`); `cargo test -p mackesd` 1091/1091 green; `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; no-stubs + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice + runtime-reachability lints clean. DoD gate 8: outbound nmap + a new GFS file + the `probe/changed` Bus topic (min priority, mesh-only, Nebula-replicated) — no new listener/D-Bus/exec. Scope = mesh peers; LAN + arbitrary targets are MESH-PROBE-5. Cite: mesh-probe-subsystem.md §2 (Q1/Q2/Q6 worker + cadence + substrate).)*
  **As** each peer's `mackesd`,
  **I want** a `workers::probe` worker that runs the fast tier (~60 s) + deep tier (~10 min) + an operator manual-refresh, writes `<qnm_root>/<self>/mackesd/probe-inventory.json`, and publishes a `probe/changed` Bus message on a material diff,
  **so that** the centerless per-peer inventory exists + propagates (Q1, Q2, Q6).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] Worker spawned in `run_serve` (`RestartPolicy::Always`); fast (60s) + deep (600s) tiers fire on their cadences, deep rate-limited mutex-guarded like the gluster quota probe.
    - [✓] `probe-inventory.json` written atomically (temp+rename); `probe/changed` Bus publish fires only when the inventory content changes (verified by `write_inventory_detects_change_then_noop` + the e2e).
    - [✓] Manual refresh (`mackesd probe refresh`) forces an immediate deep pass — validated end-to-end against real nmap (wrote localhost inventory).

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-5: v1.0 — target resolver (mesh + LAN + arbitrary) + safety controls** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. `probe_nmap` gains the Q5/Q9 target resolver: `resolve_targets(qnm_root, home) -> TargetSet { targets, excludes }` unions mesh-peer overlay IPs (`mesh_targets`) + detected LAN CIDRs (`detect_lan_cidrs` shells `ip -j addr`, parsed by the pure `lan_cidrs_from_ip_json` which computes network CIDRs via `ipv4_network_cidr` + skips lo/nebula/docker/podman/virbr/cni/veth/br- ifaces) + operator-arbitrary targets from `~/.config/mde/probe-targets.toml` (`targets=[…]`), deduped first-seen-order via `merge_targets`; excludes loaded from `~/.config/mde/probe-do-not-scan.toml` (`exclude=[…]`) via `read_toml_string_list`. The Q9 escape hatch is plumbed through `scan()` (new `excludes` param → nmap `--exclude <list>`). `run_probe_cycle` refactored into a testable core `run_probe_cycle_with(TargetSet)` + a resolving wrapper `run_probe_cycle(qnm_root, self_node_id, home, …)` (the worker + `probe refresh` CLI use the wrapper; tests use the core with an explicit set so they never touch the real LAN). `ProbeWorker` gains a `home` field (env `$HOME`). 39 probe tests (6 new resolver: ipv4-cidr-masking, lan-cidrs-skip-loopback/overlay, lan-garbage-empty, merge-union-dedupe, toml-list-read, resolve-merges-arbitrary+excludes); `cargo test -p mackesd` green (1097 pass; the lone intermittent `workers::heartbeat::tests::heartbeat_worker_exits_on_shutdown_token` failure is a PRE-EXISTING flaky 3s-timeout async test — passes on clean HEAD + 3/4 warm runs with this change — NOT a MESH-PROBE regression; see DEAD-FLAKY-HEARTBEAT follow-up); `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; no-stubs + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice + runtime-reachability lints clean (dropped a `tailscale` iface-prefix that tripped legacy-mesh — unnecessary on a Nebula-only platform). DoD gate 8: the resolver widens nmap's outbound scan scope to LAN + arbitrary per the operator-confirmed Q9 lock (CFAA/ToS risk operator-accepted, design §7); the do-not-scan list is the carve-out. Cite: mesh-probe-subsystem.md §1 Q5+Q9 + §7.)*
  **As** the probe worker,
  **I want** to resolve scan targets from the nebula roster (mesh peers) + the local LAN CIDR + an operator arbitrary-target list, honoring an optional do-not-scan exclusion list,
  **so that** scope (Q5) + the Q9 "scan everything actively" default (with the exclusion escape hatch) are both served.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] Resolver unions mesh-peer overlay IPs + detected LAN CIDR (`ip -j addr` → network CIDRs) + `~/.config/mde/probe-targets.toml` arbitrary entries; deduped (`merge_targets`, `resolve_targets`).
    - [✓] A CIDR/IP in `~/.config/mde/probe-do-not-scan.toml` is excluded from every pass via nmap `--exclude` (unit-tested: `resolve_targets_merges_arbitrary_and_reads_excludes`).
    - [✓] Default with no exclusion file = scan all resolved targets (Q9) — empty exclude list ⇒ no `--exclude` flag.

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-6: v1.0 — `mackesd::probe` read library + Bus-event subscription** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. `probe_nmap` gains the consumer-facing read API: `inventory(qnm_root) -> Vec<Card>` (walks `<qnm_root>/*/mackesd/probe-inventory.json`, parses each fail-open — a malformed/missing file is skipped+logged, never aborts — and unions all host cards); `peers_with_service(qnm_root, kind) -> Vec<HostService>` (`HostService { host: HostFacts, service: ServiceFacts }`; case-insensitive service-kind match — the query app_sync uses in MESH-PROBE-7); `inventory_fingerprint(qnm_root) -> u64` (XOR of per-file path+len+mtime hashes, order-independent — a poll consumer compares it each tick to skip a re-parse when nothing changed). New `mackesd probe list [--service <kind>]` CLI prints the merged inventory (host JSON lines) or the filtered `ip\tkind\thost:port` rows — the runtime entry point (§0.12). **Naming deviation (§0.9):** the design (Q8) named this `mackesd::probe::`; it lives in `mackesd::probe_nmap` co-located with the `inventory_path` + format + writer it mirrors (a separate module would only re-export those). **`subscribe_changed` reframed:** in-process consumers (app_sync, MESH-A workers) poll `inventory()`/`inventory_fingerprint()` on their existing tick — the established mackesd worker pattern (gluster_worker etc. all poll, none push-subscribe); the `probe/changed` Bus PUSH (published by the MESH-PROBE-4 worker) drives the Portal's subscription via the standard mde-bus subs (MESH-PROBE-9), not a bespoke `subscribe_changed` fn. 25 probe_nmap tests (4 new: inventory-unions+fail-open, missing-root-empty, peers_with_service case-insensitive filter, fingerprint-changes-on-write); validated end-to-end via `probe list` (seeded 2-peer mesh-home → merged union + `--service jellyfin` filter correct). `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; all 6 lints clean. Cite: mesh-probe-subsystem.md §1 Q8.)*
  **As** every consumer,
  **I want** `mackesd::probe::{inventory, peers_with_service, subscribe_changed}` that merges all peers' GFS card-files,
  **so that** tools read one merged inventory in-process (Q8).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] `inventory()` returns the union of every `<qnm_root>/*/mackesd/probe-inventory.json`; missing/malformed files skipped (fail-open per file).
    - [✓] `peers_with_service("airsonic")` returns the hosts whose service Cards match (case-insensitive); unit-tested over a multi-peer tmpdir fixture + the `probe list --service` e2e.
    - [✓] Reactivity: in-process consumers poll `inventory()`/`inventory_fingerprint()` per tick; the `probe/changed` Bus push (MESH-PROBE-4) drives the Portal subscription via standard mde-bus subs (MESH-PROBE-9). *(reframed from a bespoke `subscribe_changed` — mackesd workers poll, they don't push-subscribe)*

- [✓] **MESH-PROBE-7: v1.0 — repoint `app_sync` media-discovery onto the probe library** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. The epic capstone: `app_sync`'s `run_once` now calls a new `discover_from_inventory(qnm_root)` that reads `probe_nmap::peers_with_service` for `airsonic` + `navidrome` (both → `KIND_AIRSONIC`, spoken by Sublime Music) + `jellyfin` (→ `KIND_JELLYFIN`) from the shared probe inventory + maps each `HostService` → `MediaServer`, replacing the bespoke `mesh_media::discover` TCP-probe. **One prober (the MESH-PROBE-4 worker) now feeds media discovery** — proving the epic end-to-end: probe worker writes `probe-inventory.json` → app_sync reads it. **Retired** `mesh_media::{discover, scan_probe, probe_port, dedupe}` + `PROBE_TIMEOUT` + their 4 tests (the TCP-probe path); `mesh_media` shrinks to the shared model (`MediaServer` + `server_from_probe` for app_sync) + `peer_overlay_ips` (the probe worker's mesh-target source via `probe_nmap::mesh_targets`). The byte-faithful Sublime/Delfin config writers + the `MediaApp` trait are UNCHANGED (still take `MediaServer`) — only the discovery source swapped. 8 app_sync tests (the 7 byte-faithful/writer tests intact + 1 new `discover_from_inventory_maps_probe_services_to_media_servers`: jellyfin→KIND_JELLYFIN, navidrome→KIND_AIRSONIC, ssh ignored) + mesh_media 4 tests; `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; all 6 lints clean. Precise media `service_kind` comes from the bundled NSE detector (MESH-PROBE-3) on the worker's deep pass; the fast pass's generic `http` won't match (eventual-consistent within the 10min deep cadence). Cite: mesh-probe-subsystem.md §3 (consumer repoint).)*
  **As** `app_sync`,
  **I want** to read `probe::peers_with_service("airsonic"/"jellyfin")` instead of TCP-probing itself,
  **so that** the double-probe is gone + `mesh_media`'s probe path retires (Q10 consumer repoint).
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] `app_sync` discovery sources from `probe_nmap::peers_with_service`; `mesh_media::{discover, scan_probe, probe_port, dedupe}` deleted (mesh_media reduced to the shared `MediaServer`/`server_from_probe` model + `peer_overlay_ips`, which probe_nmap still uses — so it's the "thin remainder" the acceptance allows, not full deletion).
    - [✓] The byte-faithful Sublime/Delfin config tests still pass against probe-sourced servers (7 writer tests unchanged + green).
    - [✓] No remaining direct TCP-probe of media ports in `app_sync`/`mesh_media` (the `TcpStream`/`probe_port` path is gone; discovery is inventory-read only).

- [✓] **DEAD-FLAKY-HEARTBEAT: stabilize the flaky `heartbeat_worker_exits_on_shutdown_token` test** *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B. **Root-caused, not papered over.** The flake wasn't the test's 3 s timeout being too tight — it was the inner sync heartbeat thread (`telemetry::spawn_heartbeat_worker`) sleeping the **full `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_S`** between shutdown-flag checks. On shutdown the async worker sets the flag + block-joins that thread; if the thread was mid-`sleep(interval)`, `join()` blocked up to a full interval — a race (flag-set vs thread-reaching-sleep) that flaked the test ~25% AND stretched the supervisor's real SIGTERM→exit latency. Fix: the inner thread now sleeps in 100 ms chunks, re-checking the flag each chunk, so shutdown is honored within ~100 ms. No test change needed — the existing 3 s-timeout test now passes deterministically (verified 6/6 single-test runs + 2/2 full-suite runs, 1099/0 each; was intermittently 1098/1-failed). Production benefit: responsive worker shutdown. `cargo test -p mackesd` 1099/1099 green twice consecutively; all 6 lints clean. Touches only `crates/mackesd/src/telemetry/mod.rs`. Bug-fix-against-existing-work per §0.16.)* **Acceptance:** the test must pass deterministically — root-caused the inner-thread join latency (chunked flag-check) rather than widening the timeout; 8 consecutive green runs (6 single + 2 full-suite) confirm.

#### EPIC-BUS-EXT (Bus refinements beyond BUS-1..7)

- [✓] **EPIC-BUS-EXT-ACTION: Implement `action/<domain>/<verb>` namespace + `reply/<ulid>` RPC pattern** *(Q31 + Q32)* *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DH. The Bus gains a request/reply RPC idiom so it can be the migration target for the D-Bus command surfaces MDE retires by 1.0 (Q96). New `crates/mde-bus/src/rpc.rs` (~140 LOC + 7 unit tests): the correlation key is the action message's **ULID** (no side-channel metadata — the requester knows its own `Persist::write` return; a responder reads the action message's ULID to know where to answer). `publish_request(persist, action_topic, priority, title, body) -> ulid` enforces the `action/` namespace prefix (rejects non-`action/` topics so events + commands never collide) + publishes + returns the request ULID. `await_reply(persist, ulid, timeout)` polls `reply/<ulid>` every 200 ms until a reply lands or the deadline fires (sleep clamped so it never overshoots), returning the first reply or `RpcError::Timeout`. `request(...)` is the convenience publish+await a Rust caller uses in place of a D-Bus method call. `DEFAULT_RPC_TIMEOUT = 30 s` per the Q31 lock; `reply_topic(ulid)` + `ACTION_PREFIX` exported. **Operator surface**: new `mde-bus request <action-topic> [body] [--title] [--priority] [--timeout-secs] [--json]` CLI verb (`cli/request.rs`) publishes the command + waits for the reply, prints the reply body (or `--json` full envelope), exits non-zero on timeout so scripts/hooks detect a missing responder. **Design deviation (best-choice, §0.9)**: the acceptance named a separate `mde-bus-client` Rust crate; realized instead as the `mde_bus::rpc` module + the `request` CLI verb — `mde-bus` already IS the bus library that owns `Persist`, so a thin wrapper crate carrying only the caller half (no responder framework exists yet) would be §0.12 scaffolding. The RPC pattern + protocol IS the deliverable; in-process Rust callers `use mde_bus::rpc::request`. **Responder side** (a worker subscribing to `action/<domain>/+` + replying to `reply/<ulid>`) ships per-domain with its consuming epic (e.g. EPIC-BUS-EXT-CONFLICT's `action/gluster/dismiss-conflict`); this bundle ships the caller + convention + the operator-reachable CLI. 10 new tests (7 rpc: reply-topic format, non-action-topic rejected, publish-returns-ulid + writes to action topic, await returns posted reply, await times out, request round-trips, request rejects bad topic before waiting; 3 CLI: timeout-no-responder, bad-priority, non-action-topic). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 300/300 green; `cargo build -p mde-bus --bin mde-bus` clean; runtime-reachability + no-stubs + dbus-shape + voice + legacy-mesh + public-ports lints clean. DoD gate 8: no new public port / D-Bus method / Tera `{{exec}}` — the RPC rides the existing per-peer persist tree (Nebula-replicated, never network-exposed) + is the OPPOSITE of new D-Bus surface (it's the retirement target). Cite: docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md §10 (Bus as IPC default); ref: Linear (request/response over a pub/sub bus).
    - [✓] `mde-bus publish action/gluster/resolve-conflict ...` works *(any `action/` topic publishes; `mde-bus request` is the RPC-aware wrapper)*.
    - [✓] Caller subscribes to `reply/<ulid>` before/after publishing; response arrives there *(await_reply polls `reply/<request-ulid>`; the message ULID is the correlation key)*.
    - [✓] Timeout handling: configurable per-request; default 30 s (`DEFAULT_RPC_TIMEOUT`; CLI `--timeout-secs`).
    - [✓] RPC library wraps the pattern for ergonomic use *(`mde_bus::rpc::{request, publish_request, await_reply}` + `mde-bus request` CLI — realized as a module in the bus crate rather than a separate `mde-bus-client` crate per the §0.9 best-choice note above)*.

- [ ] **EPIC-BUS-EXT-CONFLICT: `mesh/conflict` Bus topic for gluster file conflicts (high priority + persistent until ack)** *(Q23)* *(.publish half shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-A; bare `[>]` — marker released so a later session can claim the `.ack` follow-on. The GF-2.8 conflict detector in `crates/mackesd/src/workers/gluster_worker.rs` now publishes each newly-detected split-brain GFID to the `mesh/conflict` Bus topic at `priority=high` by shelling out to `mde-bus publish` (the established worker→Bus pattern; see `urgency_router`). New `CONFLICT_TOPIC` const + `conflict_publish_argv(gfid, brick)` pure helper + `publish_conflict(&self, gfid)` method; the publish call sits INSIDE the existing `mark_gfid_heal_requested` dedup guard so it fires exactly once per GFID — that dedup IS the gfid-thread-key coalescing the original acceptance named (BUS-1.7 has no literal `thread_id` field; per-GFID dedup is the faithful equivalent). Body carries `gfid:<uuid>` + the brick path so an operator can locate the conflict; title "Mesh file conflict". Best-effort + graceful-degrade: a pre-enrollment peer (no broker yet) or a box without the `mde-bus` binary logs at warn and the conflict still heals via the GF-2.9 LWW path. Stale "future GF-2.2 D-Bus signal subscriber" comment replaced with the real Bus-publish description. 1 new unit test (`conflict_publish_argv_matches_mde_bus_publish_shape` — asserts verb/topic/priority=high/title/body-flag shape + body contains gfid+brick). `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` 1047/1047 green (1046 + 1); `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; voice + legacy-mesh + dbus-shape + no-stubs + public-ports lints clean. Runtime-reachable: `publish_conflict` ← `tick_once` (the live 5s tick) ← `Worker::run`. **DoD gate 8 security review**: (a) surface — a new Bus TOPIC publish (`mesh/conflict`), not a new public port / D-Bus method / Tera `{{exec}}` template; (b) what reaches it — the publish rides the per-peer persist tree via the `mde-bus publish` CLI (Nebula-replicated mesh-home, never network-exposed); the topic is readable by any mesh peer per the open-mesh transparency directive; (c) fits the open-mesh / flat-trust directive — split-brain conflicts are fleet-wide operational state every peer should see. Cite: docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md §6 (priority→surface table: high = status-zone strip, persistent until ack); ref: Linear (high-priority incident surfacing).)*
  **As** the gluster_worker,
  **I want** conflict events to publish at `mesh/conflict` with high priority,
  **so that** operators see split-brain conflicts via the status-zone strip + must explicitly ack.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] GF-2.9 conflict resolver publishes to `mesh/conflict` with `priority=high` after LWW completion. *(shipped — `conflict_publish_argv` + `publish_conflict` inside the LWW dedup block)*
    - [✓] Status-zone strip (BUS-2.4) surfaces the conflict. *(surfacing is automatic — `priority=high` routes to the status-zone strip via the existing `mde_bus::surface::dispatch`; the "persistent until any peer acks" enforcement is the `.ack` follow-on below)*
    - [✓] Multiple conflicts coalesce per gfid (gfid:<uuid> as thread key). *(satisfied by the existing `mark_gfid_heal_requested` per-GFID dedup — one publish per unresolved GFID regardless of how many 5s ticks observe it; the original "BUS-1.7 thread_id" reference is loose — BUS-1.7 is the subscription manifest, not a threading field)*
    - [ ] **EPIC-BUS-EXT-CONFLICT.ack: action/gluster/dismiss-conflict responder + persistent-until-ack strip interaction** *(follow-on, opened 2026-05-28)* — a mackesd subscriber on `action/gluster/dismiss-conflict` (per the EPIC-BUS-EXT-ACTION responder convention) that clears the conflict on the gfid, plus the mde-portal status-zone strip rendering the ack gesture that publishes it. Needs the portal strip UI (visual bench) + the responder plumbing; split out per §0.12 because the publish half ships complete + runtime-reachable on its own.

- [✓] **EPIC-BUS-EXT-CORRELATION-5: Ship 5 correlation rule examples with BUS-6.5** *(Q34)* *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DE. Natural follow-on to BUS-6.5.evaluator. New `data/bus/correlate.yaml.tmpl` (~130 lines) ships the 5 sample rules using REAL fleet topic names (aligned to the actual publishers, not the loose worklist shorthand): **power-outage** (`power/grid-loss` from the NUT/UPS adapter + `mon/network`; 120 s; → `incident/power-outage` urgent), **disk-pressure** (`mon/cpu` + `mon/disk` + `mon/memory`; 180 s; → `incident/disk-pressure` high), **mesh-degraded** (`mesh/peers` + `mon/gluster`; 300 s; → `incident/mesh-degraded` high), **vpn-flap** (`mon/nebula` + `mesh/peers`; 60 s; → `incident/vpn-flap` high), **gfs-quota-trending** (`mon/disk` + `mon/gluster`; 600 s; → `incident/gfs-quota-trending` default). Each rule carries a comment block explaining its trigger logic + priority rationale. Header documents the copy-to-`~/.config/mde/bus-correlate.yaml`-and-trim workflow + the `mde-bus correlate validate` sanity-check + the source-topic provenance (which adapter/worker publishes each `power/*`, `mon/*`, `mesh/peers`). **Reference template, NOT auto-seeded** — correlation is opt-in, so operators copy + trim rather than getting 5 incident rules firing by default; the daemon reads the live config only. RPM spec installs it to `/usr/share/mde/bus/correlate.yaml.tmpl` (covered by the `%{_datadir}/%{name}/` catch-all in `%files`). 3 new tests embed the template via `include_str!` (so a broken example fails the build, not just a bench run): shipped-template-parses-to-five-rules (asserts all 5 names present), shipped-template-passes-validation (`validate_config` returns zero issues), shipped-template-rules-are-well-formed (every rule ≥2 sources + non-zero window + `incident/` emit topic). `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib correlate` 37/37 green (34 + 3). `make rpm` clean (DoD gate 3 — new data file + spec install line). voice + no-stubs + legacy-mesh lints clean on the template's comment prose. Cite: motion-language.md §6 (correlated-incident templates); ref: Linear (out-of-box automation recipes).
    - [✓] `data/bus/correlate.yaml.tmpl` ships 5 rules: power-outage, disk-pressure, mesh-degraded, vpn-flap, gfs-quota-trending. *(topic names aligned to real publishers: NUT `power/grid-loss`, Netdata `mon/*`, seeded `mesh/peers`)*
    - [✓] Each rule has a comment block explaining the trigger + expected synthesized topic.
    - [ ] Bench: scripted trigger of each correlation pattern fires the expected synthesized topic. *(HW/bench carve-out — needs a live fleet publishing the source topics; the `include_str!` parse+validate tests cover the static-correctness half)*)*

  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):

- [✓] **EPIC-BUS-EXT-AUDIT-BUS: Migrate BUS-7.1 audit from per-peer JSONL to `audit/<peer>` Bus topic** *(Q28)* *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-28-ship-B, the dedicated session the 2026-05-27 scoping note called for. The 3-subsystem change landed together: **(a) write-path** — `Persist::write` now emits the metadata-only `AuditEntry` as a recursive `self.write` to `audit/<peer>` (`<peer>` = `publisher_id()` hostname) at `Priority::Min`, replacing the JSONL `audit::append`. **Cycle guard:** `if !topic.starts_with(AUDIT_TOPIC_PREFIX)` — audit records aren't re-audited (no infinite recursion); best-effort + failure-isolated (a failed audit emit never fails the original write). **(b) retention** — `run_pass_at` excludes `topic NOT LIKE 'audit/%'` so audit/* is retention=forever despite being min-priority. **(c) read-path** — new `audit::read_entries_from_bus` reads `audit/*` messages via Persist + decodes bodies → `AuditEntry`; the `mde-bus audit` CLI (list + count) swapped onto it; the JSONL fns (`append`/`append_at`/`list_files`/`read_entries` + `audit_dir`/`filename_for` + perms helpers) retired (only `AuditEntry`/`AuditError` survive). **Empirically-measured blast radius** (per the scoping note's caution): made the write-path change alone + ran the FULL suite → exactly 6 count/state-invariant tests shifted (no `list_topics` surprises) — all updated to reflect the audit-doubling (count == messages + their audit records; per-topic `list_since` unaffected); retention fixture `open_tmp_with` purges audit records after seeding so retention tests stay focused; +3 new audit tests + 1 new retention-exemption test. `cargo test -p mde-bus --lib` 292/292 green; `cargo build -p mde-bus` + bin clean; no-stubs + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice + runtime-reachability lints clean. No external consumer used the retired fns (workspace grep clean). **DoD gate 8 (security review):** (a) surface — the audit trail moves from local 0600 JSONL to Bus messages on `audit/<peer>` (metadata-only, never bodies); no new public port / D-Bus method / Tera-exec. (b) what reaches it — audit records are subscribable mesh-wide via `audit/+` (the default `#` manifest already covers it); GFS-replicated over Nebula, never network-exposed. (c) fits flat-trust — this DELIBERATELY increases audit visibility from local-only to mesh-wide per Q28 + Q54 (mesh-wide transparency); acceptable + intended on the flat-trust personal mesh; the records carry no message bodies. Cite: docs/design/v6.x-mackes-bus.md §7-audit + Q28/Q54.)*
  **As** the audit subsystem,
  **I want** audit events on Bus instead of JSONL files,
  **so that** cross-peer audit visibility is trivial + the substrate is uniform.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] BUS-7.1 write-path updated: `Persist::write` emits to `audit/<peer>` at `priority=min`; retention exempts `audit/*` (forever).
    - [✓] All peers subscribe to `audit/+` by default — satisfied by the existing default manifest `topics: ["#"]` (the `#` wildcard matches `audit/<peer>`); no change needed.
    - [✓] Audit-emit bypasses the audit hook for `audit/*` topics (the `!topic.starts_with("audit/")` cycle guard in `Persist::write`).

- [✓] **EPIC-BUS-EXT-CUT-DM: Remove BUS-6.6 DM addressing from worklist** *(Q37)* *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DG. BUS-6.6 annotated `[✓] RETIRED — superseded by Q37` in place (the `[~] Retired` status the acceptance named was itself retired by the "no silent deferrals" directive, so the closest valid mark is `[✓]` with a RETIRED banner + the original body struck through for historical context). Verified zero shipped code to remove — `crates/mde-bus/src/dm.rs` + `crates/mackesd/src/workers/active_peer.rs` never existed; the cut is purely a worklist-scope decision. BUS-6.x sub-epic drops from 8 tasks to 7. No code, no tests, no lints — pure worklist hygiene closing a §11 roadmap item per §0.17.)*

- [✓] **EPIC-PROC-QUARTERLY: Establish quarterly retirement audit cadence + first audit landed before 1.0** *(Q65)* *(closed 2026-05-26 by session=opus-47-2026-05-26-ship-X — both acceptance bullets [✓]; first audit landed inline during this /ship session.)*

#### EPIC-SEC (security + audit)

- [ ] **EPIC-SEC-PASSCODE-CREDS: Store mesh passcode via systemd-creds TPM-or-host-key** *(Q52)* *(mechanism shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DI; bare `[>]` — the systemd-creds store/load mechanism + CLI landed, but the epic stays in-progress until the two follow-on bullets (.birthright-wire + .mded-read) land so the real provisioning + enrollment paths use it instead of libsecret. Marker released so a later session can claim the follow-ons. The new `crates/mackesd/src/passcode_creds.rs` (~210 LOC + 5 unit tests + 1 ignored real-host round-trip) wraps `systemd-creds encrypt`/`decrypt`: `store(plaintext, cred_path, name)` feeds plaintext on **stdin** (`-` input — never a temp plaintext file) + writes ciphertext to the cred file + chmods 0600; `load(cred_path, name)` decrypts to stdout + returns the passcode; `encrypt_argv`/`decrypt_argv` pure-fns (`--name=<name> --with-key=auto` → TPM-when-present-else-host-key) are unit-tested for argv shape; `PasscodeCredsError` maps ENOENT→`BinaryMissing` so a non-systemd host degrades clearly. `DEFAULT_CRED_PATH = /var/lib/mackesd/mesh-passcode.cred` is host-local + asserted NOT under any mesh-home/QNM-Shared path (the cred is host-bound — a peer can't decrypt another peer's copy; each peer encrypts the shared passcode under its own key). CLI: `mackesd generate-passcode --store [--cred-path]` + `mackesd rotate-passcode --store [--cred-path]` encrypt the generated/rotated code to the cred file (replacing the printed libsecret hint); `mackesd show-passcode [--cred-path]` decrypts + prints (the inverse + the path mded's .mded-read follow-on will call via `passcode_creds::load`). 5 unit tests green (argv-stdin-ordering, argv-decrypt-ordering, default-path-host-local-not-mesh, ENOENT→BinaryMissing mapping, actionable-error-messages); the real encrypt→decrypt round-trip is `#[ignore]`d (needs systemd-creds + the host key, which wants root — verified the argv is correct: a manual `systemd-creds encrypt` on this box reaches `InteractiveAuthenticationRequired`, i.e. parses the command + hits the privilege gate mded clears in production). `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` 1046/1046 green (1041 + 5); `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; no-stubs + runtime-reachability + dbus-shape + legacy-mesh + public-ports + voice lints clean. DoD gate 8: no new public port / D-Bus / Tera-exec — shells out to the system `systemd-creds`; the cred is host-local ciphertext, never network-exposed.)*
  **As** the platform,
  **I want** the mesh passcode encrypted at rest with hardware-backed key when available,
  **so that** disk-image theft doesn't leak the mesh credential.
  **Acceptance** (each bench-observable):
    - [✓] systemd-creds encrypt mechanism: `mackesd generate-passcode --store` / `rotate-passcode --store` encrypt the code to `/var/lib/mackesd/mesh-passcode.cred` via `systemd-creds encrypt --with-key=auto` (auto-detects TPM, falls back to host key). *(shipped — `crates/mackesd/src/passcode_creds.rs`)*
    - [✓] systemd-creds decrypt mechanism: `mackesd show-passcode` + the `passcode_creds::load()` library fn mded will call at startup. *(shipped; mded-startup wiring is the `.mded-read` follow-on)*
    - [✓] Plaintext passcode never written to disk — plaintext transits stdin/stdout/memory only; the cred file holds ciphertext (0600). *(shipped)*
    - [ ] **EPIC-SEC-PASSCODE-CREDS.birthright-wire:** Birthright wizard invokes `mackesd generate-passcode --store` so first-login provisioning writes the encrypted cred instead of printing the libsecret hint. *(follow-on, opened 2026-05-28)*
    - [ ] **EPIC-SEC-PASSCODE-CREDS.mded-read:** mded enrollment path reads the passcode via `passcode_creds::load()` at startup (replacing the libsecret lookup the module docs reference). *(follow-on, opened 2026-05-28)*

- [✓] **EPIC-SEC-BANLIST: Compromised-node ban list — revoked node-id refuses re-join even with correct passcode** *(Q53)* *(shipped 2026-05-28 — session=opus-47-2026-05-27-ship-DF. New `crates/mackesd/src/ca/ban_list.rs` (~190 LOC + 9 unit tests) ships the data model + storage + mesh-wide union: `BanList { node_ids: BTreeSet<String> }` (serde JSON), `ban_list_path(qnm_root, self_node_id)` = `<qnm_root>/<self>/mackesd/ban-list.json` (same per-peer layout as `pending_enroll_path`, so the GFS-replicated mesh-home root propagates a ban to every peer), `load_file` (missing → empty; malformed → Err), `save_file` (atomic temp+rename + mkdir parent), `load_union(qnm_root)` (walks `<qnm_root>/*/mackesd/ban-list.json`, unions all node-ids, **fail-open per file** — a corrupt one peer's file is logged + skipped so it can't blind the gate to other peers' bans; missing root → empty union), `is_banned`, `add_banned` (idempotent), `remove_banned`. **Enrollment gate**: `nebula_enroll::sign_pending_csr` gains a ban-union check BEFORE the TUNE-11 cap check + before any signing work — `is_banned(qnm_root, csr.node_id)` → new `SignCsrError::NodeBanned { node_id }`. **A ban is absolute: no override flag** (unlike the cap's `--override-cap`) — even `allow_override = true` can't sign a banned node (tested). Emits a `tracing::warn!(target: "mackesd::ban_list", event = "enroll.banned.refused")` audit line. **CLI**: `mackesd ca ban <node-id>` (add to this peer's list, idempotent), `mackesd ca unban <node-id>` (lift THIS peer's entry; if another peer still bans it, the output says so since the gate enforces the union), `mackesd ca ban-list` (print the mesh-wide union the gate enforces). 11 new tests: 9 ban_list module (path layout, missing-file-empty, save/load round-trip + no-tmp-leftover, union-combines-peers, missing-root-empty, union-skips-malformed, is_banned, add idempotent, add accumulates) + 2 sign-gate (`sign_csr_refuses_banned_node` — banned node rejected even with override_cap=true + asserts no cert row written; `sign_csr_allows_unbanned_node_when_others_banned` — innocent peer still signs when the list holds other ids). `cargo test -p mackesd --features async-services --lib` 1041/1041 green (1030 + 11); `cargo build --bin mackesd` clean; dbus-shape + no-stubs + runtime-reachability + legacy-mesh + public-ports lints clean. **DoD gate 8 security review**: (a) surface — a new GATE on the existing CSR enrollment path, not a new public port / D-Bus method / Tera `{{exec}}` template; (b) what reaches it — only the lighthouse signing CSRs reads the union; the ban list is GFS-mesh-only (Nebula-replicated), never network-exposed; (c) fits the open-mesh / flat-trust directive — a ban is the one deliberate exception to flat-trust (a compromised identity), enforced at the single CSR chokepoint, with no override, surviving CA rotation as Q53 requires. **Bench (HW carve-out)**: live multi-peer GFS propagation of `ban-list.json` + the union spanning real peer mounts needs a bench fleet; the `load_union` over a tmpdir multi-peer layout covers the static-correctness half. Cite: docs/design/security-posture.md §5 (flat-trust threat model + the deliberate exceptions); ref: Apple System Settings (blocklist management).
    - [✓] Ban list at `<qnm_root>/<self>/mackesd/ban-list.json` (GFS-replicated mesh-home; same per-peer layout as pending-enroll + bundles). *(worklist's `~/.mde-mesh/` shorthand = the qnm_root post EPIC-RETIRE-QNM rename)*
    - [✓] mackesd enrollment check (`sign_pending_csr`) rejects any node-id present in the union of all peers' ban lists — before the cap check, no override.
    - [✓] CLI `mackesd ca ban <node-id>` adds to local ban list; GFS propagates. *(+ `ca unban` + `ca ban-list` for the full lifecycle)*
    - [ ] Bench: live multi-peer GFS ban propagation + union across real mounts. *(HW carve-out — needs a bench fleet; static union-over-tmpdir tested)*
